alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network classifier outperformed clinical geneticists at recognizing face images of Williams syndrome and 22q11.2 deletion syndrome across different age groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our classifier outperformed clinical geneticists at recognizing face images of Williams syndrome and 22q11.2 deletion syndrome across different age groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication statement is effectively identical in meaning and scope to the preprint claim, reporting the same result about classifier performance across age groups.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall accuracy improvement by the neural network classifier over clinical geneticists was 15.5% for Williams syndrome and 22.7% for 22q11.2 deletion syndrome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall accuracy improvement by our classifier over clinical geneticists was 15.5% for Williams syndrome and 22.7% for 22q11.2 deletion syndrome. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric improvements and wording are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key facial features learned by the neural network differed slightly with respect to age. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Saliency maps revealed that key facial features learned by the neural network differed with respect to age. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the methodological detail (saliency maps) explaining how the age-related differences were observed, clarifying the original claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Joint training with real images and multiple types of fake images created by a generative adversarial network showed up to 3.25% accuracy gain in classification accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Joint training with real and fake images showed up to 3.25% accuracy gain in classification accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same gain but omits the explicit mention of GAN/multiple fake-image types, so the claim is presented with fewer methodological specifics (narrower detail).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability of clinical geneticists to diagnose these conditions is influenced by the age of the patient. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about clinicians' diagnostic ability and age was not stated in the preprint and appears newly emphasized in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models improve cardiovascular disease prediction model performance when incorporating social determinants of health and related variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most studies that compared performance with or without social determinants of health showed increased performance with them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state that including social determinants of health improves model performance; the publication frames this as a finding across studies but retains the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most studies that compared machine learning and regression showed increased performance of machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most studies that compared machine learning algorithms and regression showed increased performance of machine learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning match directly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The limited variety of sources and data in studies indicates an opportunity to include more social determinants of health variables in machine learning cardiovascular disease prediction models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The limited variety of sources and data in the reviewed studies emphasizes that there is an opportunity to include more social determinants of health variables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation and implication about limited data variety and the opportunity to include more variables.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most frequently included social determinants of health variables were race/ethnicity, income, education, and marital status. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most frequently included social determinants of health variables were gender, race/ethnicity, marital status, occupation, and income. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The lists differ: the publication swaps education for gender/occupation and thus revises the specific variables reported as most frequent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning may provide an opportunity to incorporate and model the complex nature of social determinants of health. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not explicitly restate the broader conceptual claim about ML's opportunity to model SDoH complexity; it appears omitted in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recording environmental social determinants of health data in electronic databases will enable their use in cardiovascular disease risk assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This publication recommendation about recording environmental SDoH in electronic databases is new and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved an overall accuracy of 99.10% in cardiac image classification, compared to 91.7% of the state of the art. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for automatic recognition of cardiac views achieves an overall accuracy of 99.10% on cardiac image classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 99.10% result but omits the explicit direct numeric comparison to the 91.7% state-of-the-art value, so the claim is slightly rephrased/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean AUC of the proposed method is 99.36%, indicating high performance in cardiac image classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean AUC of the proposed method is 99.36%, which is better than known methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the comparative statement ('better than known methods'), clarifying the significance of the reported AUC.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall accuracy on an independent test set is 98.15%, with a mean AUC of 98.96%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall accuracy of the proposed method on an independent test set is 97.73%, with a mean AUC of 98.59%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different numeric results on the independent test set (both accuracy and AUC), indicating a revision of those quantitative outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is expected to serve as an automated recognition tool for cardiac views recognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed automated recognition model can be applied clinically to help find standard cardiac views. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the method as clinically applicable rather than merely expected to serve as a tool, which strengthens the practical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall response rate and disease control rate (DCR) for patients receiving immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) were 15.3% and 53%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall response rate and disease control rate (DCR) for patients receiving immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) were 15.3% and 53%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical reporting of response and DCR values between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In multivariable analysis, DCR was significantly associated with performance status and hemoglobin level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In multivariable analysis, DCR was significantly associated with performance status and hemoglobin level. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same multivariable analysis result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best machine learning algorithm, a random forest, could predict DCR with satisfactory efficacy based on performance status, hemoglobin level, and neutrophils-to-lymphocytes ratio. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best machine learning algorithm for predicting DCR was a random forest model, which demonstrated satisfactory efficacy based on performance status, hemoglobin level, and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same model and predictor variables with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combination of simple clinical and biological data could accurately predict disease control rate at the individual level. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of simple clinical and biological data could accurately predict disease control rate at the individual level. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning applied to a large set of NSCLC patients could predict efficacy of immunotherapy with a 69% accuracy using simple routine data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric claim of 69% accuracy is not present in the publication claims list and appears omitted or not emphasized in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models trained with laboratory or a combination of laboratory + clinical data achieved an area-under-the ROC curve of 0.82 and 0.84 for predicting 31-day mortality, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models trained with laboratory or a combination of laboratory + clinical data achieved an area-under-the ROC curve of 0.82 and 0.84 for predicting 31-day mortality, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match of reported AUC performance between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the validation set, machine learning models outperformed internal medicine physicians and clinical risk scores in sensitivity (92% vs. 72% vs. 78%; p<0.001, all comparisons). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the validation set, machine learning models outperformed internal medicine physicians and clinical risk scores in sensitivity (92% vs. 72% vs. 78%; p<0.001, all comparisons). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model had higher diagnostic accuracy with an area-under-the-ROC curve of 0.85 compared to abbMEDS (0.63), mREMS (0.63), and internal medicine physicians (0.74). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model had higher diagnostic accuracy with an area-under-the-ROC curve of 0.85 compared to abbMEDS (0.63), mREMS (0.63), and internal medicine physicians (0.74). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The diagnostic accuracy comparison is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models outperformed internal medicine physicians and clinical risk scores in predicting 31-day mortality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models outperformed internal medicine physicians and clinical risk scores in predicting 31-day mortality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is restated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These models are a promising tool to aid in risk stratification of patients presenting to the ED with sepsis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These models are a promising tool to aid in risk stratification of patients presenting to the ED with sepsis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The contribution/implication is maintained with equivalent wording and intent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning model was developed that automatically detects patients with metastatic cancer using pathology reports.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study's findings demonstrate the feasibility of developing algorithms to automatically identify metastatic cancer cases from unstructured pathology reports.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim as demonstrating feasibility rather than stating a completed deployed detector, which clarifies and slightly tempers the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model outperforms a general-purpose LLM, achieving a recall of 0.894 compared to 0.824.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our deep learning architecture trained on task-specific data outperforms a general-purpose LLM, with a recall of 0.894 compared to 0.824.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance comparison and identical recall figures, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retaining 72.9% of reports increased recall from 0.894 to 0.969.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Retaining 72.9% of reports increased recall from 0.894 to 0.969.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the identical numeric result and conclusion about retention improving recall.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach could streamline population-based cancer surveillance to help address the unmet need to capture recurrence or progression.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present or emphasize the population-surveillance implication present in the preprint, so this specific policy/surveillance claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A smaller deep learning architecture trained on task-specific data outperforms a general LLM.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a claim about model size and task-specific training not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of uncertainty quantification is critical to model performance, achieved here through an abstention mechanism.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a methodological claim about uncertainty quantification/abstention that was not present in the preprint claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning model demonstrates high discrimination power for detecting LV systolic dysfunction from ECG images, achieving an AUROC of 0.91 in internal validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated high discrimination across various ECG image formats and calibrations in internal validation with an AUROC of 0.91.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the AUROC but adds that performance held across formats/calibrations, broadening and clarifying the original internal-validation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An ECG suggestive of LV systolic dysfunction is associated with over 27-fold higher odds of LV systolic dysfunction on transthoracic echocardiography.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ECG suggestive of LV systolic dysfunction portended >27-fold higher odds of LV systolic dysfunction on transthoracic echocardiogram.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same odds ratio relationship with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A positive ECG screen in individuals with LV ejection fraction ≥ 40% is linked to a 3.9-fold increased risk of developing incident LV systolic dysfunction in the future.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A positive ECG screen in individuals with an LV ejection fraction ≥40% at the time of initial assessment was associated with a 3.9-fold increased risk of developing incident LV systolic dysfunction in the future.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same longitudinal risk association and numeric estimate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provides an automated and accessible screening strategy for LV systolic dysfunction, particularly in low-resource settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach represents an automated and accessible screening strategy for LV systolic dysfunction, particularly in low-resource settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion but reports it with a lower stated certainty (medium), indicating a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed and externally validated a deep learning model that identifies LV systolic dysfunction from ECG images.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit external validation that was not present in the preprint claims list, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML algorithm demonstrated a positive predictive value of 0.91 for IC+ cases and a negative predictive value of 0.94 for IC- cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The positive predictive value for IC+ cases was 0.91, and the negative predictive value for IC- cases was 0.94.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same PPV and NPV values with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Admission, neurosurgical intervention, and 30-day mortality rates for IC+ cases were significantly higher than for IC- cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Admission, neurosurgical intervention, and 30-day mortality rates for IC+ cases were significantly higher than for IC- cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative outcome findings with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm classified most head CTs into clinically relevant groups with high predictive values.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ML algorithm incorporating uncertainty classified most head CTs into clinically relevant groups with high predictive values.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that uncertainty was incorporated into the algorithm (via abstention/uncertainty), clarifying method while preserving the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 32% of NP cases had intracranial hemorrhage or other urgent abnormalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 32% of NP cases had intracranial hemorrhage or other urgent abnormalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same statistic and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods can be used to accurately predict uterine dehiscence with up to 95.6% accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can be used to accurately predict uterine dehiscence by using clinical features and lower uterine segment ultrasonography findings.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim that ML can accurately predict uterine dehiscence, matching the preprint's overall result-oriented conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using lower uterine segment full thickness or myometrium thickness increases the accuracy of Naive Bayes algorithm by 4.8% and 5.1%, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification performance increased from 90.5% to 95.6% when adding the myometrium measurement to the base features.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the numeric improvement specifically for the myometrium measurement (to 95.6%) but does not corroborate the preprint's dual-percentage claim for both full thickness and myometrium, narrowing the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Naive Bayes algorithm yields the best prediction performance among the methods tried.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The naive Bayes algorithm showed the best predictive performance for uterine dehiscence among the machine learning techniques tested.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms naive Bayes as the best-performing method among those tested, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ultrasonographic lower uterine segment measurements can be used as features in machine learning to increase its prediction performance of uterine dehiscence and hence the risk of uterine rupture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can be used to accurately predict uterine dehiscence by using clinical features and lower uterine segment ultrasonography findings.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates that sonographic lower uterine segment measurements are useful features for ML prediction, consistent with the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using essential features such as parity, gravidity, tocographic contraction, cervical dilation, dilation and curettage, and sonographic thickness of lower uterine segment myometrium yielded the best predictive results.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists a set of essential features (parity, gravidity, etc.) that were not enumerated in the preprint claims, so this is an added detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models trained using both water and fat images performed best, giving DSC = 0.94 and NRMSE = 4.2%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models trained using both water and fat images performed best, giving DSC = 0.94 and NRMSE = 4.2%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces identical performance metrics for the best-performing models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained without the water image as input all performed worse, including in participants with elevated liver fat.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained without the water image as input all performed worse, including in participants with elevated liver fat.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same comparative performance claim about omission of the water image.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models using the T2-weighted anatomical images underperformed the Dixon-based models, but provided acceptable performance (DSC ≥ 0.92, NRMSE ≤ 6.6%) for use in longitudinal pediatric obesity interventions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models using the T2-weighted scout images underperformed the Dixon-based models, but provided acceptable performance (DSC ≥ 0.92, NMRSE ≤6.6%) for use in longitudinal pediatric obesity interventions.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparison and acceptable-performance thresholds; wording differs only in naming the T2 image type.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model using Dixon water and fat images as input gave the best performance, with results comparable to inter-reader variability and state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model using Dixon water and fat images as input gave the best performance, with results comparable to inter-reader variability and state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative and benchmark claim for the Dixon-based model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEEP * HLA achieved the highest accuracies in both Japanese and European HLA reference panels, with accuracies of 0.987 and 0.976 respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DEEP*HLA achieves the highest accuracies with significant superiority for low-frequency and rare alleles.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's overall high accuracy claim to emphasize particular superiority on low-frequency/rare alleles rather than the specific panel-wide accuracy figures, so the claim is clarified and somewhat narrowed in focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEEP * HLA is less dependent on distance-dependent linkage disequilibrium decay of the target alleles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DEEP*HLA is less dependent on distance-dependent linkage disequilibrium decay of the target alleles.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DEEP * HLA successfully disentangled independently associated class I and II HLA variants with shared risk between diverse populations in type 1 diabetes GWAS data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DEEP*HLA successfully disentangles independently associated class I and II HLA variants with shared risk among diverse populations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with only minor wording/tense adjustments; the substantive claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study illustrates the value of deep learning in genotype imputation and trans-ethnic MHC fine-mapping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study illustrates the value of deep learning in genotype imputation and trans-ethnic MHC fine-mapping.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm DLA-RB achieved an AUC of 0.991 in identifying active retinoblastoma tumors from clinical-suspected patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DLA-RB reached an AUC of 0.991 in identifying active retinoblastoma tumors from all clinical-suspected patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same AUC and population description; the claim content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DLA-RB reached an AUC of 0.962 in distinguishing active retinoblastoma from stable retinoblastoma patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the 0.962 AUC for active vs stable cases and provides no direct equivalent, indicating this specific claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cost-utility analysis indicates that the DLA-RB based diagnosis mode is more cost-effective for retinoblastoma diagnosis and activity surveillance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DLA-RB-based diagnosis mode is cost-effective for both retinoblastoma diagnosis and active retinoblastoma identification.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the cost-effectiveness conclusion with stronger certainty and explicitly extends it to both diagnosis and active identification, amplifying confidence and broadening scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DLA-RB achieved high accuracy and sensitivity in identifying active retinoblastoma from normal and stable retinoblastoma fundus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Learning Assistant for Retinoblastoma Monitoring (DLA-RB) algorithm achieved an area under curve (AUC) of 0.998 in distinguishing normal fundus from active retinoblastoma.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a higher, specific AUC for distinguishing normal versus active fundus but does not report the combined normal+stable comparison or the sensitivity claim as stated in the preprint, so the result is revised and narrowed to a specific comparison with stronger performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DLA-RB can be incorporated within telemedicine programs in the future.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make or repeat the telemedicine-integration claim present in the preprint, so it appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated CTG interpretation enhances the early detection of fetal hypoxia during labor.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on label choice, timing, and robustness to temporal shifts rather than asserting that automated CTG interpretation directly enhances early detection of fetal hypoxia, so this higher-level deployment claim is not retained explicitly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using objective umbilical cord blood pH outcome measurements yields more consistent and robust model performance than clinician-defined Apgar scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained with objective umbilical cord blood pH measurements outperformed those trained with subjective clinician-assigned Apgar scores.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical finding about objective pH labels outperforming Apgar-based labels.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study explores the impact of temporal distribution shifts on the performance of deep learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aligning training signal intervals with the timing of outcome measurement exhibited superior performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific, actionable finding about alignment of training intervals (a narrower, clearer result) arising from the exploration of temporal shifts described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are tradeoffs between performance and fairness in evaluating deep learning models across demographic and clinical subgroups.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the fairness/performance tradeoff claim or subgroup fairness analyses present in the preprint, indicating omission.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have practical implications for the real-world deployment of automated CTG interpretation systems in medical settings with limited resources.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly discuss deployment in resource-limited settings, so this practical-implication claim appears to have been dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm demonstrated a median probability of glaucoma in eyes with glaucomatous optic neuropathy (GON) of 99.8% compared to 0.03% for normal eyes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Likelihood ratios indicated that the deep learning algorithm provided large changes in the post-test probability of disease for the majority of eyes.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same idea (large changes in post-test probability) but replaces specific median probability numbers with likelihood-ratio–based statements, so the numeric claim is revised and presented more generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) for the deep learning algorithm was 0.92, indicating high performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm demonstrated an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.92 for discriminating between GON and normal.
alignments.rationale: The AUC value and interpretation are carried forward identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed objective definition of glaucomatous optic neuropathy (GON) may enhance the comparability of diagnostic studies across devices and populations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The newly proposed objective definition of GON may increase the comparability of diagnostic studies of glaucoma across devices and populations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about improved comparability with only minor rephrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm provided large changes in the post-test probability of disease for the majority of eyes tested.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Likelihood ratios indicated that the deep learning algorithm provided large changes in the post-test probability of disease for the majority of eyes.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same result using likelihood-ratio language; the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Magnetic resonance imaging using hyperpolarized gases enables novel visualization of airspaces in the human lung.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on segmentation and image-based CNN improvements and does not repeat the specific claim about hyperpolarized-gas MRI visualization, so that specific contribution is not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many algorithmic approaches for lung imaging segmentation reduce complex spatial image information to intensity-only considerations, leading to a loss of important spatial cues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Histogram-based segmentation methods discard important contextual spatial information and are consequently less robust in terms of measurement precision in the presence of common MRI artifacts relative to the image-based convolutional neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation about histogram/intensity-only methods discarding spatial/contextual information and links that to reduced robustness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A convolutional neural network optimized directly within the image domain can leverage spatial information and mitigate issues associated with histogram-based approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Direct optimization within the image domain using convolutional neural networks leverages spatial information, which mitigates problematic issues associated with histogram-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological claim about image-domain CNN optimization leveraging spatial information to mitigate histogram-based issues.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed image-based optimization leads to improved measurement precision in lung imaging segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Histogram-based segmentation methods discard important contextual spatial information and are consequently less robust in terms of measurement precision in the presence of common MRI artifacts relative to the image-based convolutional neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the precision improvement in the context of robustness to common MRI artifacts, clarifying the circumstances in which measurement precision is improved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The entire processing and evaluation framework, including deep learning functionality, is provided as open-source through the Advanced Normalization Tools ecosystem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The entire processing and evaluation framework, including the newly reported deep learning functionality, is available as open source through the well-known Advanced Normalization Tools ecosystem.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same open-source availability claim with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work identifies one statistically significant region of fixation and one significant gaze transition pattern that differ between our two cohorts during gameplay.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gaze fixation patterns differ between children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and neurotypical (NT) peers, with one statistically significant region of fixation identified (P<.001).
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same statistically significant fixation region and cohort difference; wording is effectively the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our deep learning model demonstrates mild predictive power in identifying ASD based on coarse annotations of gaze fixations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning model trained on coarse gaze fixation annotations demonstrates mild predictive power in identifying ASD.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about mild predictive power using coarse gaze annotations with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate the utility of game-based mobile health platforms in quantifying visual patterns and providing insights into ASD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heterogeneous video data sets collected from mobile devices hold potential for quantifying visual patterns and providing insights into ASD.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the idea but softens it to a potential/hold potential framing and emphasizes heterogeneous mobile-collected video data, which clarifies and slightly tempers the original assertion of demonstrated utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We show the importance of automated labeling techniques in generating large-scale datasets while simultaneously preserving the privacy of participants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automated labeling techniques are important for generating large-scale data sets while preserving the privacy of participants.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about the role of automated labeling and privacy preservation with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first abdominal age predictor was built using convolutional neural networks trained on liver and pancreas MRI images.
alignments.rationale: The published paper reports model performance metrics but does not restate the contribution claim about being the first predictor or the specific architectural/training description, so this preprint claim is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accelerated abdominal aging is defined as the difference between abdominal age and chronological age and is found to be partially heritable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Abdominal aging is a complex trait that is partially heritable with a heritability estimate of h_g2 = 26.3 ± 1.9%.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the partial heritability claim but provides a quantitative heritability estimate, clarifying and strengthening (amplifying) the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accelerated abdominal aging is associated with seven single nucleotide polymorphisms in six genes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Abdominal aging is associated with 16 genetic loci, including PLEKHA1 and EFEMP1, as well as various biomarkers, clinical phenotypes, diseases, and environmental and socioeconomic factors.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a larger set of associated loci (16 rather than 7) and additional associated factors, representing a substantive revision and broadening of the genetic association claim with stronger evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictor could be used to assess the efficacy of emerging rejuvenating therapies on the abdomen.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make or support this prospective application claim about assessing rejuvenating therapies, so the claim is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimized random forests generally perform best among the tested machine learning models for predicting diabetic complication onset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimized random forest models performed best to accurately predict the diagnosis of a diabetic complication.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same result that optimized random forests were the top-performing models for predicting diabetic complications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining all data sources yields the best predictive performance for diabetic complication onset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using all data sets combined allowed for the highest model predictive performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly corroborates the preprint claim that combining data sources produced the best predictive performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model interpretation identifies specific patient metrics that play a unique role in the progression of each diabetic complication.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model interpretation is a critical step in evaluating the validity of a user-intended endpoint for a model when using EHR data.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the general importance of model interpretation for validity rather than explicitly claiming identification of specific patient metrics for each complication, narrowing and softening the original, more specific claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our clinical interpretation of machine learning models can identify patients at risk for poorer outcomes years in advance of their diabetic complication.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the forward-looking claim about identifying at-risk patients years in advance, so this specific conclusion does not appear in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recurrence rate at less than 1 year after surgery for oesophageal adenocarcinoma was found to be 29.1%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recurrence rate at less than 1 year after oesophagectomy for adenocarcinoma was 29.1 percent.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact recurrence rate and timeframe reported in the preprint with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Ensemble model demonstrated the best performance with an AUC of 0.806 in predicting early recurrence after surgery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All predictive models demonstrated good discrimination, with the ensemble model performing best with an AUC of 0.805.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the ensemble model as best-performing with essentially identical AUC (minor rounding difference), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived model using machine learning approaches provided excellent performance in quantifying the risk of early recurrence after surgery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model derived using machine learning approaches provided excellent performance in quantifying the risk of early recurrence after surgery.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same overall conclusion about excellent model performance for risk quantification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most important variables in the final model were the number of positive lymph nodes and vascular invasion, contributing 25.7% and 16.9% respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most important variables in the final model were the number of positive lymph nodes and lymphovascular invasion, contributing 25.7 percent and 16.9 percent respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same variable importance values and same key predictors (minor wording difference 'vascular' vs 'lymphovascular'), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DenseNet achieved higher accuracy (84.7% ± 0.7), precision (75.6% ± 1.3), sensitivity (88.7% ± 2.7), specificity (82.3% ± 1.6), F1-score (81.6% ± 1.0), and AUROC (93.1% ± 0.4) than EfficientNet for aortic elongation detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EfficientNet achieved higher accuracy (86.7% ± 2.1), precision (82.7% ± 2.7), specificity (89.4% ± 1.7), F1 score (82.5% ± 2.9), and area under the receiver operating characteristic (92.7% ± 0.6) compared to DenseNet.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly contradicts the preprint by reporting EfficientNet as the better model with different metric values, representing a substantive revision of the model-performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of Grad-CAM and LIME explainability methods allowed for the successful identification of the expected location of aortic elongation in the x-ray images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study employed gradient-weighted class activation mapping and local interpretable model-agnostic explanations to gain insights into the decision-making process of deep learning models.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state that Grad-CAM and LIME (GW-CAM and LIME) were used to gain model decision insights; the methodological claim is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating explainable AI techniques enhanced the interpretability and understanding of the models’ decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of aortic elongation detection models with explainable artificial intelligence techniques enhances the interpretability and understanding of the models' decisions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same conclusion that explainable AI techniques enhance model interpretability, matching the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach holds promise for aiding clinicians in timely and accurate diagnosis, potentially improving patient outcomes in clinical practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach holds promise for aiding clinicians in timely and accurate diagnosis, potentially improving patient outcomes in clinical practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same prospective clinical implication about aiding clinicians and improving outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML model based on the Naïve Bayes algorithm has an accuracy of around 73% to predict the drugs that could be used for the treatment of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML model based on the Naive Bayes algorithm has an accuracy of around 73% to predict the drugs that could be used for the treatment of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and reported accuracy are effectively identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study predicts around ten FDA approved commercial drugs that can be used for repurposing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study predicts around ten FDA approved commercial drugs that can be used for repurposing in the treatment of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication keeps the same core claim but adds explicit mention of treatment and lowers stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The antiretroviral drug Atazanavir is suggested to be one of the most effective drugs for COVID-19 treatment based on selected criteria. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Among the predicted drugs, the antiretroviral drug Amprenavir is likely to be the most effective based on the selected criteria. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the specific drug named (Atazanavir -> Amprenavir), representing a substantive revision of the conclusion while keeping scope and confidence similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ML based approach for drug discovery can be a futuristic smart drug designing strategy for community applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML based approach for drug discovery can serve as a futuristic smart drug designing strategy for community applications. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same conclusion but reduces stated certainty, so it is a clarification with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vaccine hesitancy (VH) represents a serious threat to the next COVID-19 mass immunization campaign. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vaccine hesitancy (VH) might represent a serious threat to the next COVID-19 mass immunization campaign. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the certainty ("might") though the substantive concern remains, so the claim is clarified with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest algorithm best predicts areas with a high risk of VH, improving the unpredictable baseline level by 24% in terms of accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Random Forest algorithm best predicts areas with a high risk of VH, improving the unpredictable baseline level by 24% in terms of accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and reported improvement are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proportion of waste recycling and the employment rate are the most powerful predictors of high VH among area-level indicators. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proportion of waste recycling and the employment rate are found to be the most powerful predictors of high VH. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The predictors named are the same but the publication expresses higher certainty, so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach can support policymakers to target area-level provaccine awareness campaigns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach can support policymakers to target area-level provaccine awareness campaigns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical policy-relevant conclusion with the same stated certainty in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 2.4 million patients in Japan need treatment for thyroid disease, but only 450,000 are receiving treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical epidemiologic statistic in the preprint does not appear in the publication, indicating it was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a machine learning method to construct a classification model for detecting hyperthyroidism and hypothyroidism using 11 routine laboratory tests. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated the potential of machine learning approaches for diagnosing the presence of thyroid dysfunction from routine laboratory tests. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a broader demonstration of potential rather than the development wording in the preprint, so the core contribution is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification models for hyperthyroidism and hypothyroidism achieved high classification accuracy as a result of cross-validation and external evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved high classification accuracies with AUROC of 93.8% for the hyperthyroidism model and 90.9% for the hypothyroidism model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication supplies specific AUROC metrics supporting the preprint's claim of high accuracy, so the result is clarified with more detail.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Serum creatinine, mean corpuscular volume, and total cholesterol were the three features most strongly correlated with the hyperthyroidism model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The feature-importance details appear in the publication but were not present in the preprint, so this is an added result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further validation, including prospective clinical studies, is necessary prior to application of our method in the clinic. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit limitation/recommendation for further validation that was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A metasurface platform for direct sensing of COVID-19 from unprocessed saliva has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gold-nanopatterned metasurface platform achieved 95.2% sensitivity and specificity in detecting SARS-CoV-2 from saliva samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the platform contribution but reports detection performance, clarifying the original development claim with quantitative results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning approach used in this study identifies the virus from Raman spectra with 95.2% sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of machine learning to analyze Raman spectra enhances the identification of SARS-CoV-2 in unprocessed saliva. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims emphasize ML on Raman spectra for identification, but the publication frames it more generally and with reduced certainty, so the claim is clarified with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method can distinguish between wild-type, alpha, and beta variants of SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The nanoplasmonic aptasensor can distinguish between wild-type, Alpha, and Beta variants of SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same variant-distinction result with consistent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of this study could pave the way for effective, safe, and quantitative preventive screening and identification of variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method may facilitate effective, safe, and quantitative preventive screening for COVID-19 variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the prospective public-health implications in similar terms, clarifying the potential application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques may result in potential biases when making decisions for people in different subgroups. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques may result in potential biases when making decisions for people in different subgroups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is unchanged but the publication expresses higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Algorithmic bias can lead to detrimental effects on the health and well-being of vulnerable groups such as ethnic minorities. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Algorithmic bias can lead to detrimental effects on the health and well-being of specific demographic groups such as vulnerable ethnic minorities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim about harms to vulnerable groups is preserved with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of algorithmic bias on medicine and effective mitigation strategies remains unclear. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The explicit statement about the unclear impact and mitigation uncertainty in the preprint does not appear among the publication claims, indicating removal.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents a comprehensive review of algorithmic fairness in the context of computational medicine. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a comprehensive review of algorithmic fairness in the context of computational medicine. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper summarizes popular software libraries and tools for bias evaluation and mitigation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper summarizes popular software libraries and tools for bias evaluation and mitigation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication lowers the stated certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate high predictability of antibiotic resistance across four antibiotics – nitrofurantoin, co-trimoxazole, ciprofloxacin, and levofloxacin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates high predictability of antibiotic resistance across four antibiotics: nitrofurantoin, co-trimoxazole, ciprofloxacin, and levofloxacin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in content and strength to the preprint claim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the generalizability of our methods on a separate cohort of patients with uncomplicated UTIs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods presented in the study are generalizable to a separate cohort of patients with uncomplicated UTIs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim about generalizability with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-based methods can help reduce the risk of non-susceptible treatment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-driven approaches can help alleviate the potential of administering non-susceptible treatments and facilitate rapid effective clinical interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about reducing non-susceptible treatments but adds facilitation of rapid interventions, expanding the claim's implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning-based methods facilitate rapid clinical intervention and enable more personalized approaches for treatment recommendation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning-driven approaches can help alleviate the potential of administering non-susceptible treatments and facilitate rapid effective clinical interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the rapid-intervention element but omits the claim about enabling more personalized treatment recommendations, thus weakening/narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our methods allow model interpretability that explains the basis for predictions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The techniques used in this study provide model interpretability, explaining the basis for generated predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the interpretability claim but presents it with higher certainty, so the content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NLP system achieved an overall precision of 0.85, recall of 0.93, and F1-measure of 0.89 to correctly identify ABI/TBI values and laterality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NLP system achieved an overall precision of 0.85, recall of 0.93, and F1 measure of 0.89 for identifying ABI/toe-brachial index values and laterality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Metrics and target extraction task are the same in preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NLP system achieved a positive predictive value of 92.3%, sensitivity of 83.1%, and specificity of 93.1% to identify PAD when compared to a structured chart review. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NLP system achieved a positive predictive value of 92.3%, sensitivity of 83.1%, and specificity of 93.1% for identifying PAD among patients undergoing ABI testing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core diagnostic performance metrics are identical, but the publication frames the evaluation population/context (patients undergoing ABI testing) rather than comparison to chart review, clarifying context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recall was higher for ABI (0.95 to 0.97) while precision was higher for TBI (0.94 to 0.95). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the ABI/TBI per-subtask precision/recall breakdown present in the preprint, indicating this specific claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have broad implications for PAD research and quality improvement efforts in large health systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings have broad implications for PAD research and quality improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the broad-implication conclusion with equivalent meaning and strength.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings of the study were consistent across a range of sensitivity analyses. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new claim about sensitivity analyses that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We successfully developed and validated an NLP system for identifying patients with PAD within the Veterans Affairs electronic health record. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the VA EHR deployment/validation context, a specific claim not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UDEs, particularly when supplied with learning biases, are capable of learning coupled behavior-disease dynamics and predicting second waves in a variety of populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UDEs are capable of learning coupled behavior-disease dynamics and predicting second waves in a variety of populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core capability of UDEs to learn coupled dynamics and predict second waves, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts a second wave of infections 55% of the time across all populations, having been trained only on the first wave. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric claim (55% of the time) and the exact evaluation framing do not appear in the publication, so this quantitative claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The biased model consistently predicts the expected increase in the transmission rate with rising mobility, whereas the unbiased model predicts a decrease in mobility as often as a continued increase. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of UDEs in making predictions depends on being supplied with learning biases that describe simple assumptions about disease transmission and population response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the finding more generally as dependence on learning biases rather than the specific directional prediction behaviors described in the preprint, so the claim is clarified/generalized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding additional terms to the universal differential equation’s loss function that penalize implausible solutions improves training time and leads to improved predictions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate potential benefits, drawbacks, and useful techniques when applying universal differential equations to coupled systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication abstracts specific methodological improvements into more general lessons about benefits, drawbacks, and useful techniques, broadening the original specific methodological claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Universal differential equation (UDE) models are able to extract the interplay between pandemic waves and population responses from data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level statement about extracting interplay is not directly mirrored by a single preprint claim (though related), so it is treated as an added generalized claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models, particularly gradient boosting algorithms, outperformed traditional warning signs in predicting severe dengue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML models, particularly gradient boosting algorithms, outperformed traditional warning signs in predicting severe dengue. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core finding that gradient boosting models outperform traditional warning signs with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient boosting algorithms achieved AUC-ROC values exceeding 94% for CatBoost, LightGBM, and XGBoost. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient boosting algorithms achieved the highest predictive performance for severe dengue, with an AUC-ROC of 97.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific, higher overall AUC (97.1%) compared with the preprint's '>94%' for multiple algorithms, revising the numeric performance and amplifying reported performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key predictors of severe dengue include hemoconcentration, leukopenia, and timing of presentation to a healthcare facility at 4–6 days post-symptom onset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature importance analysis identified hemoconcentration, leukopenia, and timing of presentation as key predictors of severe dengue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same key predictors but states them with higher certainty, so content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining three or more warning signs improved specificity to 80.9% while maintaining moderate sensitivity of 78.6%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining three or more warning signs improved specificity to 80.9% while maintaining moderate sensitivity of 78.6%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance of combining warning signs is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The subanalysis excluding hemoconcentration confirmed the models’ applicability in resource-limited settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The subanalysis excluding hemoconcentration confirmed the models’ applicability in resource-limited settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the subanalysis conclusion with equivalent wording and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate machine learning meta-models can be generated from simulation models with no strong interventions using small amounts of simulation data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate machine learning meta-models are generated from agent-based models (ABMs) without strong interventions using small amounts of simulation data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim (with explicit mention of ABMs) without changing the claim's meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Meta-models for simulation models that include strong interventions required much more training data to achieve a similar accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Meta-models for ABMs employing strong interventions require at least 60% of training data to achieve similar accuracy as those without strong interventions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the preprint's qualitative claim (specifying 'at least 60%'), clarifying the requirement while keeping the same overall point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning meta-models could be used in some scenarios to assist in faster decision making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning meta-models can assist in faster decision-making in some scenarios. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion but expresses it with higher certainty, so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning meta-models can be trained on a small subset of simulation runs to predict future disease trajectories resembling original simulation results. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broader conclusion about training on a small subset of runs to reproduce trajectories that was not explicitly stated in the preprint as a separate claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest model showed the highest predictive reliability, outperforming other algorithms in sensitivity and F-measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Random Forest model showed the highest predictive reliability, outperforming other algorithms in sensitivity and F-measure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is identical between preprint and publication with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using only the top-ranked features identified from the feature importance analysis, the model maintained comparable performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using only the top-ranked features identified from the feature importance analysis, the model maintained comparable performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same in wording and meaning; only the label changed from result to conclusion in the publication, not the claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings highlight the potential of machine learning in optimizing ischemic stroke treatment outcomes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings highlight the potential of machine learning in optimizing ischemic stroke treatment outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is preserved but the publication reports lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random Forest proved effective as a decision-support tool, offering clinicians valuable insights for more tailored treatment approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random Forest proved effective as a decision-support tool, offering clinicians valuable insights for more tailored treatment approaches. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical in wording, scope, and expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model’s use in clinical settings could significantly enhance patient outcomes by informing better treatment decisions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about direct clinical outcome improvement appears in the publication, so the preprint claim appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under ROC curves for seven imaging features and one diagnosis ranged from 0.71 to 0.93 on patients from the CheXnet dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific AUC range for the seven imaging features on CheXnet as stated in the preprint, so this claim is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning features between 'Worse' and 'Improved' outcome categories were significantly different for three radiological signs and one diagnostic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning predictions between 'Worse' and 'Improved' outcome categories were significantly different for three radiological signs and one diagnostic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result about significant differences across the listed radiological signs and diagnostic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Features from the first CXR of each pair could correctly predict the outcome category between 'Worse' and 'Improved' cases with 82.7% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Features from the first CXR of each pair could correctly predict the outcome category between 'Worse' and 'Improved' cases with a 0.81 AUC in the open-access dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core predictive claim remains but the reported performance metric changes from an 82.7% accuracy to a 0.81 AUC, so the quantitative performance statement was revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CXR deep learning features show promise for classifying the disease trajectory. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CXR deep learning features show promise for classifying disease severity and trajectory. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim to explicitly include disease severity in addition to trajectory, clarifying and broadening the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This information may be considered to inform triage decisions once validated in studies incorporating clinical data and with larger sample sizes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Once validated in studies incorporating clinical data and with larger sample sizes, this information may be considered to inform triage decisions. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is retained but the publication lowers the expressed certainty from medium to low, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHEPHERD is a few shot learning approach for multi-faceted rare disease diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHEPHERD is a few-shot learning approach for multi-faceted rare disease diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHEPHERD performs deep learning over a knowledge graph enriched with rare disease information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHEPHERD performs deep learning over a knowledge graph enriched with rare disease information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHEPHERD demonstrates effectiveness across diverse diagnostic tasks, including causal gene discovery and characterizing novel disease presentations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature ranking derived from statistically validated decision rules helped in automatically identifying the main factors influencing impaired quality of life. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The main factors influencing impaired quality of life include pharynx/larynx and upper airways when asthma is under control, and asthma itself and digestive trait when asthma is not controlled. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The obtained if-then rules identified specific thresholds on the symptoms associated with impaired quality of life. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings may prove helpful in supporting physicians in the choice of the most adequate diagnostic/therapeutic plan. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings may prove helpful in supporting physicians in the choice of the most adequate diagnostic/therapeutic plan. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding applicability to clinical support is retained without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical machine learning model can rapidly identify and trace the geographical source of Salmonella enterica serovar Enteritidis infections from whole genome sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hierarchical machine learning model was developed to rapidly identify and trace the geographical source of Salmonella enterica infections from whole genome sequencing data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved the highest classification accuracy at the continental level with a macro F1 score of 0.954. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest classification accuracy was achieved at the continental level with a macro F1 score of 0.954. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictions for countries commonly visited by UK travellers were made with high accuracy, achieving an hF1 score greater than 0.9. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions for countries commonly visited by UK travelers were made with high accuracy, achieving a hF1 score greater than 0.9. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions made by the model were robust to prospective external datasets as validated by longitudinal analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the specific claim about robustness to prospective external datasets and longitudinal validation, so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical machine learning framework facilitates rapid outbreak resolution and real-time genomic epidemiology by providing geographical source prediction in less than 4 minutes per sample. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical machine learning framework can provide geographical source predictions directly from sequencing reads in less than 4 minutes per sample. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-HOPE identified a significant enrichment of TP53 mutations in late-stage (III/IV) colorectal cancer compared to early-stage (I/II) cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-HOPE identified significant enrichment of TP53 mutations in late-stage colorectal cancer compared to early-stage cases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-HOPE revealed a significant association between KRAS mutations and poorer progression-free survival in FOLFOX-treated patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-HOPE uncovered a strong association between KRAS mutations and poorer progression-free survival in FOLFOX-treated patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-HOPE demonstrates the capability to independently uncover meaningful insights without prior user assumptions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-HOPE demonstrates the ability to generate meaningful insights independently, without prior assumptions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-HOPE represents a transformative advancement in precision medicine research, offering a scalable, user-friendly framework for integrating clinical and genomic data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this high-level 'transformative/scalable/user-friendly' characterization and instead emphasizes a different system feature (see added claim), so this preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-HOPE's closed-system design prevents clinical data leakage. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific contribution about a closed-system design preventing data leakage does not appear in the preprint and is newly stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT regurgitated extensive knowledge of cirrhosis and HCC, but only a small proportion of correct responses were labelled as comprehensive. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT regurgitated extensive knowledge of cirrhosis with 79.1% correctness and HCC with 74.0% correctness. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT answered 76.9% of quality measure questions correctly but failed to specify decision-making cut-offs and treatment durations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT answered 76.9% of questions correctly regarding quality measures but failed to specify decision-making cut-offs and treatment durations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compared to physicians/trainees, ChatGPT lacked knowledge of regional guidelines variations, such as HCC screening criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT lacked knowledge of regional guidelines variations, such as HCC screening criteria. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
