alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT provided practical and multifaceted advice to patients and caregivers regarding the next steps and adjusting to a new diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT's performance was better in basic knowledge, lifestyle, and treatment than in the domains of diagnosis and preventive medicine. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT may have a role as an adjunct informational tool for patients and physicians to improve outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT may have a role as an adjunct informational tool for patients and physicians to improve outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion verbatim, with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Among 58 variables measured at admission, 11 clinical and 3 radiological variables were associated with severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the same variable-count breakdown at admission; that specific enumerated-result is omitted from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CT image-based deep learning models predicted severity better than models based on the radiologists’ reports. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network analysis of CT scans brings unique prognosis information for predicting disease severity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same core finding about CT deep learning adding prognostic value, phrased to emphasize unique prognostic information rather than an explicit head-to-head with radiologist reports.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding CT scan information to clinical and biological data improves prediction of severity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of CT-scan information to clinical variables results in a measurable but limited 0.03 increase of AUC. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the improvement as measurable but limited (0.03 AUC), tempering the preprint's broader claim that CT adds predictive value.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CT scans contain novel and unique prognostic information, which we included in a 6-variable ScanCov severity score. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network analysis of CT scans brings unique prognosis information for predicting disease severity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-severity demonstrates significantly improved prognosis performance compared to 11 existing severity scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This comparative performance claim versus 11 existing scores is not present in the preprint claims and therefore appears as a new publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hybrid framework combining external device tracking and image-based tracking methods can localise the catheter tip target automatically. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid framework combines external electromagnetic tracking and deep learning-based image analysis to localise the catheter tip automatically. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The external device used in the framework is an electromagnetic tracking system from North Digital Inc (NDI). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The external device tracking method provides precise location determination of the catheter tip. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the precision outcome of the external electromagnetic tracking; the device provenance from NDI in the preprint is not reiterated but the functional claim is maintained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ultrasound image analysis in the framework is based on UNet, a deep learning network for semantic segmentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning platform effectively segments the exact catheter tip in ultrasound images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same deep-learning segmentation result; the reliance on UNet in the preprint aligns with the published segmentation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The external method determines the tip’s location precisely. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The external device tracking method provides precise location determination of the catheter tip. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is directly preserved in the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning platform segments the exact catheter tip automatically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning platform effectively segments the exact catheter tip in ultrasound images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same automatic segmentation result with unchanged scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This hybrid localisation framework offers a new solution for identifying moving medical devices in low-resolution ultrasound images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A supervised machine learning model was developed and validated to distinguish between influenza and COVID-19 infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A supervised machine learning model was developed and validated to distinguish between influenza and COVID-19 infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this development and validation claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model achieved an AUC of at least 91% when evaluated on 100 virtual patients from each cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model achieved an AUC of at least 91% when evaluated on 100 virtual patients from each cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The investigation highlighted the ability of machine learning models to accurately identify two different diseases based on major components of viral infection and immune response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicted a dominant role for viral load and productively infected cells through the feature selection process. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predicted a dominant role for viral load and productively infected cells through the feature selection process. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this model-derived insight identically, with the same level of certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed a machine learning model which predicts mortality for COVID-19 patients, resulting in an AUC of almost 80%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are essentially identical in content and metrics, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Molecular mechanisms related to dementia intersect with those associated with COVID-19, with tyrosine kinase 2 (TYK2) being a notable molecule. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Molecular mechanisms related to dementia intersect with those associated with COVID-19, with tyrosine kinase 2 (TYK2) being a notable molecule. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work demonstrates that interpretation of machine learning based risk models can point towards drug targets and new treatment options for COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work demonstrates that interpretation of machine learning based risk models can point towards drug targets and new treatment options for COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lesions in the temporal lobes, cerebellum, and the right occipital lobe are associated with an increased incidence of PTE. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lesions in the temporal lobes, cerebellum, and the right occipital lobe are associated with an increased posttraumatic epilepsy incidence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three distinct immunotypes were identified that predict very different clinical courses in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The immune-types do not associate with disease duration at hospital admittance but reflect variations in individual patient’s immune response. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning models predict patient outcomes of bloodstream infections with an area under the receiver-operating characteristics curve of 0.82 for a full featured inclusive model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine learning models predict patient outcomes of bloodstream infections with an area under the receiver-operating characteristics curve of 0.82 for a full featured inclusive model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same performance metric and claim as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The compact model using only 25 features achieved an area under the receiver-operating characteristics curve of 0.81. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The compact model using only 25 features achieved an area under the receiver-operating characteristics curve of 0.81. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and metric presented in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of these models may facilitate selective rapid microbiological identification, leading to earlier administration of appropriate antibiotic therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of these models may facilitate selective rapid microbiological identification, leading to earlier administration of appropriate antibiotic therapy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same implementation implication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models may help reduce the development of bloodstream infections and their associated adverse health outcomes and complications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models may help reduce the development of bloodstream infections and their associated adverse health outcomes and complications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this potential impact claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chest CT has high sensitivity in diagnosing novel coronavirus pneumonia (NCP) at an early stage, providing an advantage over nucleic acid detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chest computed tomography (CT) has high sensitivity in diagnosing novel coronavirus pneumonia (NCP) at the early stage. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the high sensitivity claim for CT at early-stage NCP; the advantage over nucleic acid detection is not emphasized but the core diagnostic sensitivity claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification schemes accurately distinguished NCP and influenza pneumonia (IP) lesions with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) above 0.93. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Trinary scheme accurately distinguished NCP from influenza pneumonia (IP) lesions, achieving an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.93. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Trinary scheme achieved a higher F1 score of 0.847 compared to the Plain scheme (0.774), specialists (0.785), and residents (0.644). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Trinary scheme outperformed the Plain scheme in patient-level classification, with an AUC of 0.87 compared to 0.71. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different metrics (patient-level AUCs) and omits the comparisons to specialists/residents and the F1 scores, revising and narrowing the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides an accurate early diagnosis tool on chest CT for NCP with high transferability and efficiency in differentiating NCP from IP. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides an accurate tool on chest CT for early diagnosis of NCP with high transferability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings could help to reduce misdiagnosis and contain the pandemic transmission. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader public-health impact statement (reducing misdiagnosis and containing transmission) does not appear in the preprint and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LVH-Fusion achieved an F1-score of 0.71 in predicting HCM and 0.96 in predicting HTN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LVH-fusion achieved an F1-score of 0.71 in predicting HCM and 0.96 in predicting HTN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The metrics and targets are identical between preprint and publication with only minor typographic capitalization differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LVH-Fusion had higher sensitivity and specificity rates than human readers in head-to-head comparisons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LVH-fusion had higher sensitivity and specificity rates than human readers in head-to-head comparisons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about comparative performance versus human readers is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can provide effective physician augmentation in diagnosing left ventricular hypertrophy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can provide effective physician augmentation in diagnosing left ventricular hypertrophy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about deep learning as physician augmentation is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnostic power of lateral T wave inversion on the ECG and proximal septal hypertrophy on the echocardiogram for HCM was confirmed through explainability techniques. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diagnostic power of lateral T-wave inversion on the ECG and proximal septal hypertrophy on the echocardiogram for HCM was confirmed through unsupervised analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The diagnostic finding is retained but the asserted supporting method changed from 'explainability techniques' to 'unsupervised analysis', altering the explanation of how the result was obtained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Support vector machines (SVM) models provided the greatest classification performances for different kernels, with PUK kernel providing accuracy of 89.14% for the detection of major depressive disorder (MDD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random tree models with 100 trees achieved a mean accuracy of 87.5575% for the detection of major depressive disorder. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The primary model and reported top accuracy changed between versions (SVM/89.14% → random tree/87.56%), indicating a substantive revision of the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of machine learning classifiers with vocal acoustics features has shown to be very promising for the detection of major depressive disorder. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of machine learning classifiers with vocal acoustic features is promising for the detection of major depressive disorder. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is retained with slightly toned phrasing ('very promising' → 'promising'), a minor wording clarification without scope change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further tests with a larger sample will be necessary to validate our findings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further experiments with a larger sample will be necessary to validate the findings of this exploratory study. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation/request for larger-sample validation remains but the publication expresses it with higher certainty (medium → high), increasing emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean kappa index, sensitivity, and specificity for the detection of major depressive disorder were 0.7508, 0.9149, and 0.8354, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This detailed performance statistics claim appears in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint, so it is an added result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Eye-tracking achieves accuracy and precision of 69% and 53% respectively compared to manual annotation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eye tracking achieves accuracy and precision of 69% and 53%, respectively, compared to manual annotation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric accuracy and precision results are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods for processing gaze points and scripts developed in this study offer a first step in developing novel uses for eye-tracking for clinical decision support. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods for processing gaze points developed in this study offer a first step in developing novel uses for eye tracking in clinical decision support. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study explores a novel application of eye tracking to collect training data for a machine learning-based clinical decision support tool. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates a novel application of eye tracking for collecting training data for a machine learning-based clinical decision support tool. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim language from 'explores' to 'demonstrates' and raises certainty from medium to high, amplifying confidence in the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained on all features outperform those using only significant features and frequently provide high predictive performance across nine health states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained with all features outperform those using only significant features and frequently provide high predictive performance across nine health state categories. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication adds a population qualifier ('for healthy participants') and lowers certainty, narrowing the claim and tempering confidence relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine learning models can discriminate between COVID-19 positive and negative patients with an accuracy ranging between 82% and 86%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine learning classification models achieved an accuracy ranging between 82% and 86% and sensitivity between 92% and 95% for discriminating between COVID-19 positive and negative patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The accuracy range is identical; the publication combines accuracy and sensitivity into one result statement but does not change the accuracy claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity of the machine learning models ranges between 92% and 95%, which is comparably effective to the gold standard rRT-PCR test. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine learning classification models achieved an accuracy ranging between 82% and 86% and sensitivity between 92% and 95% for discriminating between COVID-19 positive and negative patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The sensitivity values are the same in both versions; the publication reports them within a combined result statement rather than separately.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrated the feasibility and clinical soundness of using blood test analysis and machine learning as an alternative to rRT-PCR for identifying COVID-19 positive patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrated the feasibility and clinical soundness of using blood test analysis and machine learning as an alternative to rRT-PCR for identifying COVID-19 positive patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about feasibility as an alternative to rRT-PCR is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed Decision Tree model serves as a simple decision aid for clinicians interpreting blood tests for COVID-19 suspect cases. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An interpretable Decision Tree model was developed as a decision aid for clinicians interpreting blood tests for COVID-19 suspect cases. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the Decision Tree as an interpretable/simple decision aid for clinicians; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models explained up to 90% of variance in hospital admissions in 2019 and 75% in 2020 due to the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models explained up to 90% of variance in hospital admissions in 2019 and 75% in 2020 due to the effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The explained variance figures and attribution to COVID-19 effects are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model in 2019 was a machine learning model (elastic net) with a mean absolute error of 7.25. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model in 2019 was a machine learning model (elastic net) with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 7.25. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The 2019 best-model result and MAE are the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model in 2020 was a time series model that adjusted more quickly to the shock effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model in 2020 was a time series model with a mean absolute error (MAE) of 10.44. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific MAE for the 2020 best model; the core claim (time series model best) remains but numeric performance was clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weekly time series models did not perform better than monthly and yearly models in 2019 but did in 2020. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weekly time series models adjusted quicker to the COVID-19 related shock effects compared to other models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the quicker adjustment of weekly models (especially relevant to COVID shock), omitting the explicit 2019/2020 comparative framing, which narrows the temporal detail but preserves the core point about weekly models' responsiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different forecast horizons could be used simultaneously to allow both early planning and quick adjustments to external effects. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple individual forecast horizons could be used simultaneously for better planning and adjustment to sudden changes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation to use multiple forecast horizons for planning and adjustment is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inception-Resnet-v2 achieved a mean accuracy of 0.723 and a mean kappa of 0.506 without data augmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inception-ResNet-v2 achieved a mean accuracy of 0.723 and a mean kappa of 0.506 without data augmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical results and conditions are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data augmentation significantly improved the performance of Inception-Resnet-v2 to a mean accuracy of 0.857 and a mean kappa of 0.703. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data augmentation significantly improved the performance of Inception-ResNet-v2 to a mean accuracy of 0.857 and a mean kappa of 0.703. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported effect and metrics from augmentation are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study compared eleven different CNN architectures for classifying wrist radiographs into normal and abnormal categories. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study compared eleven different CNN architectures for classifying wrist radiographs into normal and abnormal categories. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Class Activation Mapping was used to interpret the network's activation in relation to the location of anomalies in the radiographs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This interpretation/visualization claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dynamic Causal Modeling (DCM) approach has been applied to the COVID-19 pandemic, providing insights into the mechanisms and latent factors driving its evolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dynamic causal modeling (DCM) framework has been applied to the COVID-19 pandemic to reveal hidden factors underlying the outbreak dynamics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert application of DCM to reveal latent/hidden factors; wording differs slightly but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DCM model accurately predicts the evolution of the COVID-19 pandemic, including social mobility and seroprevalence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DCM model demonstrated predictive validity by accurately forecasting the dynamics of outbreak diffusion across different regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains predictive validity but omits the specific mention of social mobility and seroprevalence, focusing on forecasting outbreak diffusion across regions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts that a second wave of COVID-19 could arise due to a loss of effective immunity after about 7 months. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific prediction about timing and cause of a second wave (loss of immunity after ~7 months) does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second wave of COVID-19 is predicted to be substantially worse if outbreaks are not promptly isolated and contained. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this specific claim about severity of a second wave conditional on containment speed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic causal modeling is a reliable tool for shaping and predicting the spread of COVID-19 and identifying effective containment strategies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DCM is a reliable tool for investigating the mechanisms governing the spread of SARS-CoV-2 and identifying effective containment and control strategies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state DCM is a reliable tool for understanding spread and identifying containment strategies with closely aligned scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A short-term prediction of the effective reproduction number (R t ) of COVID-19 in regions of Greece is achieved based on online mobility data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Short-term prediction of the effective reproduction number (Rt) of COVID-19 in regions of Greece is achieved based on online mobility data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result—short-term Rt prediction from mobility data in Greek regions—is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Various machine learning methods are applied to predict R t and attribute importance analysis reveals the most important variables affecting the accurate prediction of R t. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Random Forest algorithm achieved the highest R2 (approximately 0.8) and outperformed other models across all metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies Random Forest as the best-performing model and reports quantitative performance, narrowing the preprint's broader statement about multiple ML methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates robust results and the methodology represents a promising approach towards COVID-19 outbreak prediction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall methodology represents a promising approach to COVID-19 outbreak prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the methodology as promising; the publication expresses this at a slightly lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper can help health related authorities when deciding non-nosocomial interventions to prevent the spread of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim guidance value for health authorities regarding non-nosocomial interventions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Work and Park categories are identified as the most important mobility features affecting the accurate prediction of Rt. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The identification of specific mobility feature importance (Work and Park) appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first laparoscopic real-time multispectral imaging (MSI) system has been developed, featuring a compact and lightweight multispectral camera. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A laparoscopic real-time multispectral imaging system was developed to enhance tissue perfusion characterization during surgery. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the system development claim but omits emphasis on 'first' and specific camera form-factor, and emphasizes its purpose for perfusion characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A first-in-human trial performed on 10 patients demonstrates the feasibility of the MSI approach for fully-automatic live ischemia monitoring during laparoscopic surgery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An in-human trial demonstrates the feasibility of the approach for functional laparoscopic imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the feasibility trial but removes some specific wording (e.g., '10 patients', 'fully-automatic live ischemia monitoring'), broadening the statement to functional imaging feasibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed MSI-based method does not require the injection of a contrast agent and is repeatable if the wrong segment has been clamped, compared to the clinical state-of-the-art approach based on indocyanine green (ICG) fluorescence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for ischemia detection does not rely on data from any other patient and utilizes an ensemble of invertible neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the emphasis: it highlights the modeling approach (invertible neural networks) and not the contrast-agent-free / repeatability comparison to ICG, so the original comparative claim is altered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spectral imaging combined with advanced deep learning-based analysis tools could evolve as an important tool for fast, efficient, reliable and safe functional imaging in minimally invasive surgery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of spectral imaging and advanced deep learning-based analysis tools has the potential to provide fast, efficient, reliable, and safe functional laparoscopic imaging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both conclude that the combination of spectral imaging and deep learning has potential to provide fast, efficient, reliable, and safe functional laparoscopic imaging with equivalent nuance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A genetic test that could identify people who are at high risk of developing glioblastoma multiforme could improve our understanding of this form of brain cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identification of an effective germ line genetic test to stratify risk of developing glioblastoma multiforme should lead to a better understanding of how this cancer forms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about a germ line genetic test improving understanding of glioblastoma; wording is closely aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm could classify TCGA glioblastoma multiforme patients from the other TCGA patients with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.875. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning algorithm could classify TCGA glioblastoma multiforme patients from other TCGA patients with an AUC of 0.875. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim (AUC=0.875) is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest ranked quintile by the machine learning algorithm had an odds ratio of 3.78 higher than the average odds ratio. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest ranked quintile by the machine learning algorithm had an odds ratio of 3.78 higher than the average odds ratio. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The odds-ratio result is reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identification of an effective germ line genetic test to stratify risk of developing glioblastoma multiforme should lead to a better understanding of how this cancer forms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identification of an effective germ line genetic test to stratify risk of developing glioblastoma multiforme should lead to a better understanding of how this cancer forms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion is directly carried over from preprint to publication with the same emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Common germ line DNA copy number variations were found in the TCGA population. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result about common germ line DNA copy number variations is present in the publication but not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This result might ultimately lead to better treatments of glioblastoma multiforme. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The suggestion that the findings could ultimately improve treatments is an added forward-looking statement in the publication not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning quantification method using Trainable Weka Segmentation (TWS) demonstrates strong correlation with manual segmentation in both training and application sets of images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TWS is strongly correlated with manual segmentation in both the training set (R = 0.98; p = 0.0033) and the application set of images (R = 0.94; p = 0.016). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result and provides correlation coefficients, directly matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TWS outperforms the color thresholding method in quantifying amyloid deposition in histological slides of ligamentum flavum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Color thresholding was weakly correlated with manual segmentation in both the training set (R = 0.78; p = 0.12) and the application set (R = 0.65; p = 0.23). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents data showing weak performance of color thresholding while presenting strong TWS correlations, which clarifies and provides numeric context; the reported certainty for color-threshold results is lower than in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel machine learning method for quantifying amyloid deposition is a precise, objective, accessible, high throughput, and powerful tool for future research and clinical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This novel machine learning method to quantify amyloid deposition is a precise, objective, accessible, high throughput, and powerful tool for future research and clinical applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding statement with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Establishing clusters of Parkinson's disease patients based on their progression patterns can illuminate clinical differences among patient subgroups. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work contributes to a better understanding of the heterogeneity in Parkinson's disease and suggests potential biological pathways and genes underlying these differences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the conclusion similarly but emphasizes biological pathways and is reported with lower certainty, thus clarifying and slightly tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of an artificial intelligence-based algorithm allowed for the identification of specific clusters of Parkinson's disease that showed significantly different patterns of progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Specific clusters of Parkinson's disease patients were identified that showed significantly different patterns of disease progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same result (identification of clusters with different progression patterns) with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of genetic variants and biomarker data enabled the association of established progression clusters with distinct biological mechanisms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The established progression clusters were associated with distinct biological mechanisms, including perturbations in vesicle transport and neuroprotection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific biological mechanisms underlying the associations described in the preprint, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients in identified progression clusters exhibited significant differences in their responsiveness to symptomatic treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients within identified progression clusters exhibited significant differences in their responsiveness to symptomatic treatment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result verbatim and with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IDentif.AI demonstrated that the optimal combination therapy against SARS-CoV-2 was comprised of remdesivir, ritonavir, and lopinavir, which mediated a 6.5-fold improvement in efficacy over remdesivir alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IDentif.AI pinpointed the optimal drug combination as remdesivir, ritonavir, and lopinavir, which was experimentally validated to mediate a 6.5-fold enhanced efficacy over remdesivir alone. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the identical experimental result and effect size reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IDentif.AI showed hydroxychloroquine and azithromycin to be relatively ineffective. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hydroxychloroquine and azithromycin were shown to be relatively ineffective against SARS-CoV-2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same finding but with higher stated certainty than the preprint, representing an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identification of a clinically actionable optimal drug combination was completed within two weeks, with a 3-order of magnitude reduction in the number of tests typically needed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study was completed within 2 weeks, with a three-order of magnitude reduction in the number of tests needed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the time-to-completion and efficiency reduction claims verbatim and with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IDentif.AI analysis was able to independently confirm clinical trial outcomes to date without requiring any data from these trials. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IDentif.AI independently mirrored clinical trial outcomes to date without any data from these trials. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same independence claim but with lower stated certainty and frames it more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of the IDentif.AI platform suggests that it may be applicable towards rapid development of optimal drug regimens to address current and future outbreaks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness of this digital drug development approach paired with in vitro experimentation and AI-driven optimization suggests that IDentif.AI may be clinically actionable toward current and future outbreaks. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same forward-looking conclusion but adds emphasis on pairing with in vitro work and reports lower certainty, thus clarifying and tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models developed in this study demonstrated high predictive performance for reencounters leading to hospitalization with an AUC of 79.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forest models achieved significant improvement over baseline performance in predicting reencounters and urosepsis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports improved predictive performance for the same outcomes but frames the result in terms of improvement over baseline rather than reporting the specific AUC value from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models also achieved an AUC of 90% for predicting urosepsis diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models can predict hospital admission and urosepsis diagnosis for patients with an outpatient UTI encounter. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the feasibility of predicting urosepsis but expresses the finding more generally without the specific AUC point estimate, thus clarifying/formulating the claim at a higher level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patient age, sex, and urinary WBC count were identified as the top three predictive features for the models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified patient age, sex, and urinary WBC-count as the top three predictive features for urosepsis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same top-feature result with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study has the potential to assist clinicians in identifying high-risk patients and improving patient care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive power of the models represents meaningful clinical risk stratification despite the challenge of predicting rare events. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the potential clinical utility more cautiously and in context of rare-event prediction challenges, clarifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This scoping review will explore and map the existing scientific evidence and knowledge on the use of machine learning (ML) in risk communication for healthcare workers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This scoping review will explore and map the existing scientific evidence and knowledge on the use of machine learning in risk communication for healthcare workers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same stated contribution and scope as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study allows for comprehensive mapping of existing evidence on ML in occupational risk communication. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This scoping review will explore and map the existing scientific evidence and knowledge on the use of machine learning in risk communication for healthcare workers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same planned comprehensive mapping of evidence; the publication restates this contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of ML models is dependent on the quality of the data used. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The specific limitation about data-quality dependence is not present in the publication's claims or methods summary, indicating it was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of ML in healthcare requires careful evaluation of ethical, legal, and privacy issues. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the stated ethical/legal/privacy implementation considerations, so this preprint limitation appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most respondents are familiar with AICs and half used AICs in their own research.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report respondents' familiarity or the fraction who have used AICs; this preprint claim is not represented in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 11.4% of respondents reported that their institution offered training on using AI tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 11.4% of respondents reported having institutional training on AI tools.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same statistic and meaning verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Respondents expressed interest in learning more and receiving training on AIC use in the scientific process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A substantial majority of researchers (69.7%) expressed interest in further training on AI chatbots.
alignments.rationale: Both describe respondents' interest in additional training; the publication supplies a quantified proportion but preserves the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Respondents had mixed opinions about the potential benefits of using AICs, but most agreed on their cons/challenges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A significant portion of researchers (66.9%) valued AI chatbots for reducing administrative workload.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a clear benefit (administrative workload reduction) whereas the preprint emphasized mixed views and predominant concerns; the tone and emphasis have shifted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Respondents were most concerned about the lack of understanding behind how AICs make decisions and generate responses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is insufficient understanding of the decision-making process related to AI chatbots, as indicated by 77.2% of respondents.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same concern about lack of understanding, with the publication adding a quantified percentage but preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Top journals are more likely to provide author guidelines and reviewer guidelines compared to whole-spectrum sample journals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A higher proportion of top SJR ranked journals provided author guidelines compared to the random sample of non-top SJR ranked journals.
alignments.rationale: The publication supports the higher provision of author guidelines among top journals but does not explicitly corroborate the reviewer-guideline component, narrowing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SJR score was positively associated with the provision of author guidelines and references to external guidelines among whole-spectrum sample journals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A higher SJR score was positively associated with providing GAI usage guidelines for both authors and reviewers among the random sample of non-top SJR ranked journals.
alignments.rationale: Both describe a positive association with SJR, but the publication frames it for GAI guidelines and for both authors and reviewers in a different (random non-top) sample, altering scope and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The provision of GAI usage guidelines is limited across medical journals, especially for reviewer guidelines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both groups of journals had lower percentages of journals providing recommendations for data analysis and interpretation.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents low percentages for certain recommendation types, but shifts emphasis toward data analysis/interpretation recommendations rather than explicitly stating limited GAI usage guidelines for reviewers as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of specificity and consistency in existing guidelines highlights areas deserving improvement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some recommendations remained unspecified, indicating a potential gap that warrants attention.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same concern about unspecified recommendations and frames it as a gap needing attention, clarifying the observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immediate attention is needed to guide GAI usage in scholarly publishing to safeguard integrity and trust in medical research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Collaborative efforts are needed to develop specific recommendations that better guide authors and reviewers.
alignments.rationale: Both call for action to improve guidance; the publication frames the response as collaborative development of recommendations and presents the conclusion with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recurrent neural network detected aortic stenosis with a sensitivity of 90.9% and specificity of 94.5%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of aortic stenosis (AS) was detected with a sensitivity of 90.9% and a specificity of 94.5%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same performance metrics for AS detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm's performance in detecting aortic stenosis exceeded that of similar studies based on selected cohorts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrated excellent performance in detecting AS in a general cohort, surpassing observations from similar studies on selected cohorts.
alignments.rationale: Both statements make the same comparative conclusion about outperforming prior selected-cohort studies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Detection of aortic and mitral regurgitation based on heart sound audio was unreliable, but sensitivity improved significantly for symptomatic cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detection of aortic and mitral regurgitation (AR and MR) based on heart sound audio was poor, but accuracy was considerably higher for symptomatic cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about poor detection overall with better performance in symptomatic individuals.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Including clinical variables as predictors increased the area-under-the-curve for aortic and mitral regurgitation detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of clinical variables improved the performance of the model significantly for predicting AR and MR.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same improvement in performance when clinical variables are included.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Screening jointly for at least moderate regurgitation or presence of stenosis detected 54.1% of positive cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Screening jointly for symptomatic regurgitation or presence of stenosis resulted in an AUC of 0.86, with 97.7% of AS cases detected.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different metrics (AUC and a very high AS detection percentage) and frames the joint screening with emphasis on symptomatic regurgitation, changing the earlier numeric detection claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LSTM-Climate-Spatial model consistently demonstrated superior performance in forecasting dengue cases across Brazil's 27 federal states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LSTM-Climate-Spatial model consistently demonstrated superior performance in forecasting dengue cases across Brazil's 27 federal states.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same primary result about the model's superior performance across all states.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHAP-enhanced variable selection improved accuracy by focusing on key drivers such as temperature, precipitation, and humidity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHAP-enhanced variable selection improved accuracy by focusing on key drivers such as temperature, precipitation, and humidity.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about SHAP-enhanced variable selection and key drivers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of spatial effects further strengthened forecasts in highly connected states, showcasing the model’s adaptability and robustness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of spatial effects further strengthened forecasts in highly connected states, showcasing the model’s adaptability and robustness.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same result with increased certainty (publication-level claim marked high certainty), indicating amplified confidence in this finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study presents a scalable and robust framework for dengue forecasting that effectively integrates temporal, climatic, and spatial information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents a scalable and robust framework for dengue forecasting across Brazil, effectively integrating temporal, climatic, and spatial information.
alignments.rationale: Both texts present the same contribution; the publication emphasizes geographic scope (across Brazil) but preserves the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model’s successful application across Brazil’s diverse regions demonstrates its generalizability to other dengue-endemic areas with varying climatic and epidemiological conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit claim about generalizability to other endemic areas that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recurrence rate of rectal cancer after curative resection was found to be 13.2%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recurrence rate of rectal cancer after curative resection was found to be 13.2%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same recurrence rate figure and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The support vector machine (SVM) method demonstrated the highest area under the curve (AUC) of 0.831 among the machine learning techniques used.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The support vector machine (SVM) method demonstrated the highest area under the curve (AUC) of 0.831 among the machine learning techniques used.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same performance metric and ranking for SVM.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variable pT was identified as the most influential factor for predicting recurrence across all machine learning methods except logistic regression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pathologic Tumor stage (pT) was identified as the most influential factor for rectal cancer recurrence across all machine learning methods except logistic regression.
alignments.rationale: Both state the same finding about pT being the most influential predictor, with identical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinicians should be more alert if patients have a high pT stage during postoperative follow-up in rectal cancer patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with a high pT stage during postoperative follow-up require closer surveillance to monitor for recurrence.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same clinical recommendation (closer surveillance for high pT patients) with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hippocampus, fusiform, and inferior temporal gyri are the strongest predictors of Alzheimer's disease progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hippocampus, fusiform, and inferior temporal gyri are the strongest predictors of AD dementia progression. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same specific brain regions as strongest predictors with equivalent certainty and wording, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method was proposed to evaluate the degree to which specific regional atrophy is predictive of Alzheimer's disease progression. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method evaluates the degree to which specific regional atrophy patterns are predictive of AD dementia progression. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same methodological contribution with slightly clarified wording (adds 'patterns' and 'AD dementia') but preserves the original intent and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study's findings are in agreement with established staging models of Alzheimer's disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Occlusion of limbic ROIs defined according to Braak stage III had the largest impact on the performance of the model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific result tied to Braak stage III (related to staging models) but does not explicitly assert the broader claim of agreement with established staging models, changing the claim's framing and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thrombolysis use in patients arriving within 4 hours of stroke onset varied from 7% to 49% between hospitals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thrombolysis use in patients arriving within 4 h of known or estimated stroke onset ranged 7% -49% between hospitals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric range and context with negligible wording differences, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The odds of receiving thrombolysis reduced 9 fold over the first 120 minutes of arrival-to-scan time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The odds of receiving thrombolysis reduced 9-fold over the first 120 min of arrival-to-scan time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative temporal effect with identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hospital attended explained 56% of the variance in between-hospital thrombolysis use. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of between-hospital variance in thrombolysis use may be explained by differences in in-hospital processes and differences in attitudes to judging suitability for thrombolysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint stated a specific numeric attribution (56% explained by hospital attended); the publication omits the numeric estimate and reframes attribution toward processes/attitudes with more tentative language ('may'), so the claim is revised and somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using explainable machine learning, we identified that differences in in-hospital processes and attitudes explain the majority of the variation in thrombolysis use. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of between-hospital variance in thrombolysis use may be explained by differences in in-hospital processes and differences in attitudes to judging suitability for thrombolysis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about processes and attitudes but uses more cautious wording ('may be explained'), clarifying and slightly tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sybil outperforms a conventional data-centric approach in forecasting epidemic trends. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sybil outperforms conventional data-centric approaches in forecasting epidemic trends. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sybil accurately forecasts changes in the trend of the pandemic evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sybil accurately forecasts changes in the trend of the pandemic evolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sybil is capable of predicting the future prevalence of new variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sybil is capable of predicting the future prevalence of new variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same capability claim with unchanged certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning model achieved moderate but statistically significant accuracy in the menarche classification task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning model achieved moderate but statistically significant accuracy in the menarche classification task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim verbatim with identical certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Continuous menarche class probabilities were positively associated with brain age gaps. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous menarche class probabilities were positively associated with brain age gaps. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The association is presented the same way in the publication with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Menarche class probabilities were associated with age at menarche, while brain age gaps were not. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Menarche class probabilities, but not brain age gaps, were associated with age at menarche. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same directional association but downgrades certainty from high to medium, so the semantic content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the use of a machine learning model to classify menarche status from structural MRI data while accounting for age-related neurodevelopment. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the use of a machine learning model to classify menarche status from structural MRI data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the demonstration claim but omits the explicit phrase about accounting for age-related neurodevelopment, narrowing the scope of the stated contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work suggests that menarche class probabilities may be developed toward an objective brain-based marker of pubertal development. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that menarche class probabilities may be developed toward an objective brain-based marker of pubertal development. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same cautious translational suggestion with equivalent low certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ali-M3 demonstrates excellent performance for the detection of COVID-19 patients with a sensitivity and specificity of 98.5% and 99.2%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these extreme sensitivity/specificity figures and instead reports more moderate performance metrics, so this specific claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the curve (AUC) of Ali-M3 for predicting a COVID-19 diagnosis was 0.797. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ali-M3 demonstrates an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.797 for predicting a COVID-19 diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC value and phrasing, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With a cut-off of probability of COVID-19 by Ali-M3 diagnosis set at 0.5, the sensitivity and specificity were 80.6% and 68.3%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensitivity and specificity of Ali-M3 at a cut-off probability of 0.5 for diagnosing COVID-19 are 80.6% and 68.3%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same thresholded sensitivity and specificity values with identical certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sensitivity increased for both cut-off values after 5 days from symptom onset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensitivity of Ali-M3 increases after 5 days from symptom onset. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the temporal sensitivity increase claim (both statements align in meaning and certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In patients requiring oxygen support, the AUC was higher at 0.825. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this subgroup AUC figure; instead it focuses on overall performance and other conclusions, so this specific subgroup claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analysis identifies requirements of efficacy, coverage, and duration of effect for five novel malaria interventions to achieve targeted reductions in malaria prevalence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis identifies requirements of efficacy, coverage, and duration of effect for five novel malaria interventions to achieve targeted reductions in malaria prevalence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim are textually identical and convey the same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Coverage is generally identified as the largest driver of impact, but higher efficacy, longer protection duration, or multiple deployments per year are needed to increase prevalence reduction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Coverage is generally identified as the largest driver of impact, but higher efficacy, longer protection duration, or multiple deployments per year are needed to increase prevalence reduction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is unchanged between preprint and publication in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interventions on multiple parasite or vector targets, as well as combinations of the new interventions with drug treatment, lead to significant burden reductions and lower efficacy or duration requirements. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interventions targeting multiple parasite or vector targets, as well as combinations of the new interventions with drug treatment, lead to significant burden reductions and lower efficacy or duration requirements. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach uses disease dynamic models and machine learning to support decision-making and resource investment, facilitating development of new malaria interventions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach uses disease dynamic models and machine learning to support decision-making and resource investment, facilitating development of new malaria interventions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion wording and emphasis are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis allows prioritization of interventions and their specifications from an early stage in development, enabling cost-effective investments towards impact maximization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis allows prioritization of interventions and their specifications from an early stage in development, enabling cost-effective investments towards impact maximization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The composite 'rECHOmmend' model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.91 and a positive predictive value (PPV) of 42% at 90% sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The composite rECHOmmend model achieved a 0.91 area under the receiver operating characteristic curve and a 42% positive predictive value at 90% sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Metrics and phrasing are equivalent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual disease models had AUROCs ranging from 0.86 to 0.93 and lower PPVs from 1% to 31%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual disease models had area under the receiver operating characteristic curves ranging from 0.86 to 0.93, with positive predictive values between 1% and 31%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numerical performance ranges and interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-site validation of the model showed an aggregate AUROC of 0.91 across an independent test set of 11 clinical sites. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The composite rECHOmmend model achieved a 0.91 area under the receiver operating characteristic curve and a 42% positive predictive value at 90% sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core aggregate performance (AUROC 0.91) is retained but the publication omits the explicit "multi-site / 11 sites" framing, narrowing the stated validation context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ECG-based machine learning model can predict previously undiagnosed, clinically significant structural heart disease while outperforming single disease models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ECG-based machine learning model can identify a high-risk population for undiagnosed, clinically significant structural heart disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the conclusion more strongly (higher certainty) as identifying a high-risk group; this preserves the core predictive claim but amplifies confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach can facilitate targeted screening with echocardiography to improve under-diagnosis of structural heart disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach can facilitate targeted screening with echocardiography to improve underdiagnosis of structural heart disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The practical screening implication is maintained with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The beat-to-beat deep learning algorithm surpasses human expert performance in segmenting the left ventricle, estimating ejection fraction, and assessing cardiomyopathy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EchoNet-Dynamic algorithm surpasses the performance of human experts in segmenting the left ventricle, estimating ejection fraction, and assessing cardiomyopathy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance superiority claim with matching metrics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accurately segments the left ventricle with a Dice Similarity Coefficient of 0.92 and predicts ejection fraction with a mean absolute error of 4.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EchoNet-Dynamic accurately segments the left ventricle with a Dice similarity coefficient of 0.92 and predicts ejection fraction with a mean absolute error of 4.1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Metrics and phrasing are consistent between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model has less variance than human experts in evaluating cardiac function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has variance that is comparable to or less than that of human experts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the claim (comparable to or less than) and lowers stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can identify subtle changes in ejection fraction by leveraging information across multiple cardiac cycles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about leveraging multiple cardiac cycles to detect subtle EF changes does not appear in the publication; related external-evaluation claims were added instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides one of the largest medical video datasets of over 10,000 annotated echocardiograms to promote further innovation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A large dataset of 10,030 annotated echocardiogram videos is made publicly available to promote further innovation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset release claim is preserved with the same scale and purpose; publication gives precise count.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved a per-patient sensitivity of 100%, specificity of 93.55%, and accuracy of 95.24% in detecting COVID-19 pneumonia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model achieved a per-patient accuracy of 95.24% and a per-image accuracy of 98.85% in the internal retrospective dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core accuracy (95.24%) is preserved but the publication emphasizes per-image accuracy and omits the per-patient sensitivity/specificity figures, narrowing the reported metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's performance was comparable to that of expert radiologists when evaluating 27 prospective patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrated comparable performance to that of expert radiologists for detecting COVID-19 pneumonia. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core comparison to expert radiologists though it frames it as a system-level result; meaning is consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the model significantly decreased the reading time of radiologists by 65%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model significantly decreased the reading time of radiologists by 65%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reading-time reduction figure and significance are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model has the potential to relieve the pressure of frontline radiologists and improve early diagnosis and treatment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model improved the efficiency of radiologists in clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's potential benefit as an observed improvement in efficiency, clarifying the practical impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved an accuracy of 91.2% for classifying COVID-19 and bacterial pneumonia. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieved an accuracy of 91.2% for classifying COVID-19 and bacterial pneumonia patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The classification accuracy claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrated a 95% accuracy for severity classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric claim of 95% severity-classification accuracy does not appear in the publication; the paper reports improved severity classification but omits the explicit 95% figure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Twenty patterns were found to have a major effect on the classification performance of the deep learning model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two clusters showed typical findings of COVID-19, while two other clusters showed typical findings related to bacterial pneumonia. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes specific cluster findings rather than the broader "twenty patterns" indicated in the preprint, representing a narrowed/revised characterization of influential patterns.
