alignments.pub_claim_text: Sensitivity of detecting IgM test bands was 100%, and specificity was 95.8%.
alignments.rationale: The IgM detection sensitivity and specificity claims remain the same between preprint and publication (minor CI formatting difference only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All COVID-19 antigen RDTs were correctly read by the app.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All COVID-19 antigen RDTs were correctly read by the app.
alignments.rationale: The result that the app correctly read all antigen RDTs is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed reading system is automatic, reducing variability and uncertainty associated with RDTs interpretation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reading system is automatic, reducing variability and uncertainty associated with RDTs interpretation.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about automation reducing interpretive variability is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform can serve as a real time epidemiological tracking tool and facilitate reporting of positive RDTs to relevant health authorities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The web platform serves as a real-time epidemiological tracking tool and facilitates reporting of positive RDTs to relevant health authorities.
alignments.rationale: The platform's role as a real-time tracking and reporting tool is restated in the publication with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lil’Flo is a socially assistive robotic telerehabilitation system designed for community deployment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lil'Flo represents a novel approach to delivering rehabilitation care in the community while maintaining the clinician-patient connection.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the community-deployment aim but frames Lil'Flo as a novel approach that emphasizes clinician-patient connection, adding nuance to the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The humanoid robot has sufficient range of motion and can clearly communicate several emotions.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention the robot's range of motion or emotional communication capabilities, so this preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lil’Flo is portable, extensible, and cheaper than the prior iteration of the system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lil'Flo is more portable, extensible, and cheaper than the prior iteration of the system.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about portability, extensibility, and lower cost with essentially identical phrasing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system allows operators to focus on interactions while the computer vision system stores data for analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lil'Flo is more capable of remote stretching, strength building, and motor assessments than traditional video only telepresence.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized an operator-focused workflow and data capture; the publication shifts to a stronger claim about clinical assessment capabilities relative to video-only telepresence, broadening the functional claim and expressed with higher confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinicians believe Lil'Flo could be deployed in rural and elder care facilities.
alignments.rationale: This deployment-in-context claim appears in the publication but was not present among the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods can be used to identify women at risk for postpartum hemorrhage who may benefit from individualized preventative measures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methods can be used to identify women at risk for postpartum hemorrhage who may benefit from individualized preventative measures.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion about using ML to identify at-risk women with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model including all data mildly outperformed the second stage model with an AUC of 0.979 compared to 0.955.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model including all data mildly outperformed the second stage model with an AUROC of 0.979 compared to 0.955.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance comparison is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using 28 predictor features, the model successfully classified 73.7% of patients who ultimately had a postpartum hemorrhage using information available prior to delivery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models limited to data available prior to delivery perform nearly as well as those with more complete data sets.
alignments.rationale: The preprint gives a specific pre-delivery performance figure; the publication generalizes this into a broader conclusion about pre-delivery-limited models performing nearly as well, with somewhat lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models stratified by delivery method achieved good to excellent discrimination but noted lower sensitivity and need further investigation.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention stratified-model sensitivity limitations or the need for further investigation, so this limitation appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many previously identified risk factors for postpartum hemorrhage were not included in the final model, potentially discounting their contribution to hemorrhage risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An unbiased approach to hemorrhage risk prediction may be superior to human risk assessment.
alignments.rationale: The preprint noted omission of known risk factors as a limitation; the publication emphasizes the potential strength of an unbiased ML approach versus human assessment, reframing the concern into a broader methodological claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient boosted decision trees achieved the best discrimination in the overall model for predicting postpartum hemorrhage.
alignments.rationale: The publication names the specific algorithm achieving best discrimination, which was not present among the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The occurrence of cerebrovascular events (CVE) after transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVR) is associated with an increased risk of death.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The occurrence of CVE is associated with an increased risk of death, with a hazard ratio of 2.62.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same association and provides a quantified hazard ratio, adding precision to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning-based predictive model for TAVR-related CVE was constructed using less than 107 clinical and imaging variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model utilizes less than 107 clinical and imaging variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution about the model using <107 variables with equivalent framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.79, indicating its performance in predicting 30-day CVE.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The constructed predictive model has an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.79, indicating its performance in predicting 30-day CVE.
alignments.rationale: The AUC result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TAVR-related CVE can be estimated using the developed deep learning-based predictive algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cerebrovascular events (CVE) can be predicted using a deep learning-based predictive algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same predictive/conclusion claim about CVE estimation using the DL algorithm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning method performed similarly to radiologists and outperformed previously reported methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning method for classifying time since stroke (TSS) performed similarly to board-certified neuroradiologists' assessments of DWI-FLAIR mismatch.
alignments.rationale: The publication corroborates similar performance to neuroradiologists; the preprint's additional claim of outperforming prior methods is addressed elsewhere in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best model achieved an average evaluation accuracy of 0.726, sensitivity of 0.712, and specificity of 0.741 on an internal cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best deep learning model achieved an average evaluation accuracy of .726 on an internal cohort and .724 on an external cohort for TSS classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the internal accuracy and adds external cohort performance, broadening the evaluation results beyond the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved higher generalization performance on external evaluation datasets than the current state of the art for TSS classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model achieved higher generalization performance on external evaluation datasets than the current state-of-the-art for TSS classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about superior generalization on external datasets.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning technique demonstrates the potential for automatic assessment of onset time from imaging without the need for expertly trained radiologists.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds this broader implication about automating assessment without expert radiologists, which was not present among the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The miLab TM algorithm for malaria detection achieved a total accuracy of 98.86% for infected red blood cell (RBCs) classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The miLab™ algorithm for malaria detection achieved a total accuracy of 98.86% for infected red blood cell (RBC) classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the exact reported accuracy result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinical validation performed in Malawi demonstrated an overall percent agreement of 92.21%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinical validation performed in Malawi demonstrated an overall percent agreement of 92.21%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the clinical validation agreement percentage verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The miLab TM method minimizes human error and facilitates remote assistance and review by experts through digital image transmission.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method minimizes human error and facilitates remote assistance and review by experts through digital image transmission.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same conclusion about reducing human error and enabling remote expert review.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed miLab TM device incorporates a solid hydrogel staining method for consistent blood film preparation.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention the specific solid hydrogel staining method, so this technical contribution is not represented in the provided publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This method can set a new paradigm for on-site malaria diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The miLab™ can become a reliable and efficient tool for decentralized malaria diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's broader, more grandiose claim about 'setting a new paradigm' is softened in the publication to a more measured statement about becoming a reliable, efficient decentralized diagnostic tool.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The document classification model achieved an F1 score of 0.75, surpassing the predictive performance of a psychiatry team who reviewed the same documents, which had an F1 score of 0.5. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The document classification model achieved an F1 score of 0.75, surpassing the predictive performance of a psychiatry team who reviewed the same documents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric performance and direct comparison to the psychiatry team with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The baseline regression model using structured data achieved an F1 score of 0.72. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structured data model achieved an F1 score of 0.72, which also exceeds the performance of the psychiatry team. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same numeric result but adds an explicit comparison to the psychiatry team, clarifying the context of the performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the first deep learning model capable of predicting violent events within healthcare settings using clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the first deep learning model capable of predicting violent events within healthcare settings using clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding claim verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The named entity recognition classifier trained on annotations achieved an overall F1 score of 0.7. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The named entity recognition classifier achieved an overall F1 score of 0.7, aiding in the explainability and understanding of risk factors for violent events. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same performance metric but adds an explainability interpretation while lowering stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods developed in this study can be generalized and extended to enable intervention at other hospital systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods developed in this study can be generalized and extended to enable intervention at other hospital systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same generalizability/contribution claim with similar certainty; only the label (contribution) changed, not the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conjunctive clause evolutionary algorithm (CCEA) effectively selects features significant for distinguishing individuals with and without autism spectrum disorder (ASD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conjunctive clause evolutionary algorithm (CCEA) effectively selects features significant for distinguishing individuals with and without autism spectrum disorder (ASD). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Potential biomarker candidates for ASD include brain volume, area, cortical thickness, and mean curvature in specific regions around the cingulate cortex, frontal cortex, and temporal-parietal junction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Potential biomarker candidates for ASD include brain volume, area, cortical thickness, and mean curvature in specific regions around the cingulate cortex, frontal cortex, and temporal-parietal junction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same biomarker candidates and regional specificity; only the label (result vs conclusion) differs, not the content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study findings demonstrate that machine learning tools can improve diagnostic and predictive models of ASD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study findings demonstrate that machine learning tools can improve diagnostic and predictive models of ASD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interaction-based prediction model outperforms the polygenic risk score (PRS) with an AUC of 0.85 compared to the PRS AUC of 0.58. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interaction-based prediction model achieved an AUC of 0.85, significantly outperforming the polygenic risk score model which had an AUC of 0.58. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication provide the same AUC values and the same comparative claim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data preprocessing using data-mining of contextual information enables machine learning algorithms to identify gene-gene interactions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel machine learning algorithms can identify gene-gene interactions to improve predictive performance over polygenic risk scores. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint focused on a preprocessing contribution enabling interaction discovery; the publication reframes this as a broader conclusion about novel algorithms' ability to find interactions and improve prediction, with stronger stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature importance analysis of the model provided insights into the mechanism of Parkinson’s Disease, revealing interactions of drug target candidate genes TMEM175 and GAPDHP25. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature importance analysis of the model provided insights into the mechanism of Parkinson's disease, revealing interactions between drug target candidate genes TMEM175 and GAPDHP25. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same specific mechanistic insight but states it with lower certainty, so meaning is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interaction-based machine learning models can improve genetic prediction models and might provide an answer to the missing heritability problem. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interaction-based machine learning models might provide a solution to the missing heritability problem in genetic prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same cautious conclusion about addressing missing heritability, with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An inherently interpretable model for 3-day ICU readmission prediction was developed using a retrospective cohort of 15,589 ICU stays. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed explainable boosting machine used 67 features and showed an area under the precision-recall curve of 0.119 ± 0.020 and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.680 ± 0.025. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms development of the interpretable model and adds implementation details (67 features) and performance metrics; this clarifies the original contribution without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting model showed an area under the precision-recall curve of 0.119±0.020 and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.680±0.025. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed explainable boosting machine used 67 features and showed an area under the precision-recall curve of 0.119 ± 0.020 and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.680 ± 0.025. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numerical performance metrics for the model, adding only auxiliary implementation detail (feature count).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's performance is on par with state-of-the-art gradient boosting machines and outperforms the Simplified Acute Physiology Score II. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The explainable boosting machine outperformed the Simplified Acute Physiology Score II, logistic regression, and recurrent neural networks in predicting ICU readmission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the comparative claim to additional models (logistic regression and RNNs) while retaining the core assertion about outperforming SAPS II, thus broadening the scope of the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: External validation with the Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care database version IV confirmed the findings of the study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: External validation confirmed that explainable boosting machines performed similarly to gradient boosting machines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state that external validation confirmed the study's findings; the publication specifies the nature of the similarity to gradient boosting machines but does not change the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning model for readmission prediction with a high level of human control is feasible without sacrificing performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is feasible to develop ML models that allow a high level of human control without sacrificing performance for low- to medium-dimensional datasets common in health care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the feasibility claim but narrows it to low- to medium-dimensional datasets and lowers stated certainty, so the claim is clarified, narrowed, and confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model inspection process showed that explainable boosting machines can be useful to detect and remove problematic risk functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This methodological observation about model inspection and removing problematic risk functions was not present in the preprint and appears as a new contribution in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All 30 studies developed a new machine learning model and none sought to validate an existing model, producing a total of 39 new models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All 30 studies developed a new ML model and none sought to validate an existing ML model, producing a total of 39 new ML models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same aggregate result about number of studies and new models with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean area-under-the-curve for all models and endpoints was 0.91 (SD 0.08). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean area under the curve for all models and endpoints was 0.91 (SD 0.08). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numerical summary of model performance without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The risk of bias was high for at least one domain in all 39 models, particularly for the analysis domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The risk of bias was high for at least one domain in all 39 models, particularly for the analysis domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same risk-of-bias finding verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Studies reported on only 15 out of 27 items from the TRIPOD+AI standards, indicating significant deficiencies in reporting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Studies reported on only 15/27 items from TRIPOD+AI standards, indicating poor adherence to reporting guidelines. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The core reporting-adherence finding (15/27 items) is unchanged, but the publication frames it with lower certainty and a different label, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are significant deficiencies in the methodology and reporting of recently published machine learning based prognostic models in acute pancreatitis patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are significant deficiencies in the methodology and reporting of recently published ML based prognostic models in AP patients. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same high-level conclusion about deficiencies in methodology and reporting; only the label changed to 'limitation' without altering substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated natural language processing (NLP) pipeline developed in this study has a precision of 0.85 for identifying men who have reported on Twitter that they are gay, bisexual, or MSM.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated natural language processing (NLP) pipeline developed in this study has a precision of 0.85 for identifying men who have reported on Twitter that they are gay, bisexual, or MSM.
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical between preprint and publication with the same metric and interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 58.2% of the identified users are located in the 10 states with the highest numbers of new HIV diagnoses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 58.2% of the identified users were located in the 10 states with the highest numbers of new HIV diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: Same numeric finding; only minor tense wording change does not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 68.1% of the users with county-level geolocation are in counties or states considered priority jurisdictions by the Ending the HIV Epidemic (EHE) initiative.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 68.1% of the identified users were in counties or states considered priority jurisdictions by the Ending the HIV Epidemic initiative.
alignments.rationale: Same statistic and context reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of identified users are in the same two age groups as the majority of MSM in the United States with new HIV diagnoses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The age distribution of the identified users reflects that of MSM in the United States with new HIV diagnoses.
alignments.rationale: Publication wording is slightly more general (age distribution reflects) versus the preprint's specific "same two age groups," so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated NLP pipeline lays the groundwork for using Twitter on a large scale to target PrEP-related interventions directly at MSM in the United States.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated NLP pipeline lays the groundwork for using Twitter on a large scale to directly target PrEP-related interventions at MSM in the United States.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion language is effectively the same in preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SMD network is characterized by strong autocorrelations and predominantly positive connections, with aggression and tearfulness identified as the most central features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Aggression and tearfulness were identified as the most central prodromal features in the severe mental disorder network.
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the identification of aggression and tearfulness as central features but omits the explicit statements about strong autocorrelations and predominantly positive connections, narrowing the original composite claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UMD sub-network showed few significant differences compared to PSY and BMD, indicating a high degree of similarity among these disorders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sub-networks for unipolar mood disorders, bipolar mood disorders, and psychotic disorders showed minimal differences in their connections.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about minimal differences across sub-networks using slightly different terminology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides further evidence to support transdiagnostic early detection services across severe mental disorders.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides evidence to support transdiagnostic early detection services across severe mental disorders.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over verbatim in meaning from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that central features identified in the SMD network could serve as potential intervention targets.
alignments.rationale: Publication reports central features but does not make the translational recommendation that these features could serve as intervention targets; therefore the recommendation was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FGP-NET achieved a high area under the curve (AUC) of 0.969 and 0.855 for internal and external testing, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FGP-NET achieved AUCs of 0.969 and 0.847 for internal and external tests, respectively.
alignments.rationale: Internal AUC is identical but the external AUC differs slightly between preprint and publication, so the numeric result has been revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HONORS-guided FGP-NET identified benign nodules with a high sensitivity of 95.5% in the screening scenario.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HONORS-guided FGP-NET identified benign nodules with high sensitivity (95.5%) and specificity (72.5%) in screened nodules.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same high sensitivity and additionally provides specificity, clarifying the screening-performance profile.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of HONORS and FGP-NET provides well-organized stratification for pulmonary nodules and offers the potential for reducing medical errors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HONORS might reliably contribute a second opinion, aiding in optimizing the clinical workflow.
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the system more cautiously as a second-opinion support and workflow aid rather than asserting broad claims about stratification and reducing medical errors, narrowing and tempering the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FGP-NET detected lung cancer with a high specificity of 94.5% in routine diagnostic scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FGP-NET demonstrated a higher sensitivity (93.3%) but a relatively inferior specificity (64.0%) compared to the average sensitivity and specificity of 126 radiologists.
alignments.rationale: Publication reports a substantially lower specificity and frames performance relative to radiologists, revising and tempering the preprint's high-specificity claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients undergoing radiotherapy experience an accelerated aging rate of 2.78 years per year, significantly higher than the normal aging rate of 1 year per year.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The linear mixed effects model resulted in an accelerated aging rate of 2.78 years/year after radiotherapy, which is significantly higher than the normal aging rate of 1 year/year.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same modeled accelerated aging rate and interpretation as reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The saliency maps identified numerous anatomically well-defined areas, such as Heschl’s gyrus, that are important for predicting brain age.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Saliency maps identified numerous anatomically well-defined areas, such as Heschl's gyrus, that are important for predicting brain age.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication reports this result with slightly reduced reported certainty (medium), so confidence is marked as tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Radiotherapy leads to significant radiation-induced accelerated aging in patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients undergoing radiotherapy experience significant post-radiation accelerated aging.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is restated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimated brain age could serve as a method for quantifying quality of life post-radiotherapy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated brain age could provide a method for quantifying quality of life post-radiotherapy.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same potential application of estimated brain age for QoL quantification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model (DL-binary) achieved clinician level performance in ascertaining knee osteoarthritis cases from DXA scans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep-learning model trained on knee DXA scans achieved clinician-level performance in ascertaining knee osteoarthritis cases.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same performance claim with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of the DL-binary model identified 2,603 (240%) more knee osteoarthritis cases than currently diagnosed in the ICD-10 record.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using our model, we identified 1931 (178%) more cases of knee osteoarthritis than currently diagnosed in the health record.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a substantially different case-count and percentage increase, revising and reducing the magnitude of the original numeric claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The heritability of minimum knee joint space width (mJSW) was an order of magnitude greater than that of the ICD-10 code M17 or DL-binary phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific heritability comparison claimed in the preprint; that heritability claim is absent in the published text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A GWAS run on mJSW identified 18 genome-wide significant loci, compared to only 1 and 6 for the DL-binary and ICD-10 code M17 phenotypes, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of a quantitative measure of knee joint space width improved the number of genome-wide significant loci discovered by an order of magnitude compared to a binary model of cases and controls.
alignments.rationale: Publication conveys the same core finding (order-of-magnitude improvement in discovered loci) in more general terms rather than listing the specific locus counts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reduced mJSW is associated with increased risk of adult fractures, while neither case-control phenotype is associated with this risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We discovered associations between our quantitative measure of knee osteoarthritis and increased risk of adult fractures.
alignments.rationale: Publication reports the association between the quantitative measure and fractures but does not reiterate the preprint's negative finding about the case-control phenotypes, therefore the comparative part of the original claim was removed/revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 22.5% of patients had vitamin B1 deficiency, 16.1% had vitamin B12 deficiency, and 14.5% had folate deficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 22.5% of patients had vitamin B1 deficiency, 16.1% had vitamin B12 deficiency, and 14.5% had folate deficiency.
alignments.rationale: The numeric prevalence values and phrasing are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learning models were well generalized to predict vitamin deficiencies in future unseen data, particularly using random forest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest model showed the best performance in predicting vitamin deficiencies, with areas under the receiver operating characteristic curves of 0.716, 0.599, and 0.796 for the validation dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's emphasis on random forest but adds specific performance metrics (AUCs), clarifying how the model generalized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates that machine-learning can efficiently predict some vitamin deficiencies in patients with active psychiatric symptoms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine-learning models can efficiently predict vitamin deficiencies in patients with active psychiatric symptoms.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion wording and meaning are effectively the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a potential association between vitamin deficiencies and alkaline phosphatase (ALP) or thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH).
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a potential association between vitamin deficiencies and alkaline phosphatase (ALP) or thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH).
alignments.rationale: The association claim is preserved with the same qualifiers and certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An ensemble machine learning approach incorporating gait, physical activity, and clinical/demographic features showed good performance for predicting cartilage worsening over two years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An ensemble machine learning model was developed to predict worsened cartilage MRI Osteoarthritis Knee Score at follow-up from gait, physical activity, clinical and demographic data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution (ensemble ML using the same feature types) with consistent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The median AUC across the 100 held-out test sets was 0.73, indicating the model's predictive capability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning approach showed good performance for predicting cartilage worsening over 2 years, with a median area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.73.
alignments.rationale: The reported median AUC of 0.73 and interpretation of good performance are directly preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High lateral ground reaction force impulse, more time spent lying, and low vertical ground reaction force unloading rate were associated with increased risk of cartilage worsening over two years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Baseline cartilage damage, higher Kellgren-Lawrence grade, greater pain during walking, higher lateral ground reaction force impulse, greater time spent lying, and lower vertical ground reaction force unloading rate were associated with greater risk of cartilage worsening.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the three gait/activity features from the preprint but additionally lists several other baseline risk factors and reports the associations with higher certainty, broadening and amplifying the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results suggest that lateral ground reaction force impulse, time spent lying, and vertical ground reaction force unloading rate should be investigated further as potential early intervention targets to reduce medial tibiofemoral cartilage worsening.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lateral ground reaction force impulse, time spent lying, and vertical ground reaction force unloading rate should be investigated further as potential early intervention targets to reduce medial tibiofemoral cartilage worsening.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is preserved verbatim but the publication assigns a higher certainty level (medium vs. preprint's low), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Random Forest model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.94, significantly outperforming other models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Random Forest model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.94, significantly outperforming other models.
alignments.rationale: The performance metric and comparative statement are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates significant improvements in predicting sepsis outcomes using a Random Forest model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates significant improvements in predicting sepsis outcomes using a Random Forest model, supported by advanced machine learning techniques and thorough data preprocessing.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the main conclusion but adds methodological detail (techniques and preprocessing), clarifying the basis for the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model provided detailed insights into the importance of various clinical features, with the Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score and average urine output being highly predictive.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provided detailed insights into the importance of various clinical features, with the Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score and average urine output being highly predictive.
alignments.rationale: The feature-importance finding is retained but the publication raises the stated certainty to high, representing an amplification in confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SHAP analysis enhanced the model's interpretability, offering a clearer understanding of feature impacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provided detailed insights into the importance of various clinical features, with the Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score and average urine output being highly predictive.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly name SHAP but conveys the same interpretability/feature-importance result; certainty is increased, so the claim is clarified and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach offers a valuable tool for healthcare professionals to make data-driven decisions, ultimately aiming to minimize sepsis-induced fatalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach enhances the model’s practical utility in clinical settings, offering a valuable tool for healthcare professionals to make data-driven decisions.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same claim about practical clinical utility; wording differences do not change meaning or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVID-19Net was notably more accurate than the other models in predicting confirmed cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COVID-19Net was notably more accurate than other models such as CNN, GRU, and CNN-GRU.
alignments.rationale: The comparative accuracy claim is preserved, with the publication listing specific comparator models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean absolute percentage error generated by COVID-19Net was 1.447 for Germany, 1.801 for Italy, and 2.828 for Spain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute percentage error generated by COVID-19Net was 1.447 for Germany, 1.801 for Italy, and 2.828 for Spain.
alignments.rationale: The numerical error values are identical and retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework can accurately predict the accumulated number of confirmed cases in Germany, Italy, and Spain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COVID-19Net framework can accurately predict the accumulated number of confirmed COVID-19 cases in Germany, Italy, and Spain.
alignments.rationale: The claim is unchanged in substance, but the publication assigns a higher certainty level (high vs. preprint's medium), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results serve as a crucial reference for devising public health strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework could serve as an essential reference for devising public health strategies.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same suggestion about policy relevance with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of glioma polygenic risk scores with radiomics increased the AUC for distinguishing IDH-wildtype vs. IDH-mutant glioma from 0.83 to 0.88 in the elastic net model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of glioma polygenic risk scores with radiomics increased the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve for distinguishing IDH-wildtype vs. IDH-mutant glioma.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim that adding polygenic risk scores increased AUC; the preprint's numeric specifics are consistent with this statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating age at diagnosis and sex further improved the classifiers, achieving an AUC of 0.93 for both the elastic net and neural network models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating age at diagnosis and sex further improved the classifiers, achieving an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.93 for both elastic net and neural network models.
alignments.rationale: The performance improvement and numerical AUC equal 0.93 are carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients predicted to have IDH-mutant tumors had significantly lower mortality risk compared to those with IDH-wildtype tumors, with a hazard ratio of 0.18.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients predicted to have IDH-mutant tumors had significantly lower mortality risk compared to those with IDH-wildtype tumors.
alignments.rationale: The mortality-risk finding is preserved; the publication omits the numeric hazard ratio in the short claim but conveys the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The augmentation of imaging-based classifiers with genetic risk profiles may help delineate molecular subtypes of gliomas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The augmentation of imaging-based classifiers with genetic risk profiles may help delineate molecular subtypes of gliomas.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is identical in scope and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic data can be effectively used throughout the modelling pipeline even without eventual access to the real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic data can be effectively used throughout the medical prognostic modeling pipeline even without eventual access to the real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are effectively identical aside from the addition of 'medical prognostic' as context in the publication; core result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analyses performed on synthetic data can be applied downstream without modification, allowing synthetic data to act in place of real data for a wide range of use cases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analyses performed on synthetic data can be applied downstream without modification, allowing synthetic data to act in place of real data for a wide range of use cases. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Text is the same between preprint and publication; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The role that synthetic data might play in all aspects of clinical model development remains unknown. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The role that synthetic data might play in all aspects of clinical model development remains unknown. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is carried forward verbatim, so semantic, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different data release approaches have implications on how synthetic data could be deployed within the healthcare system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different data release approaches have implications on how synthetic biobank data could be deployed within the healthcare system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies 'synthetic biobank data', narrowing the domain compared with the broader 'synthetic data' in the preprint while keeping the same implication and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resting-state functional connectivity (rsFC) could be a specific predictor of performance in contingency-based cognitive flexibility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interhemispheric resting-state functional connectivity in the posterior parietal cortex could be important for optimal learning of the task. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies interhemispheric posterior parietal rsFC (narrower) and lowers certainty; core idea that rsFC relates to task performance is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Groups showed an optimal performance in the acquisition phase of the probabilistic reversal learning task (PRLT), with higher performance of healthy controls compared to the diagnostic groups in the reversal block. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The groups showed optimal performance in the discrimination block, with healthy controls outperforming patients in the reversal block. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core comparison (controls outperforming patients in reversal) is unchanged, but the named phase changed from 'acquisition' to 'discrimination', a substantive revision of the task phase description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parameters studied (reinforcement learning rate, punishment learning rate, and inverse temperature) predict task performance differently by phase and group. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model parameters predicted task performance differently by phase and group, indicating distinct mechanisms of learning and flexibility. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same finding and interpretation about model parameters and phase/group differences with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: rsFC between left posterior parietal cortex (lpPC) and right posterior parietal cortex (rpPC) seems to credibly predict performance in the acquisition block in healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interhemispheric resting-state functional connectivity in the posterior parietal cortex could be important for optimal learning of the task. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication generalizes the specific lpPC–rpPC/acquisition/healthy-control result to a broader claim about interhemispheric PPC connectivity and optimal learning, and reports lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding sensitivity to punishment or reinforcement and its influence on decision-making may be important for designing case-specific interventions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OCD appears to show greater sensitivity to punishment when there is uncertainty about the behavior-outcome association, while ADHD is guided by sensitivity to reinforcement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides disorder-specific detail linking sensitivity to punishment/reinforcement to OCD vs ADHD, which clarifies and grounds the preprint's general conclusion about interventions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The logistic regression, XGBoost, and random forest models achieved AUROC scores of 95.0, 94.0, and 94.0 respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The logistic regression, XGBoost, and random forest models achieved AUROC scores of 95.0, 94.0, and 94.0 respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported AUROC results are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Obesity, anosmia, de novo diabetes, myalgia, and nasal discharge are identified as the top five variables positively impacting the risk of mucormycosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified obesity, anosmia, de novo diabetes, myalgia, and nasal discharge as the top five variables positively impacting the risk of mucormycosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Top predictors are carried forward verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model has the potential to predict patients at high risk of mucormycosis, facilitating preventive care and early detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed model has the potential to predict patients at high risk of mucormycosis, facilitating preventive care and early detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the model's potential is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study holds potential for early treatment and better management of patients suffering from COVID-19 associated mucormycosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this broader management/early treatment claim present in the preprint, so it appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system achieved radiologist-level accuracy in identifying breast cancer in ultrasound images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI system achieves radiologist-level accuracy in identifying breast cancer in ultrasound images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The central claim of radiologist-level accuracy is maintained verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.976 on a test set of 44,755 exams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI achieves an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.976 on a test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The AUROC value is preserved but the publication omits the explicit exam count; the core performance claim remains but with a minor loss of dataset detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Radiologists using the AI system decreased their false positive rates by 37.4% and reduced the number of requested biopsies by 27.8%, while maintaining the same level of sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With the help of the AI, radiologists decrease their false positive rates by 37.3% and reduce requested biopsies by 27.8%, while maintaining the same level of sensitivity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reader-study finding is present in both versions; numeric difference (37.4% vs 37.3%) is negligible and the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system achieved an AUROC of 0.911 on an independent external test dataset, confirming its generalizability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the claimed external AUROC of 0.911, so this specific external-validation claim appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed QA framework consistently exhibited a clear advantage over traditional information retrieval approaches and bespoke BERT-family models across 31 datasets of four categories of systematic reviews. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed question-answering framework using large language models consistently exhibited a clear advantage over traditional information retrieval approaches and bespoke BERT-family models across 31 datasets of 4 categories of systematic reviews. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative result, only expanding 'QA' to 'question-answering' and 'LLMs' phrasing; meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs demonstrated proficiency in prioritising candidate studies for abstract screening using the proposed QA framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models demonstrated proficiency in prioritizing candidate studies for abstract screening using the proposed QA framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim, reworded (British vs US spelling), identical substantive content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant performance improvements were obtained by re-ranking answers using the semantic alignment between abstracts and selection criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant performance improvements were obtained by re-ranking answers using the semantic alignment between abstracts and selection criteria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this specific methodological/result claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The investigation justified the indispensable value of leveraging selection criteria to improve the performance of automated abstract screening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The investigation justified the indispensable value of leveraging selection criteria to improve the performance of automated abstract screening. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding interpretation about leveraging selection criteria is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLM-assisted brain MRI differential diagnosis yielded superior accuracy compared to conventional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLM-assisted brain MRI differential diagnosis yielded superior accuracy compared to conventional internet search.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the comparator as "conventional internet search," narrowing the original broader wording from the preprint while preserving the core result and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was no difference in interpretation time between LLM-assisted and conventional methods.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about interpretation time appears in the publication, so this preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human-LLM collaboration has the potential to improve brain MRI differential diagnosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human-LLM collaboration has the potential to improve brain MRI differential diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion with essentially identical wording and stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several challenges related to human errors and technical limitations were identified in the study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inaccurate case descriptions by readers, LLM hallucinations, and insufficient contextualization of LLM responses were identified as challenges related to human-LLM interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists specific challenge types (reader errors, hallucinations, contextualization) that make the preprint's general limitation claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 demonstrated superior performance in diagnostic tasks and medical information propagation compared to other models like Google’s Bard.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert explicit superiority of GPT-3.5/GPT-4 over Bard; it focuses on task proficiency and variability rather than direct model-to-model superiority.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 demonstrated particularly high accuracy in tasks such as interpreting hematology cases and diagnosing hemoglobinopathy, with performance metrics of 76% diagnostic accuracy and 88% accuracy in identifying normal blood cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models demonstrated proficiency in specific tasks, achieving 76% diagnostic accuracy for haemoglobinopathies.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 76% diagnostic accuracy for haemoglobinopathies reported in the preprint; numerical alignment supports an unchanged mapping (other numbers about cell ID are less emphasized in the publication).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study revealed discrepancies in model consistency and the accuracy of provided references, indicating variability in their reliability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research highlighted inconsistencies in performance and reference accuracy, indicating variability in reliability across different uses.
alignments.rationale: Both texts describe the same finding of inconsistent performance and reference accuracy; wording is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs present significant opportunities for advancing clinical hematology, but their incorporation into medical practice requires careful evaluation of their benefits and limitations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: While LLMs provide notable advantages in enhancing diagnostic processes and educational resources within haematology, their integration into clinical practice requires careful consideration.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same balanced conclusion about potential benefits and the need for careful integration; emphasis and confidence are similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT significantly outperformed Bard and trainees, achieving an average total score of 82.2%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT significantly outperformed Bard and trainees, achieving an average total score of 82.2%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this exact performance claim with the same metric and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT's performance was notably stronger in difficult questions compared to Bard and trainees.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT's performance was notably stronger in difficult questions compared to Bard and trainees.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same comparative result regarding performance on difficult questions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT showed a high consistency rate of 80%-85% across three tests, whereas Bard exhibited greater variability with consistency rates of 54%-61%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT showed a high consistency rate of 80%-85% across three tests, whereas Bard exhibited greater variability.
alignments.rationale: Consistency rates and the contrast between ChatGPT and Bard are preserved in the publication, with equivalent conclusions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: While LLMs show significant promise in pathology education and practice, continued development and human oversight are crucial for reliable clinical application.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continued development and human oversight are crucial for reliable clinical application of LLMs.
alignments.rationale: The publication isolates and affirms the preprint's emphasis on the need for further development and oversight for safe clinical use.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Open-source large language models (LLMs) outperformed the baseline pattern-matching approach in extracting social determinants of health (SDoH) data from free-text clinical notes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Open-source large language models (LLMs) outperformed the baseline pattern-matching approach in extracting social determinants of health (SDoH) data from free-text clinical notes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same primary result about outperforming pattern-matching methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best-performing model, openchat_3.5, achieved higher overall accuracy across all nine SDoH aspects compared to the baseline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model openchat_3.5 was the best-performing model, achieving higher overall accuracy across all nine SDoH aspects compared to the baseline.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication identify openchat_3.5 as the top performer with the same comparative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The refined pipeline with prompt engineering reduced hallucinations and improved accuracy in extracting SDoH data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The refined pipeline with prompt engineering reduced hallucinations and improved accuracy in extracting SDoH data.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same methodological improvement and its effects on hallucinations and accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Open-source LLMs are effective and scalable tools for extracting SDoH from unstructured electronic health records (EHRs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Open-source LLMs are effective and scalable tools for extracting SDoH from unstructured EHRs, surpassing traditional pattern-matching methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly adds that these LLMs surpass traditional pattern-matching methods, making the conclusion more specific but aligned with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further refinement and domain-specific training of LLMs could enhance their utility in clinical research and predictive analytics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further refinement and domain-specific training of LLMs could enhance their utility in clinical research and predictive analytics.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for further refinement and domain training is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall accuracy rate significantly improved in Condition 2 compared to Condition 1 (70.2% vs. 64.9%, p=0.029).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall accuracy rate significantly improved in Condition 2 compared to Condition 1 (70.2% vs. 64.9%, p =  0.029).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numeric comparison and significance for Condition 2 vs Condition 1.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy rate significantly decreased in Condition 3 compared to Condition 1 (59.9% vs. 70.2%, p<0.001).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy rate significantly decreased in Condition 3 compared to Condition 1 (59.9% vs. 64.9%, p =  0.027).
alignments.rationale: The publication corrects the comparison values and reports a different p-value (less extreme), indicating a revision of the numeric result and a tempered statistical strength while preserving the direction of the effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Providing context about prior probabilities significantly affects the diagnostic performance of the LLM in radiological cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Providing information that may influence prior probabilities significantly affects the diagnostic performance of the LLM in radiological cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion about the influence of prior-probability information on LLM diagnostic performance using slightly different wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results suggest LLMs may utilize Bayesian-like principles in diagnostic reasoning, similar to human radiologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs may incorporate Bayesian-like principles and adjust the weighting of their diagnostic responses based on prior information.
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates on how Bayesian-like reasoning might manifest (adjusting weighting based on priors), clarifying the preprint's more general suggestion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MentalQLM achieves an average weighted F1-score of 0.778 on mental disorder diagnosis across five benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MentalQLM achieves an average weighted F1-score of 0.778 on mental disorder diagnosis across five benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MentalQLM outperforms the state-of-the-art instruction-tuned MentaLLaMA-Chat-13B model by 3.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MentalQLM outperforms the state-of-the-art instruction-tuned MentaLLaMA-Chat-13B model by 3.2%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact repetition of the performance comparison with identical numeric difference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MentalQLM outperforms the few-shot tuned GPT-4 model by 17.7%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MentalQLM outperforms the few-shot tuned GPT-4 model by 17.7%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim and numeric margin are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MentalQLM is positioned as a cost-effective and efficient solution for real-world mental healthcare applications, especially in computationally constrained environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MentalQLM is positioned as a cost-effective and efficient solution for real-world mental healthcare applications, especially in computationally constrained environments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical framing and qualification of the system's intended use and constraints.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A diagnosis aid model for early identification of suspected COVID-19 pneumonia (S-COVID-19-P) was developed and validated without the use of CT images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A diagnostic model was developed and validated for the early identification of suspected COVID-19 pneumonia patients without relying on CT images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved a recall score of 100% in the validation group for identifying suspected COVID-19 pneumonia patients. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's performance resulted in area under the ROC curves of 0.841 and 0.938 for the testing set and validation group, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has been made available as an online triage tool for early identification of S-COVID-19-P. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ML-based predictive model can accurately anticipate who will respond well to aquapheresis therapy and who will be at high risk of future heart failure events. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our deep learning method produced estimates with a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 8.79 ± 0.21. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model estimates of cancer risk perform at a similar level to human experts, within uncertainty bounds. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our modeled 'optimal method' produced image predictions with a RMSE of between 7.98 and 8.90 for cranial caudal images. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The disease outcome can be predicted with a ROC AUC of 0.92, Sensitivity of 0.88 and Specificity of 0.82 for the best prediction model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The disease outcome can be predicted with a Receiver Operating Characteristic AUC of 0.92, Sensitivity of 0.88 and Specificity of 0.82 for the best prediction model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric performance metrics and claim with equivalent wording (ROC AUC vs spelled-out), so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques fed with demographic and clinical data along with comorbidities of the patients can assist in the prognostic prediction and physician decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques fed with demographic and clinical data along with comorbidities of the patients can assist in the prognostic prediction and physician decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate diagnosis of patients with COVID-19 is extremely important to offer adequate treatment and avoid overloading the healthcare system. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate diagnosis of patients with COVID-19 is extremely important to offer adequate treatment and avoid overloading the healthcare system. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is a preliminary retrospective study which can be improved with the inclusion of further data. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate high R 2 values between the labels and predictions for the in-distribution, out-of-distribution, and country-based cross-validation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates high R² values for in-distribution predictions of COVID-19 infection growth using machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The differing accuracies obtained from the three tasks suggest a strong influence of the use case. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The differing accuracies of the models suggest a strong influence of the use case on the prediction of confirmed infection growth. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about use-case influence on model accuracy with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work provides new considerations in using machine learning techniques with non-pharmaceutical interventions and cultural dimensions data for predicting the national growth of confirmed infections of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides new considerations for using machine learning techniques with nonpharmaceutical interventions and cultural dimensions to predict COVID-19 infection growth. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution with the same scope and certainty; wording is slightly condensed but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatENT showed enhanced performance in the analysis and interpretation of OHNS information, outperforming ChatGPT 4.0 in both the Canadian Royal College OHNS sample examination questions challenge and the US board practice questions challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatENT showed enhanced performance in the analysis and interpretation of OHNS information, outperforming ChatGPT4.0 in both the Canadian Royal College OHNS sample examination questions challenge and the US board practice questions challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatENT achieved a 58.4% and 26.0% error reduction in the respective challenges compared to ChatGPT 4.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatENT achieved a 58.4% and 26.0% error reduction in the respective examination challenges compared to ChatGPT4.0. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatENT generated fewer hallucinations and demonstrated greater consistency than ChatGPT 4.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatENT generated fewer hallucinations and demonstrated greater consistency than ChatGPT4.0. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatENT is the first specialty-specific LLM in the medical field. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatENT is the first specialty-specific knowledge retrieval artificial intelligence in the medical field that utilizes the latest LLM. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fine-tuned model has demonstrated the capacity to overcome the limitations of existing LLMs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has demonstrated the capacity to overcome the limitations of existing LLMs, signaling a future of more precise, safe, and user-friendly applications in the realm of OHNS and other medical fields. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 10-second Heart Age shows strong agreement with the 5-minute Heart Age. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 10-s Heart Age showed strong agreement with the 5-min Heart Age (R2 = 0.94, p < 0.001, mean ± SD bias 0.0 ± 5.1 years). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Heart Age Gap is 0.0±5.7 years in healthy individuals, 7.4±7.3 years in subjects with cardiovascular risk factors, and 14.3±9.2 years in patients with cardiovascular disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Heart Age Gap was 0.0 ± 5.7 years in healthy individuals, 7.4 ± 7.3 years in subjects with cardiovascular risk factors (p < 0.001), and 14.3 ± 9.2 years in patients with cardiovascular disease (p < 0.001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published version adds statistical significance annotations (p-values), clarifying the result without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heart Age can be accurately estimated from a 10-second 12-lead ECG in a transparent and explainable fashion based on known ECG measures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heart Age can be accurately estimated from a 10-s 12-lead ECG in a transparent and explainable fashion based on known ECG measures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Heart Age Gap increases markedly with cardiovascular risk and disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Heart Age Gap increases markedly with cardiovascular risk and disease. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion exactly, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The utilisation of multiple WSI instruments to produce digital replicas of the same slides may improve the robustness and generalisability of AI algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NPIC AI FORGE facility can generate approximately 4000 WSI images per day, amounting to approximately 7Tb of image data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NPIC AI FORGE can generate approximately 4,000 WSI images per day, amounting to approximately 7 TB of image data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI is often trained on data from different scanners but not usually by replicating the same slide across scanners, which presents a limitation. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The National Pathology Imaging Cooperative (NPIC) has built the AI FORGE, a unique multi-scanner facility to compare scanner performance and replicate digital pathology image datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: C3PO includes 520,868 individuals with a median follow-up of 7.2 years and a median of 13 primary care visits per individual. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding statement in the publication about cohort size, follow-up duration, or median visits; this preprint result does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of natural language processing (NLP) to recover vital signs from unstructured notes reduced missingness from 54.9% to 31% in the cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP reduced the missingness of vital signs by 31%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both the Pooled Cohort Equations (PCE) and CHARGE-AF models demonstrated good discrimination in the C3PO cohort. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Intentional sampling of individuals receiving regular ambulatory care and the use of NLP have the potential to reduce bias in EHR research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sampling patients receiving regular primary care and using NLP to recover missing data may reduce bias and maximize generalizability of EHR research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
