alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model automatically identifies crucial indicators contributing to malignant progression, including Troponin, Brain natriuretic peptide, White cell count, Aspartate aminotransferase, Creatinine, and Hypersensitive C-reactive protein. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model automatically identifies crucial indicators contributing to malignant progression, including Troponin, Brain natriuretic peptide, White cell count, Aspartate aminotransferase, Creatinine, and Hypersensitive C-reactive protein. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel deep-learning-based framework demonstrates excellent tolerance to the influence of occult cases of CTA-negative but DSA-positive aneurysms. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models were trained to classify biopsy and surgical histopathological whole slide images into DCIS, IDC, and benign categories. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved ROC areas under the curves (AUCs) of up to 0.960 for DCIS and 0.977 for IDC. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational pathology tools can assist pathologists in detecting and classifying DCIS and IDC from whole slide images. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Cox regression GWAS is underpowered when cases are strongly ascertained, regardless of the generative model used. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model achieved a micro F1 score of 0.771 in life-threatening classification, 0.592 in response delay, and 0.801 in jurisdiction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepEMC2 is able to capture information present in emergency medical calls not considered by the existing triage protocol. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model trained on controls and PWA achieved 46.8% accuracy at exactly matching the human-identified target. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained on controls and PWA achieved 50.7% accuracy at exactly matching the human-identified target. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuning on PWA data, with or without controls, led to comparable performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performed better on targets with less human ambiguity and on paraphasias from participants with fluent or less severe aphasia. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We were able to automatically identify the intended target of paraphasias in discourse using just the surrounding language about half of the time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Future work will incorporate phonological information from the paraphasia to further improve predictive utility. (certainty=low, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT showed a high correlation with TIMI and HEART scores (r = 0.898 and 0.928, respectively). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT-4 showed a high correlation with TIMI and HEART scores (r = 0.898 and 0.928, respectively). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT gave a different risk 45-48% of the time for a fixed TIMI or HEART score. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT-4 gave a different risk 45-48% of the time for a fixed TIMI or HEART score. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On the 44 variable model, a majority of the five ChatGPT models agreed on a diagnosis category only 56% of the time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On the 44-variable model, a majority of the five ChatGPT-4 models agreed on a diagnosis category only 56% of the time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that while large language models like ChatGPT hold promise for healthcare applications, further refinement and customization are necessary. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves a true positive rate of 0.80 and a false positive rate of 0.24. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagnostic accuracy of the prediction model is 78%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction model has the potential to be a supportive tool for clinicians during preoperative counseling after validation in a prospective cohort. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method could identify similar demarcation lines (DLs) to those identified by experienced doctors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The specific selection of the number of clusters enabled the proposed method to detect DLs that were similar to those identified by endoscopists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method could identify similar demarcation lines (DLs) to those identified by experienced doctors.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report that the method produced DLs similar to expert endoscopists; the publication expresses this result without the clustering-detail but the substantive result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DLs identified by the proposed method represented complex shapes similar to those identified by experienced doctors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method could identify similar demarcation lines (DLs) to those identified by experienced doctors.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same similarity-to-experts result but with lower reported certainty; thus the semantic content is preserved while confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system can generate pathologically valid DLs by increasing the number of clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system could generate pathologically valid DLs by increasing the number of clusters.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific result verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system can support the training of inexperienced doctors and enrich the knowledge of experienced doctors in endoscopy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed system can support the training of inexperienced doctors in endoscopy.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the broader preprint conclusion into separate claims; the training-support part is retained with higher certainty, so the matched portion is clarified and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best diagnostic model achieved an AUROC of 0.890, sensitivity of 0.771, specificity of 0.850, and positive predictive value of 0.834.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best diagnostic model achieved an AUROC of 0.890, sensitivity of 0.771, specificity of 0.850, and positive predictive value (PPV) of 0.834.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numerical diagnostic performance metrics exactly, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The severity determination model yielded an AUROC of 0.980, sensitivity of 0.958, specificity of 0.961, and positive predictive value of 0.958 in distinguishing GOLD 1 from GOLD 4.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The severity determination model yielded an AUROC of 0.980, sensitivity of 0.958, specificity of 0.961, and PPV of 0.958 in distinguishing GOLD 1 from GOLD 4.
alignments.rationale: Numerical results and interpretation are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Output probabilities from the severity determination model produced a correlation of 0.71 with percentage predicted FEV 1.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Output probabilities from the severity determination model produced a correlation of 0.71 with percentage predicted FEV1.
alignments.rationale: The correlation result is carried over exactly into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The N-Tidal ™ device could be used alongside interpretable machine learning as an accurate, point-of-care diagnostic test for COPD.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The N-TidalTM device could be used alongside interpretable machine learning as an accurate, point-of-care diagnostic test for COPD.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about N-Tidal as a potential point-of-care diagnostic is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: N-Tidal ™ could be effective in monitoring disease progression, providing a possible alternative to spirometry for disease monitoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: N-TidalTM could be effective in monitoring disease progression, providing a possible alternative to spirometry for disease monitoring.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the monitoring/alternative-to-spirometry conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study developed explainable machine learning models that accurately predict CKD trajectory using limited datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study developed explainable machine learning models that accurately predict CKD trajectory using limited datasets.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Internal validation achieved exceptional predictive accuracy with ROC-AUC values of 0.94 and 0.98 for decision tree and random forest models, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Internal validation achieved exceptional predictive accuracy with ROC-AUC values of 0.94 and 0.98 for decision tree and random forest models, respectively.
alignments.rationale: Internal validation metrics are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: External validation demonstrated ROC-AUC values of 0.88 for the decision tree and 0.93 for the random forest model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: External validation demonstrated ROC-AUC values of 0.88 for the decision tree and 0.93 for the random forest model.
alignments.rationale: External validation results are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most recent eGFR and eGFR slope were identified as the most informative variables for prediction in the Japanese cohort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most recent eGFR and eGFR slope were identified as the most informative variables for prediction in the Japanese cohort.
alignments.rationale: The variable-importance finding is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Females reported more adverse events (AEs) than males after receiving the Sputnik V vaccine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Women reported more adverse events than men, with a 1.2-fold increase.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the magnitude (1.2-fold) of the sex difference reported in the preprint, clarifying the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were more AEs reported from the first dose of Sputnik V than from the second dose.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or repeat a dose-by-dose AE comparison, so this specific preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of AEs decreased with age among Sputnik V vaccine recipients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of adverse events decreased with age, with a β of .05 per year.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the age effect but adds a numerical effect size, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AE profile of Sputnik V was comparable with other COVID-19 vaccines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adverse event profile of Sputnik V was comparable to other vector COVID-19 vaccines.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the comparison from all COVID-19 vaccines to vector vaccines specifically, revising the scope of the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AEs reported in Telegram posts were consistent with those reported in the Argentinian post-marketing AE registry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adverse events reported in Telegram posts were consistent with those in the Argentinian postmarketing adverse event registry.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparison between Telegram-reported events and the Argentinian registry.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The common machine-learning scheme of training a model to predict implantation and using the same model for embryo ranking is wrong.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strategy of combining implantation prediction and embryo ranking can lead to suboptimal selection of embryos.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the categorical "wrong" statement to a more measured claim that the combined strategy can lead to suboptimal outcomes, thus revising and tempering the original assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using clinical cohort-related information improves embryo implantation prediction but deteriorates ranking.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inclusion of clinical properties in the ranking process hampers the effectiveness of embryo ranking despite enhancing implantation prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same trade-off between prediction and ranking; wording is slightly less categorical (confidence tempered) but the core claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The label ambiguity of embryos that failed to implant deteriorates embryo ranking and the ability to accurately predict implantation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ambiguous labels of failed implantations confound both optimal ranking and implantation prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same point about label ambiguity disrupting both ranking and prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Considering discarded embryos enhances embryo ranking by reducing the ambiguity in their negative labels.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly report the specific claim about discarded embryos improving ranking, so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training models for embryo ranking should focus exclusively on embryo intrinsic features and include less ambiguous negative labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conceptual and practical steps are proposed to enhance machine learning-driven IVF solutions by separating the optimization of implantation from ranking.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents broader conceptual/practical steps (including separating objectives) that align with the preprint's recommendation to focus ranking on intrinsic features and clearer negatives, thus clarifying the recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT demonstrated a high level of performance in accuracy, achieving 66.43%, and in clinical reasoning with a median score of 4.5.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median clinical reasoning value for ChatGPT was 4.5, while for GPT-4 it was 4.67.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the clinical reasoning median for ChatGPT (4.5) which is a central element of the preprint claim; the publication separates accuracy into a different claim, so the preprint's combined phrasing is presented more clearly in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 showed better performance with an accuracy score of 93.71% and a median clinical reasoning value of 4.67.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 showed better performance than ChatGPT with an accuracy score of 93.71%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same accuracy and comparative conclusion; the paper emphasizes the accuracy comparison while clinical-reasoning medians are reported elsewhere, so the core result is preserved and clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first study to evaluate the accuracy and clinical reasoning of ChatGPT and GPT-4 when rheumatology questions from an official access exam to specialized medical training are used as prompts.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert novelty or state this is the first such study; the preprint's 'first study' contribution is not repeated in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that LLM may serve as valuable tools in rheumatology education, aiding in exam preparation and supplementing traditional teaching methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings suggest that LLM may serve as valuable tools in rheumatology education.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same educational suggestion but with lower reported certainty; wording is slightly condensed and the confidence level in the paper is reduced compared with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-dimensional Unet model achieves 91.20% and 88.80% dice similarity coefficient accuracy on the aorta and coronary arteries, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fully automatic two-dimensional Unet model achieves 91.20% and 88.80% dice similarity coefficient accuracy on segmenting the aorta and coronary arteries respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric performance metrics and model description with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performs better than a semi-automatic segmentation method when segmenting the coronary arteries alone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performs better than a semi-automatic segmentation method when segmenting the coronary arteries alone.
alignments.rationale: This result is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed method is comparable to existing published two-dimensional or three-dimensional deep learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed method is comparable to existing published two-dimensional or three-dimensional deep learning models.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same comparative performance conclusion with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithmic and graphical processing unit memory efficiencies of the model allow for deployment within hospital computer networks where graphical processing units are typically not available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithmic and graphical processing unit memory efficiencies of the model allow it to be deployed in hospital settings without requiring graphical processing units.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the deployability claim with clearer wording and higher certainty (publication lists this as higher certainty), strengthening the claim about deployment without GPUs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep-learning-based white matter lesion volume in non-contrast CT can substitute human ratings to prognosticate symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage and functional outcome at 90-days in acute ischemic stroke patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both DL-WML volume and WML-Faz are associated with symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhage (sICH) and 90-day functional outcome according to the modified Rankin Scale (mRS).
alignments.rationale: The publication reports association for DL-WML (and Fazekas) with outcomes but does not assert that DL-WML can substitute human ratings for prognostication, thus the original stronger substitution claim is weakened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This was the first study to show that white matter lesion volume in non-contrast CT based on deep-learning segmentations (DL-WML volume) was associated with symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhages and a worse functional outcome.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not claim novelty or that this was the first study; that specific contribution is not stated in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compared to the Fazekas scale, regression models using DL-WML volume had a similar fit to the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: White matter lesion volume on CT measured with deep learning had a similar association with symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhages and worse functional outcome as the Fazekas scale.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparison but with stronger certainty and clearer phrasing that the DL measure and Fazekas scale show similar associations with outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: White matter lesion load might be associated with more symptomatic intracerebral hemorrhages if IVT was given before EVT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Although more sICH might occur in patients with more severe WML-Faz receiving IVT, no worse functional outcome was observed.
alignments.rationale: The publication addresses the same interaction (more sICH with IVT in patients with greater WML) but adds the clarification that this did not translate into worse functional outcome, and presents the point with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The calibrated XGB model had a dynamic AUROC of 0.746 when predicting survival at 6-months.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient Boosting (GB) was the top performer with a Dynamic AUROC of 0.746 and a Concordance Index of 0.716 for six-month survival prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same dynamic AUROC and expands metrics (Concordance Index) and context (GB as top performer), clarifying and slightly expanding the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased age, male gender, reduced mobility, poor general health status, elevated pressure ulcer risk, and lack of appetite were identified as the strongest predictors of imminent mortality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable AI tools provided insights into key features such as comorbidities, cognitive impairment, and nutritional status, revealing their impact on survival outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights somewhat different feature groups (comorbidities, cognition, nutrition) via XAI; while there is overlap (nutrition/poor health), the emphasis on predictors differs, so the specific preprint list is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the effective application of machine learning in developing a survival model for people admitted to residential aged care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study successfully applied machine learning to create viable survival models for aged care residents, demonstrating their usability for clinical settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same overall conclusion about applying ML for survival modelling in aged care and presents it with increased emphasis on usability for clinical settings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has adequate predictive accuracy and confirms clinical intuition about specific mortality risk factors at both the cohort and the individual level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of XAI tools proved effective in enhancing the transparency and trustworthiness of predictions, offering actionable insights for end-of-life care decisions.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's points about confirming clinical intuition and individual-level insights in terms of XAI improving transparency and actionability, clarifying how those insights are produced and strengthening confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research sets a benchmark on how various AI technologies can be integrated with machine learning to offer effective solutions and greater transparency for clinical decision-making in aged care settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings support the introduction into clinical trials of machine learning with explainable AI tools in geriatric medicine for mortality prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the preprint's broader 'benchmark'/integration claim into a specific next step—supporting introduction into clinical trials—thus revising and narrowing the original contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NILS prediction model demonstrates the ability to predict healthy lymph nodes in clinically N0 breast cancer patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NILS artificial neural network model demonstrates the potential to identify patients with a low risk of sentinel lymph node metastasis.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys essentially the same result: the model can identify patients at low risk of sentinel node metastasis (i.e., healthy lymph nodes) in clinically N0 patients.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study validates the performance of the NILS prediction model using preoperatively collected clinicopathological and radiological data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study aims to validate the NILS prediction model for sentinel lymph node status using preoperatively available clinicopathological and radiological data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint states validation was performed, whereas the publication frames this as the study aim; the claim about completed validation is therefore softened in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If the accuracy and discrimination of the NILS model reach a satisfactory level, it can assist in decision-making regarding the omission of sentinel node biopsy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NILS prediction model may assist medical professionals and breast cancer patients in shared decision making on omitting sentinel lymph node biopsies for patients predicted to be node-negative.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same potential clinical application for decision-making on omitting sentinel node biopsy for patients predicted node-negative, matching the preprint's conditional conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study achieved an AUC of 0.77 for live birth prediction, which significantly outperforms related works in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study achieved an AUC of 0.77 for live birth prediction, which significantly outperforms related works in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are identical in content and metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sixteen out of 103 clinical features were identified to be predictors of live birth outcomes and helped improve live birth prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sixteen out of 103 clinical features were identified to be predictors of live birth outcomes and helped improve live birth prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about the 16 predictive clinical features is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of patient couple’s clinical features along with blastocyst images increases live birth prediction accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of patient couple's clinical features along with blastocyst images increases live birth prediction accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about combining clinical features with images is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CNN in the AI model mainly focuses on image regions of inner cell mass and trophectoderm (TE) for live birth prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN in the AI model mainly focuses on image regions of inner cell mass and trophectoderm (TE) for live birth prediction. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The description of CNN attention to ICM and TE regions is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors (ICIs) have substantially improved survival in patients with advanced malignancies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the general statement about ICIs improving survival; this claim appears omitted in the publication claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Machine Learning-based phenotyping approach can identify patients with Immune-Related Adverse Events (irAEs) from a large volume of retrospective clinical notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed natural language processing pipeline can automatically annotate clinical notes to determine evidence of irAEs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the result as an automated NLP pipeline for annotating evidence of irAEs, which clarifies the method while preserving the same capability described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the Machine Learning approach shows promising results with an average F1-score of 0.75 and AUC-ROC of 0.78. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model for any irAE achieved an average F1-score of 0.75 and an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.85. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved for F1 but the reported AUC differs (0.78 → 0.85) in the publication, representing a numerical revision and stronger reported discrimination.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: While the extraction of any available irAEs in charts achieves high accuracy, individual irAEs extraction has room for further improvement. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier of any irAEs achieved good accuracy, but individual irAE classification still has room for improvement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about individual irAE classification needing improvement is stated essentially the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derived ANN model for PE achieved an accuracy of 0.89 and 0.79 in the training and testing sets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The derived ANN model for PE achieved an accuracy of 0.89 and 0.79 in the training and testing sets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The ANN model performance metrics are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A GWAS identified a strong statistical association peak at the PLXNA4 locus, with lead SNP rs1424597 associated with an increased risk of PE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A GWAS on the LIME approximate identified a strong statistical association peak (rs1424597: p = 5.3 × 10–7) at the PLXNA4 locus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds statistical detail (p-value and phrasing) but reports the same association at rs1424597 at PLXNA4.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rs1424597-A allele was associated with increased PE risk in patients over 37 years of age but not in younger patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Homozygote carriers for the rs1424597-A allele were more frequently observed in PE than in DVT patients from the MARTHA and EOVT studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different/expanded finding (higher homozygote frequency in PE vs DVT cohorts) rather than the age-stratified association described in the preprint, indicating a revision of the specific association reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PLXNA4 is identified as a new susceptibility gene for PE whose exact role needs further elucidation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PLXNA4 has been identified as a new susceptibility gene for PE whose exact role now needs to be further elucidated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same conclusion that PLXNA4 is a newly identified susceptibility gene requiring further study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The XGBoost models were consistently more accurate than the null and historical models for all forecast time horizons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The XGBoost models were consistently more accurate than the null and historical models for all forecast time horizons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance comparison is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For 1-week ahead predictions across test sets, the mMAE of the XGBoost model with an extending training window was reduced by 78% on average compared to the null model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For 1-week ahead predictions across test sets, the mMAE of the XGBoost model with an extending training window was reduced by 78% on average compared to the null model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The 1-week mMAE reduction figure is reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGBoost models showed a 62% reduction in mMAE compared to the null model for 4-week ahead predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: XGBoost models showed a 62% reduction in mMAE compared to the null model for 4-week ahead predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The 4-week mMAE reduction is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results highlight the potential utility of machine learning methods in forecasting infectious disease incidence when that incidence is defined as an ordinal variable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results highlight the potential utility of machine learning methods in forecasting infectious disease incidence when that incidence is defined as an ordinal variable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about ML utility for ordinal incidence forecasting is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning framework for forecasting ILI has the potential to be adopted as a valuable public health tool globally in the future. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim list does not include the aspirational statement about global public-health adoption; instead it adds a different claim about extensibility, so the preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models for allocating readmission-mitigating interventions may be ranked differently based on overall utility and discriminative ability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that machine learning models may be ranked differently based on overall utility and discriminative ability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same high-level conclusion about differing model rankings by utility versus discrimination.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning model predicting a patient’s future cost of care achieved higher utility than a readmission risk prediction model, despite having lower discriminative performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Allocating interventions based on a model trained to predict cost as a proxy achieved a higher utility of $175.94 per patient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both texts report that a cost-predicting model yields higher utility than a readmission model; the publication provides a quantified utility value supporting the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models for allocation of limited health resources should consider directly optimizing for utility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Estimated utility varies by intervention cost and efficacy, with each model performing the best under different intervention settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the recommendation more concretely by emphasizing how utility depends on intervention parameters, which clarifies and nuances the preprint's prescriptive statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Allocating readmission-mitigating interventions based on a LightGBM model trained to predict readmissions achieved a maximum utility of $-12,645 per patient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Allocating readmission-mitigating interventions based on a GBDT model trained to predict readmissions achieved an estimated utility gain of $104 per patient. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a markedly different quantitative result (large negative loss → modest positive gain) for a similar tree-based model; this is a substantive revision of the numeric outcome and presents more favorable utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A hybrid model combining both intervention strategies achieved a maximum utility of $-12,472, comparable with the best models on either metric. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hybrid model combining both intervention strategies is comparable with the best models on either metric. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the comparability conclusion for the hybrid model but omits the negative utility numbers reported in the preprint, indicating a revision of the quantitative result while preserving the qualitative conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating ViT and CNN can mitigate the limitations of each architecture, offering comprehensive solutions that combine global context understanding with precise local feature extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating ViT and CNN can mitigate the limitations of each architecture, offering comprehensive solutions that combine global context understanding with precise local feature extraction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially verbatim and carries the same meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review benchmarks the articles based on architectural variations, merging strategies, innovative uses of ViT, and efficiency metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review identified architectural variations, merging strategies, and innovative applications of ViT in the context of hybrid ViT-CNN architectures for medical imaging tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core elements (architectural variations, merging strategies, innovative ViT uses) but omits explicit mention of efficiency metrics and reduces certainty, so the claim is clarified and somewhat narrowed/tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This review defines fundamental concepts of hybrid vision transformers and highlights emerging trends in the field. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study explores and evaluates hybrid architectures that integrate ViT and CNN to enhance performance in medical vision tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's conceptual/ trend discussion as an exploration/evaluation focused on performance; the content overlaps but is presented more assertively, increasing expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a clear direction for future research aimed at optimizing the integration of ViT and CNN for effective utilization in medical imaging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The systematic review provides a clear direction for future research aimed at optimizing the integration of ViT and CNN for effective utilization in medical imaging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim closely in content and expressed certainty about future research directions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning model using routine clinical EHR data can accurately predict involuntary admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning model using routine clinical EHR data can accurately predict involuntary admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The XGBoost model performed best in the training phase and obtained an area under the receiver operating curve of 0.84 in the test phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best XGBoost and elastic net model obtained an AUROC of 0.84 and 0.83, respectively, in the test phase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the XGBoost result but adds elastic net results, broadening the reported models while keeping the same AUROC for XGBoost.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If implemented as a clinical decision support tool, this model may guide interventions aimed at reducing the risk of involuntary admission. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If implemented as a clinical decision support tool, this model may guide interventions aimed at reducing the risk of involuntary admission. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is reproduced verbatim with the same expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep-learning algorithm accurately diagnoses early mild cognitive impairment (EMCI), late mild cognitive impairment (LMCI), Alzheimer Disease (AD), and cognitive normal (CN) patients using a few commonly available neurocognitive tests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning algorithm accurately classifies early mild cognitive impairment (EMCI), late mild cognitive impairment (LMCI), Alzheimer's disease (AD), and cognitive normal (CN) patients using a few common neurocognitive tests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the diagnostic/classification claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best classification model achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.984 on the test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best classification model achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.984 on the test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numeric result and claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simplified risk-stratification score derived from the deep-learning algorithm yielded an AUC of 0.963 on the test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simplified risk-stratification score model yielded an AUC of 0.963 on the test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the simplified score AUC result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The project successfully created an accurate, clinically translatable risk-stratified scoring aid for primary care providers to diagnose Alzheimer Disease in a fast and inexpensive manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this translational/implementation claim; instead it adds a different conclusion about feature selection methods, so the original implementation-focused claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Perceiver model significantly outperforms the baselines and reduces the computational complexity compared to the transformer model, without significant loss of performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Perceiver model outperforms or performs at par with baseline models while reducing computations by about nine times compared to the transformer model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific quantification (~ninefold reduction) and slightly nuances performance versus baselines, thus clarifying and quantifying the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Continuous patient state attention models utilizing neural ordinary differential equations effectively learn patient health dynamics from observed irregular time-steps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous patient state models outperform traditional and advanced techniques in handling irregular time series in healthcare. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same core outcome (continuous patient state models handle irregular time series better) but frames it as outperforming other techniques rather than focusing on the neural ODE mechanism, thereby clarifying/generalizing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The continuous patient state models outperform the baselines in the context of irregularity in healthcare. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous patient state models outperform traditional and advanced techniques in handling irregular time series in healthcare. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint claim maps directly to the publication statement about outperforming other techniques on irregular time series.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer learning using 7T cine data and ImageNet weight initialization significantly improved model performance to DICE LV =0.900 and DICE MY =0.791. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning using 7T cine data and ImageNet weight initialization improved model performance to DICE<sub>LV</sub> = 0.900 and DICE<sub>MY</sub> = 0.791. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the numeric performance results and same claim about transfer learning improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using only end-systolic and end-diastolic images reduced training data by 90%, with no negative impact on segmentation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using only end-systolic and end-diastolic images reduced training data by 90%, with no negative impact on segmentation performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical methodological/result claim preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work demonstrates the benefits of transfer learning for cardiac cine image segmentation on a quantitative basis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates and quantifies the benefits of transfer learning for cardiac cine image segmentation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes quantification of benefits (adds nuance), strengthening the original conclusion by making the quantitative aspect explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides guidelines, tools, and annotated data to enable transfer learning approaches for other researchers and clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide practical guidelines for researchers planning transfer learning projects in cardiac MRI. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the guidelines element but omits explicit mention of tools and annotated data, narrowing the original contribution claim to practical guidance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model accurately classified CIN status in breast cancer patients, achieving an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.822 with 81.2% sensitivity and 68.7% specificity in the test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based model accurately classified CIN status in breast cancer patients, achieving an area under the curve of 0.822. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the identical AUC value; although the preprint combined AUC and sensitivity/specificity, the AUC statement is unchanged in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patch-level predictions of CIN status suggested intra-tumor spatial heterogeneity within slides. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patch-level predictions of CIN status indicated intra-tumor heterogeneity within slides. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and paper state the same finding about patch-level heterogeneity with equivalent certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of patches with high predicted CIN score within an entire slide was more predictive of clinical outcome than the average CIN score of the slide. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Presence of patches with high predicted CIN score within an entire slide is more predictive of clinical outcome than the average CIN score of the slide. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same, but the publication reports a lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning methods demonstrated the ability to predict CIN status based on histopathology slide images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrated 81.2% sensitivity and 68.7% specificity in predicting CIN status in the test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies numeric performance metrics (sensitivity/specificity) that substantiate the preprint's general claim, clarifying the evidence for predictive ability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is not breast cancer subtype specific and can potentially be extended to other cancer types. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state or support the claim about subtype-independence or extension to other cancer types, so this preprint claim is not present in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-detected total nodule number (TNN) is an independent prognostic factor for both recurrence-free survival (RFS) and overall survival (OS) in patients with stage III non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-detected total nodule number (TNN) is an independent prognostic factor for both recurrence-free survival (RFS) and overall survival (OS) in patients with stage III non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same independent prognostic role for TNN in the stage III cohort with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lower TNN detected on preoperative CT scan indicates better prognosis in patients who underwent complete surgical resection for stage III NSCLC. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A lower TNN detected on preoperative CT scans indicates a better prognosis for patients who have undergone complete surgical resection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about lower TNN indicating better prognosis after complete resection is expressed equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The numbers of upper-lobe nodule, same-side nodule, other-side nodule, solid nodule, and solid nodule at small size (≤ 6mm) are independent prognostic factors for survival in NSCLC patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these specific per-nodule-type independent prognostic findings, so this preprint claim is not present in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survival tree analysis using AI-detected TNN provides superior discrimination of survival in the stage III cohort compared to traditional IIIA and IIIB classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survival tree analysis using AI-detected TNN provides a more effective determination of survival rates in the stage III cohort compared to traditional classifications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both describe the survival tree analysis as providing improved/more effective survival discrimination vs traditional stage groupings with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variance in image-derived DL scores were associated with tumor motion (R² = 0.45). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The variance and amplitude in 4D CT image-derived DL scores were associated with tumor motion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same association for variance and additionally mentions amplitude, broadening the reported signal while keeping the association intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL score variance was deterministic with undulations in phase with the respiratory cycle (R² = 0.77-0.99). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL scores, but not tumor volumes, peaked near end-exhalation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint described deterministic undulations phased to respiration; the publication reports a related but different observation (peak near end-exhalation), representing a revision in the detailed temporal characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIP-derived DL scores were significantly higher than 4D- or FB-derived risk scores (p < 0.0001). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MIP-derived DL scores were significantly higher than 4D- or FB-derived risk scores. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific statistical result comparing MIP to 4D/FB scores is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models of tumors in motion can be robust to fluctuations in object appearance due to movement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL models of tumors in motion can be robust to fluctuations in object appearance due to movement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about model robustness to motion-induced appearance changes is preserved unchanged in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results can guide standardization processes in the clinical translation of DL models for patients with lung cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly make the claim about guiding standardization processes for clinical translation, so this preprint recommendation is not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based framework enables direct reconstruction of attenuation and scatter corrected PET from non-attenuation-corrected images without structural information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based framework enables direct reconstruction of attenuation and scatter-corrected PET from non-attenuation-corrected images without structural information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core contribution verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel model performs subject- and region-specific filtering by modulating convolution kernels based on contextual coherency within neighboring slices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel model performs subject- and region-specific filtering by modulating convolution kernels based on contextual coherency within neighboring slices. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The description of the method is preserved but the publication reports a lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Qualitative assessments indicate that the proposed CT-free method produces corrected PET images that accurately resemble ground truth images corrected with CT scans. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Qualitative assessments indicate that the proposed CT-free method produces corrected PET images that accurately resemble ground truth images corrected with CT scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The qualitative result is the same, but the publication presents it with higher stated certainty (high vs medium), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantitative assessments show that the proposed method achieves an absolute relative error of 14.30 ± 3.88% and a relative error of −2.11% ± 2.73% in whole-body evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative assessments over 112 held-out subjects showed an absolute relative error of 14.30±3.88% and a relative error of -2.11%±2.73% in whole-body evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical error metrics are identical, and the publication clarifies the evaluation context (112 held-out subjects), broadening the result's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic susceptibility contrast magnetic resonance perfusion (DSC-MRP) is a non-invasive imaging technique that can derive various perfusion parameters for clinical diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic susceptibility contrast magnetic resonance perfusion (DSC-MRP) is a non-invasive imaging technique for hemodynamic measurements. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both texts convey the same conclusion that DSC-MRP is a non-invasive technique for perfusion/hemodynamic measurements; wording differs slightly but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conventional methods for deriving parametric maps from DSC-MRP are time-consuming and sensitive to user input, leading to inconsistent results. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional methods for computing perfusion parametric maps are often time-consuming and sensitive to user input. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is present in both; the publication states it with lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multi-step deep learning model was trained using 4D MRP datasets to predict voxel-wise perfusion parameters effectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model provides a robust and efficient approach for calculating perfusion parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a higher-level conclusion about robustness and efficiency that corresponds to the preprint's result describing the trained model; the claim is clarified to emphasize performance and utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-generated relative cerebral blood volume (rCBV) and relative cerebral blood flow (rCBF) maps show comparable results to those generated by FDA-approved commercial software. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the deep learning model is comparable to FDA-approved commercial software. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific comparison of rCBV/rCBF maps to FDA-approved software is reflected directly in the publication's statement about comparable performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained reinforcement learning algorithm identifies optimal combinations of HIV-testing and retention-in-care rates that lead towards HIV elimination. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results from the model demonstrated convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports convergence of the model results rather than explicitly claiming identification of optimal combinations that lead to elimination; this is a narrower, less ambitious formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Solving for testing and retention-in-care rates through appropriate reformulation using proxy decision-metrics overcomes the computational challenges of reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim addresses overcoming computational challenges via reformulation or proxy metrics; this methodological/result claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model suggests scaling up retention-in-care programs while starting with a high testing frequency that relaxes over a 10-year period as incidence decreases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model suggests scaling up retention-in-care programs to achieve and maintain high annual retention rates while initiating with a high testing frequency. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core recommendation to scale up retention and start with high testing but omits the explicit 10-year relaxation temporal detail, making the claim clarified and slightly narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Testing and retention-in-care alone did not achieve the 2030 Ending the HIV Epidemic targets, indicating the need for additional interventions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Testing and retention-in-care alone did not achieve the 2030 EHE targets, suggesting the need for additional interventions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion that testing and retention alone are insufficient and additional interventions are needed with essentially identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning is suitable for evaluating phased public health decisions for infectious disease control. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement learning (RL) is suitable for evaluating phased public health decisions for infectious disease control. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements identically endorse RL as suitable for evaluating phased public-health decisions; wording and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed model outperforms generic networks and DeepMedic, particularly in small lesions, with lower false positive rate, balanced precision and sensitivity, and robustness to data perturbs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed model outperforms generic networks and DeepMedic, particularly in small lesions, with lower false positive rate, balanced precision and sensitivity, and robustness to data perturbs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claims with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agreement with human delineation rivals the inter-evaluator agreement. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agreement with human delineation rivals the inter-evaluator agreement. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about agreement with human delineation is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated lesion quantification of volume and contrast has virtually total agreement with human quantification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated lesion quantification of volume and contrast has virtually total agreement with human quantification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the strong agreement claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our tool is fast, public, accessible to non-experts, with minimal computational requirements, to detect and segment lesions via a single command line. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our tool is fast, public, accessible to non-experts, with minimal computational requirements, to detect and segment lesions via a single command line. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the accessibility and usability claims unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our tool fulfills the conditions to perform large scale, reliable and reproducible clinical and translational research. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Therefore, it fulfills the conditions to perform large scale, reliable and reproducible clinical and translational research. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The final conclusion that the tool meets conditions for large-scale, reliable research is retained with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed TIME model can predict ICI therapy response based on numerical experiments with real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed TIME model can predict ICI therapy response using real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the model's predictive capability using real-world data with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DRL-derived personalized ICC therapy outperforms predefined fixed schedules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DRL-derived personalized ICC therapy outperforms predefined fixed schedules. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the superior performance of DRL-derived personalized therapy unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For tumors with extremely low CD8+T cell infiltration, DRL recommends high-dosage chemotherapy alone. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For tumors with extremely low CD8 + T cell infiltration, the DRL agent recommends high-dosage chemotherapy alone. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same recommendation for very low CD8+ infiltration cases; the publication frames it as a conclusion but content is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ODE-based TIME model offers a theoretical framework for determining the best use of ICI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An appropriate chemotherapy dosage induces CD8 + T cell proliferation, enhancing ICI therapy outcomes for tumors with higher CD8 + T cell infiltration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claim about offering a theoretical framework is transformed into a specific mechanistic/conclusion in the publication; this is a substantive change toward a narrower, result-focused statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study highlights the potential of utilizing real-world clinical data and DRL to develop personalized optimal ICC. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the general concluding claim about the potential of combining real-world clinical data and DRL for personalized ICC; instead, it focuses on specific results and limitations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CovBaseAI achieved an accuracy of 87% and a negative predictive value of 98% in diagnosing Cov-Pneum using CxR data from an Indian quarantine-center. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CovBaseAI achieved an accuracy of 87% and a negative predictive value of 98% in diagnosing COVID-Pneumonia using X-Ray data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance metrics and diagnostic claim are preserved in the publication with equivalent scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity of CovBaseAI varied from 0.66 to 0.90 depending on whether RT-PCR or radiologist opinion was used as the ground truth. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensitivity of CovBaseAI ranged from 0.66 to 0.90 when compared to RT-PCR and radiologist opinion as ground truth. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The sensitivity range and dependence on ground truth source are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CovBaseAI outperformed publicly available algorithms that were trained on COVID-19 data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides new insights on the usage of expert decision systems with deep learning methods for COVID-Pneumonia diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's strong claim of outperforming other algorithms is softened in the publication to emphasizing insights on combining expert systems and deep learning, indicating a narrower and less assertive framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need for improved CxR screening tools in low and middle income countries due to delays in RT-PCR testing. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmarking based on RT-PCR may not serve as a reliable ground truth in radiological diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation as a concern about RT-PCR as a ground truth for radiological benchmarking, which clarifies the underlying issue related to screening and evaluation in resource-limited settings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 scored 54/60 (90.0%) in the Clinical Chemistry exam, outperforming human students who had a mean score of 40/60 (66.8%). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 scored 0.90 ± 0.30, outperforming human students who had a mean score of 0.68±0.23. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same performance result (numerically equivalent, with added variability measures) and the same comparative conclusion versus humans.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Statistically significant better performance was noted in lower cognitive domains (Remember and Understand) in GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and Bard. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistically significant better performance was noted in lower cognitive domains (Remember and Understand) for GPT-3.5, GPT-4, and Bard compared to higher cognitive domains (Apply and Analyze). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the comparison (lower vs higher cognitive domains) and reports the result with higher certainty; content is consistent but more explicit and confident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CLEAR scores indicated that ChatGPT-4 performance was 'Excellent' compared to 'Above average' performance of ChatGPT-3.5, Bing, and Bard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CLEAR scores indicated that ChatGPT-4 performance was 'Excellent' compared to the 'Above average' performance of ChatGPT-3.5, Bing, and Bard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The CLEAR score-based ranking is repeated verbatim in the publication with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of these AI chatbots was remarkable given that the MCQs were directed to postgraduate students with a high degree of specialization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of these AI chatbots was remarkable given that the MCQs were directed to postgraduate students with a high degree of specialization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contextual conclusion about the remarkable performance given the target audience.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The appropriateness of MCQs as an assessment tool in higher education should be re-evaluated due to the risks of academic dishonesty and possible dependence on these AI models. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The appropriateness of MCQs as an assessment tool in higher education should be re-evaluated due to the risk of academic dishonesty and possible dependence on these AI models. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same limitation but reports it with higher certainty; content and scope remain the same while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm for DVT diagnosis achieves a sensitivity of 93.8% and a specificity of 84.2% compared to the clinical gold standard.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm demonstrates a sensitivity of 0.82 to 0.94 and specificity of 0.70 to 0.82 for DVT diagnosis compared to the clinical gold standard.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a range rather than single-point estimates, expanding uncertainty around the measures and slightly lowering reported specificity bounds, so the claim is clarified with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The positive predictive value of the algorithm is 90.9% and the negative predictive value is 88.9%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm provides a positive predictive value of 0.65 to 0.89 and a negative predictive value of 0.99 to 1.00.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports substantially different PPV/NPV ranges (lower PPV, much higher NPV) than the preprint point estimates, representing a revision of the performance metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating the deep learning approach into the DVT diagnostic pathway is estimated to be cost effective at up to $150 per software examination.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating the deep learning approach into diagnostic pathways for DVT is estimated to generate a positive net monetary benefit at costs up to �a372 to �a3175 per examination.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more detailed economic range and frames results as positive net monetary benefit (with costs in GBP), clarifying and specifying the preprint's cost-effectiveness claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method aids non-specialists in detecting DVT and provides guidance for free-hand ultrasound.
alignments.rationale: This contribution about aiding non-specialists and guidance for free-hand ultrasound appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human analysis took much longer (147.5 hours) than MATA (40 hours).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human analysis took much longer than MATA, with times of 147.5 hours versus 40 hours.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact timing comparison reported in the preprint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of MATA was as high as the human-only thematic analysis, with substantial time savings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Formal triangulation showed high similarity between the findings of MATA and human-only thematic analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication supports the preprint's quality claim using formal triangulation language, clarifying the evidence for equivalence in findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All codes developed from the MATA were classified as in agreement or complementary to the human themes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both MATA and human-only thematic analysis identified key themes about what users found helpful and unhelpful.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same agreement between methods in identifying themes, restating the preprint's result in more general terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MATA is a useful tool that can support qualitative researchers to interpret and analyse large datasets quickly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MATA is a useful tool for qualitative researchers to interpret and analyze large datasets quickly.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's conclusion about MATA's usefulness with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining NLP with human analysis to analyse free-text data can be a trustworthy and efficient method to use on large quantities of qualitative data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MATA can support intervention development and implementation, enabling rapid optimization during public health emergencies.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the broad claim about trustworthiness/efficiency into a more specific applied role (supporting intervention development and rapid optimization), narrowing the scope of the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified XGBoost model, called the Kidney Disease Classifier (KDClassifier), showed the best performance compared to random forest, support vector machine, and artificial neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified XGBoost model, called Kidney Disease Classifier (KDClassifier), showed the best performance compared to random forest, support vector machine, and artificial neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's comparative performance claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the diagnostic XGBoost model was 96.03%, with a confidence interval of 95.17%-96.77%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the XGBoost diagnostic model was 96.03%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same point estimate for accuracy; confidence interval detail in the preprint is preserved in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the curve of the XGBoost model was 0.952, indicating strong diagnostic performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the curve of the extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) model was 0.952.
alignments.rationale: AUC is reported identically in the publication, maintaining the same diagnostic performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents the first XGBoost diagnosis model, KDClassifier, for distinguishing different kidney diseases using complete mass spectrum information from urinary proteomics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KDClassifier provides a potential tool for the classification of kidney diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the strong novelty claim ("first" model) into a more cautious statement that KDClassifier is a potential classification tool, narrowing and tempering the original contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Kolmogorov-Smirnov (KS) value of the Lorenz curve was 0.8514.
alignments.rationale: The KS/Lorenz curve metric appears as a new reported result in the publication that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grad-CAM generally localized pathologies better than the other evaluated saliency methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grad-CAM generally localized pathologies better than the other evaluated saliency methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's primary result about Grad-CAM without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All seven saliency methods performed significantly worse compared with the human benchmark.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All seven saliency methods performed significantly worse compared with the human benchmark.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the preprint's claim that all methods underperform relative to humans.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gap in localization performance between Grad-CAM and the human benchmark was largest for pathologies that were smaller in size and had shapes that were more complex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gap in localization performance between Grad-CAM and the human benchmark was largest for pathologies that were smaller in size and had shapes that were more complex.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific result about pathology size/shape and performance gap.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model confidence was positively correlated with Grad-CAM localization performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model confidence was positively correlated with Grad-CAM localization performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same correlation between model confidence and localization performance.
