alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several important limitations of saliency methods must be addressed before we can rely on them for deep learning explainability in medical imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several important limitations of saliency methods must be addressed before we can rely on them for deep learning explainability in medical imaging.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's limitation statement verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep neural network trained on a hyper-scale dataset of 4.5 million images can achieve significant modality classification accuracy of 96%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep neural network trained on a hyper-scale dataset of 4.5 million images can achieve significant modality classification accuracy of 96%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's modality classification accuracy claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best performing model achieved a balanced accuracy of 86% on unseen data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved a balanced accuracy of 86%.
alignments.rationale: Balanced accuracy on unseen data is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to train Deep Learning Convolutional Neural Networks with hyper-scale multimodal datasets composed of millions of images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to train Deep Learning Convolutional Neural Networks with hyper-scale multimodal datasets composed of millions of images.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about feasibility of training CNNs on hyper-scale datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Such models can be applied in real-world applications involving large volumes of image data, such as medical imaging repositories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Such models can be applied in real-world applications with volumes of image data in the hyper-scale range, such as medical imaging repositories.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's applied contribution with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The best performing model achieved classification accuracy of 96% on unseen data, which is on-par or exceeds the accuracy of more complex implementations using EfficientNets or Vision Transformers.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a comparative claim about performance relative to EfficientNets/ViTs that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed lightweight Artificial Neural Network (ANN) model outperforms prior models in producing balanced outcomes from markedly unbalanced large survey data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach demonstrates robustness and generalizability by outperforming other models across four heavily imbalanced datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's outperforming claim but with broader scope (multiple imbalanced datasets) and stronger emphasis on robustness/generalizability, effectively clarifying and amplifying the initial result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ANN model demonstrates 80% specificity and 77% sensitivity in identifying the probability of myocardial infarction (MI). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DP-ANN model has achieved balanced performance, with an average specificity of 80%, sensitivity of 82%, and AUC-ROC of 89.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same performance metrics but with a different sensitivity (77% -> 82%) and adds an AUC, so the numeric result is revised and reported more positively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretability of the model, as measured by Shapley values, reveals substantial correlations between myocardial infarction (MI) and its risk factors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) analysis has revealed key predictors and risk factors for MI, such as coronary heart disease and bronchitis in females, and stroke among individuals aged 35-54. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the SHAP-based interpretability claim but specifies particular predictors and subgroups, narrowing and clarifying the original interpretability conclusion without changing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (AUC) of the proposed model is 0.87. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DP-ANN model has achieved balanced performance, with an average specificity of 80%, sensitivity of 82%, and AUC-ROC of 89.5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports an AUC that differs from the preprint (0.87 -> 89.5%), so the numeric result is revised and presented more favorably.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study provides a robust model for healthcare professionals to assess MI risk through targeted factors, promoting early detection and potentially improving patient outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion (practical guidance for healthcare professionals) does not appear explicitly in the preprint and therefore is an added high-level application-oriented claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning framework can accurately forecast COVID-19 spread in 203 US counties within a time-window of 15 days. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning framework can accurately forecast COVID-19 spread in 203 US counties within a time-window of 15 days. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sensitivity analysis indicates that attendance of close places has the largest impact on the basic reproduction number of COVID-19. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sensitivity analysis indicates that attendance of close places has the largest impact on the effective reproduction number. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses 'effective reproduction number' rather than 'basic reproduction number', a wording clarification while preserving the underlying sensitivity result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework enables rapid quantification of the effects of government interventions on the spread of COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework enables rapid probing and quantification of the effects of government interventions on pandemic spread. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion but slightly broadens wording to 'probing and quantification,' a minor clarification without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model employs an ensemble of deep neural networks to infer the time-dependent reproduction number of an infectious disease. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model employs an ensemble of deep neural networks to infer the time-dependent reproduction number of an infectious disease. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average Dice Similarity Coefficient testing was 0.90 for non-sclerotic glomeruli and 0.83 for sclerotic glomeruli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average Dice similarity coefficient testing was 0.90 for nonsclerotic and 0.83 for sclerotic glomeruli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the Dice scores verbatim, indicating no substantive change to the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The F1, Recall, and Precision scores for non-sclerotic glomeruli were 0.93, 0.96, and 0.90, respectively, and for sclerotic glomeruli were 0.87, 0.93, and 0.81, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: These specific classification metrics (F1/Recall/Precision) are present in the preprint but are not reported in the publication, indicating removal of these detailed results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL-derived and manual segmentation derived glomerular counts were comparable, but statistically different from reported glomerular count. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DL-derived and manual segmentation-derived glomerular counts were comparable, but statistically different from reported glomerular count. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparison between DL-derived and manual counts; wording and certainty are essentially the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL segmentation is a feasible and robust approach for automatic quantification of glomeruli. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning segmentation is a feasible and robust approach for automatic quantification of glomeruli. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The feasibility and robustness conclusion is carried forward verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work represents the first step toward new protocols for the evaluation of donor kidney biopsies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study represents the first step toward new protocols for the evaluation of donor kidney biopsies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim about being a first step toward new protocols with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ANMerge includes multimodal data from 1702 study participants and is accessible to the research community via a centralized portal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ANMerge includes multimodal data from 1,702 study participants and is accessible to the research community via a centralized portal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The participant count and accessibility claim are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ANMerge is an information rich patient-level data resource that can serve as a discovery and validation cohort for data-driven AD research. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ANMerge is an information rich patient-level data resource that can serve as a discovery and validation cohort for data-driven AD research. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The resource-role and intended use are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The originally shared data was incomplete, erroneous and lacking in interoperability. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The originally shared resources were incomplete, erroneous, and lacking in interoperability. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the limitation claim but with slightly softened certainty ('resources' and lower reported certainty), so the limitation is clarified and the confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study systematically addressed several limitations of the originally shared data and provides additional unreleased data to enhance the patient-level dataset. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that it systematically addressed those limitations or provided unreleased data, so this specific contribution appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN, SVM, KNN, and LR models based on the 73-feature set demonstrated the best discriminative power with AUC values of 0.92, 0.82, 0.63, and 0.85, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network model achieved high discriminative power for early GDM prediction, with an area under the curve (AUC) value of 0.80. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower DNN AUC and omits the 73-feature multi-model breakdown, indicating a revision of the numeric result and narrowing to the DNN-specific report with tempered performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 7-feature DNN, SVM, KNN, and LR models had the best discriminative power with AUCs of 0.84, 0.69, 0.68, and 0.84, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 7-variable logistic regression model also achieved effective discriminative power with an AUC of 0.77. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication only reports a 7-variable logistic regression result (AUC 0.77) rather than the multi-model 7-feature comparisons in the preprint, so the claim is revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our feature selection and machine learning models showed superior predictive power in early GDM detection than previous methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make an explicit superiority claim over previous methods, so this comparative conclusion appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existing prediction models did not exceed an AUC of 0.75. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this specific comparative historical benchmark, indicating the preprint's claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNN prediction models have effective discrimination power for predicting GDM in early pregnancy, but they cannot completely replace LR. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep neural network model achieved high discriminative power for early GDM prediction, with an area under the curve (AUC) value of 0.80. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms DNN effectiveness but omits the preprint's caution about not replacing LR; numeric performance is revised and overall nuance is changed, so the claim is revised with tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Low body mass index (BMI) (≤ 17) was related to an increased risk of GDM compared to a BMI in the range of 17 to 18. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific risk-factor result (low BMI relation to GDM risk) does not appear in the preprint and is thus an added publication claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Total 3,3,5'-triiodothyronine (T3) and total thyroxin (T4) were superior to free T3 and free T4 in predicting GDM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports specific biomarker findings (total T3/T4 superiority) not present in the preprint, indicating an added result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Lipoprotein(a) demonstrated a promising predictive value for early GDM with an AUC of 0.66. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The lipoprotein(a) predictive result is newly reported in the publication and does not correspond to any preprint claim, so it is treated as added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning assistant achieved a WSI-level area-under-the-receiver-operating-characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.965. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning assistant achieved a WSI-level area under the receiver-operating-characteristic curve (AUROC) of 0.965. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric AUROC and wording are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Assisted diagnoses of H. pylori-positive cases were much more accurate than unassisted diagnoses, with an odds ratio of 13.37. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Assisted diagnoses of H. pylori were faster and much more accurate than unassisted diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert substantially improved accuracy with assistance; the publication adds a speed claim and omits the specific odds-ratio detail, clarifying but not reversing the finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Assistance increased diagnostic uncertainty on H. pylori-negative cases, resulting in an overall decrease in assisted accuracy with an odds ratio of 0.435. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Assistance increased diagnostic uncertainty on H. pylori-negative cases, resulting in an overall decrease in assisted accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding, dropping the numeric odds ratio but keeping the substantive result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of deep learning into clinical workflows requires optimization to mitigate diagnostic uncertainty. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of deep learning into clinical workflows requires optimization to mitigate diagnostic uncertainty. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion language is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An artificial intelligence solution for automatic staging and prognosis based on imaging, clinical, comorbidities, and biological data has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the use of medical imaging and artificial intelligence for disease quantification, staging, and outcome prediction in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claim an AI solution for staging/prognosis, but the publication emphasizes medical imaging (CT) while the preprint claimed broader multi-modal data; this narrows the scope of the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach relies on automatic CT-based disease quantification using deep learning and robust data-driven identification of COVID-19 patient profiling. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach relies on automatic deep learning-based disease quantification using an ensemble of architectures and a data-driven consensus. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the methodological description (ensemble/consensus) consistent with the preprint's CT-based quantification, but the stated certainty is reduced from high to medium, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed solution demonstrates strong, reproducible staging and outcome prediction with good generalization properties using an ensemble of consensus methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Highly promising results on multiple external/independent evaluation cohorts indicate the potential of the proposed approach. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents similar external-evaluation results but softens the strength of the claim (from 'strong, reproducible' to 'highly promising') and lowers certainty, so the claim is revised and confidence tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Highly promising results on multiple independent external evaluation cohorts indicate the potential of the AI approach compared to expert human readers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Highly promising results on multiple external/independent evaluation cohorts indicate the potential of the proposed approach. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 'highly promising' external-evaluation result but removes the explicit comparison to expert human readers, narrowing the claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution offers perspectives for optimal patient management in the context of intensive care bed and ventilator shortages. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding statement about operational patient-management or resource allocation appears in the publication; the management-perspectives claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Miniset-DenseSENet distinguishes ALS patients from controls with 97.37% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Miniset-DenseSENet distinguishes controls, ALS patients with no cognitive impairment, and ALS patients with cognitive impairment with 97.37% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the classification into three specific groups (adding cognitive-impairment stratification) while preserving the reported overall accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Miniset-DenseSENet detects cognitive impairments, a critical but underdiagnosed feature of ALS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Miniset-DenseSENet distinguishes controls, ALS patients with no cognitive impairment, and ALS patients with cognitive impairment with 97.37% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert the model's ability to detect/stratify cognitive impairment; the publication's more detailed three-way distinction is consistent with this result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Miniset-DenseSENet outperformed other transfer learning models, achieving a sensitivity of 1 and specificity of 0.95. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Miniset-DenseSENet outperformed other transfer learning models, achieving a sensitivity of 1 and specificity of 0.95. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics and comparative claim are retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating transfer learning and attention mechanisms into neuroimaging can enhance diagnostic accuracy, enabling earlier ALS detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating transfer learning and attention mechanisms into neuroimaging can enhance diagnostic accuracy, enabling earlier ALS detection and improving patient stratification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the conclusion to explicitly include improved patient stratification in addition to earlier detection, broadening the implication of the method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model has the potential to guide clinical decisions and support personalized therapeutic strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model has the potential to guide clinical decisions and support personalized therapeutic strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The translational potential language is preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning algorithm achieved a mean ROC curve AUC of 0.97 for antibody-mediated rejection in three validation datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extreme gradient boosting classifiers achieved a mean ROC curve AUC of 0.97 for antibody-mediated rejection in external validation datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported AUC and target (antibody-mediated rejection) are the same; publication specifies the classifier type but preserves the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm demonstrated a mean ROC curve AUC of 0.94 for T cell-mediated rejection across the validation datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding T cell-mediated rejection AUC result appears in the publication; this specific result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An additional algorithm developed using the largest validation cohort achieved an accuracy of 0.95 in discriminating active and chronic active antibody-mediated rejection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifiers demonstrated 95% accuracy in discriminating between active and chronic active antibody-mediated rejection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The 0.95 accuracy for discrimination between active and chronic active antibody-mediated rejection is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Artificial Intelligence algorithm is designed to interpret histological lesions alongside routine clinical data with very high sensitivity and specificity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed artificial intelligence classifiers showed excellent concordance with the Banff rules and expert reference diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's conclusion about interpreting lesions with high sensitivity/specificity is presented in the publication as excellent concordance with established rules and expert diagnoses, clarifying the nature of the algorithm's performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm is expected to enhance biopsy interpretation homogeneity among pathologists and clinicians. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the specific claim about enhancing homogeneity of biopsy interpretation; instead it notes discrepancies suggesting classification improvements, so the homogeneity claim was not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Learning (DL) produced an AUC of 0.85 for categorizing Alzheimer's Disease (AD) from cognitively normal individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Learning (DL) produced an AUC of 0.85 for categorizing Alzheimer's Disease (AD) from cognitively normal (CN) individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The AUC and comparison groups are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGBoost produced an AUC of 0.88, outperforming both DL and Random Forest in differentiating AD from cognitively normal individuals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: XGBoost produced an AUC of 0.88, which is higher than the AUC values produced by CSF measures of amyloid, p-tau, and t-tau. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both report the same XGBoost AUC, but the publication changes the basis of comparison (from other ML models to CSF biomarker measures), so the claim is materially revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Plasma metabolites have the potential to match the AUC of well-established AD CSF biomarkers in a relatively small cohort. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Plasma metabolites have the potential to match the AUC of well-established AD CSF biomarkers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the potential-matching claim but omits the qualifier about cohort size, clarifying the general conclusion while dropping a detail about sample size.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further studies in independent cohorts are needed to validate the effectiveness of the specific panel of blood metabolites for separating AD from controls. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further studies in independent cohorts are needed to validate the effectiveness of the specific panel of blood metabolites for separating AD from controls. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation/need for external validation is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A dedicated multi-modal neural network architecture (DeepLORI) is proposed to predict the time dependent risk of six common comorbidities in epilepsy patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A dedicated multimodal neural network architecture (DeepLORI) is proposed to predict the time-dependent risk of six common comorbidities in epilepsy patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with essentially identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepLORI demonstrates superior performance compared to several existing methods for predicting comorbidity risks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepLORI demonstrates superior performance compared to several existing methods for predicting comorbidities in epilepsy patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is the same in both versions, maintaining the same comparison and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepLORI based predictions can be interpreted on the level of individual patients, making them explainable in the context of existing knowledge about epilepsy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepLORI-based predictions can be interpreted on the level of individual patients, making the model's predictions explainable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state interpretability at the individual-patient level with similar emphasis; wording is slightly streamlined but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model was validated on independent data from around 97,000 patients, showing good generalization and stable prediction performance over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model was validated on independent data from around 97,000 patients, showing good generalization and stable prediction performance over time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Validation sample size and conclusions about generalization and stability are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DR-detector model achieves a testing accuracy of 100% in detecting diabetic retinopathy after training with the combination of ResNet-50 and lesion features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DR-detector model achieves a testing accuracy of 100% in detecting diabetic retinopathy (DR) when trained with the combination of ResNet-50 and lesion features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance claim for the ResNet-50 + lesion features configuration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DR-detector model achieves 99.38% accuracy after training with the combination of VGG-16 and lesion features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DR-detector model achieves a testing accuracy of 99.38% when trained with the combination of VGG-16 and lesion features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and experimental condition match exactly between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using only the hemorrhages feature to train the model results in an accuracy of 99.38%, which is higher than the accuracy when training with all lesion features combined. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using only the hemorrhages feature to train the model results in an accuracy of 99.38% in detecting DR, which is higher than the accuracy when training with all lesion features combined. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements present the same finding about hemorrhage-only training yielding 99.38% accuracy and outperforming combined features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate a higher contribution of specific lesion features toward the performance of the DR-detector model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate a higher contribution of specific lesion features toward the performance of the DR-detector model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both texts draw the same conclusion about the importance of specific lesion features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of using clinically interpretable lesion features to develop robust AI systems for diabetic retinopathy detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of using clinically interpretable features, such as lesions, to develop robust AI systems for DR detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication keeps the same high-level conclusion about using interpretable lesion features for robust DR AI systems, with slightly broadened phrasing but same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study was unable to replicate the findings of a 2016 study that identified top maternal gene expression predictors for spontaneous preterm birth (sPTB). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study was unable to replicate the findings of a 2016 study that identified top maternal gene expression predictors for spontaneous preterm birth (sPTB). (certainty=high, type=result)
