alignments.preprint_claim_text: The random forest (RF) classifier among the ML models performed best with an F1 score of 96%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest classifier among the ML models performed best with an F1 score of 96%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result and numeric performance are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) are capable of providing personalized insights into likely diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) are capable of providing personalized insights into likely diagnoses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact same claim present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs can suggest appropriate specialists for patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs can suggest appropriate specialists for patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs can assess urgent care needs effectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs can assess urgent care needs effectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match between preprint and published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs may support clinicians in refining diagnoses and decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs may support clinicians in refining diagnoses and decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of LLMs offers a promising approach to improving patient outcomes and streamlining healthcare delivery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of LLMs offers a promising approach to improving patient outcomes and streamlining healthcare delivery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same high-level conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All eight saliency map techniques fail at least one of the criteria for trustworthiness in medical imaging. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: All eight saliency map techniques failed at least one of the criteria and were inferior in performance compared with localization networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the failure claim and adds that methods were inferior to localization networks, expanding the original statement with comparative performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most saliency map techniques are less trustworthy when compared to various baseline measures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The repeatability and reproducibility of the majority of the saliency methods were worse than localization networks for both the segmentation and detection datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific repeatability/reproducibility comparisons to localization networks, narrowing the vague 'various baselines' to a concrete baseline.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usage of saliency maps in the high-risk domain of medical imaging warrants additional scrutiny. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of saliency maps in the high-risk domain of medical imaging warrants additional scrutiny and recommend that detection or segmentation models be used if localization is the desired output of the network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the scrutiny recommendation and adds an explicit recommendation to use localization networks when appropriate, clarifying the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Detection or segmentation models should be used if localization is the desired output of the network. (certainty=medium, type=recommendation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of saliency maps in the high-risk domain of medical imaging warrants additional scrutiny and recommend that detection or segmentation models be used if localization is the desired output of the network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states this recommendation; wording and intent are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low daily mean Ct values are followed by an increase in the number of national positive COVID-19 cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The transmissibility of COVID-19 is correlated with the patient's viral load, which can be measured using the cycle threshold (Ct). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims a temporal predictive relationship; the publication reports a general correlation between viral load and transmissibility, removing the explicit temporal forecasting claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep neural network model is the first to capture the interaction of Ct values with the upcoming number of COVID-19 cases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data-driven modeling using Ct and previous number of cases can overcome limitations of previous models that relied on institution-specific variables. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution as demonstrating the value of Ct in data-driven modeling rather than emphasizing novelty ('first to capture'), thus tempering the originality claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has potential application in predicting COVID-19 incidences in other countries and in assessing post-vaccination policies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The OLS and SVR models showed promise in predicting COVID-19 incidences across different institutions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication offers evidence of promise across institutions (a narrower, evidence-based claim) rather than the broader cross-country and policy-assessment potential asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Viral load measurement can enhance outcomes and precision of viral disease predicting models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data-driven modeling using Ct and previous number of cases can overcome limitations of previous models that relied on institution-specific variables. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete modeling evidence that using Ct improves models' generalizability, strengthening and clarifying the preprint's suggestion that viral load measurement enhances predictive models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models initialized with ImageNet-pretrained weights demonstrate superior generalizability over randomly-initialized counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models initialized with ImageNet-pretrained weights demonstrate superior generalizability over randomly initialized counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImageNet-pretrained models exhibit consistent performance during internal and external testing across different training scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ImageNet-pretrained models exhibit consistent performance during internal and external testing across different training scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weight-level ensembles of these models show significantly higher recall during testing compared to individual models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weight-level ensembles of these models show significantly higher recall (p<0.05) during testing compared to individual models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds statistical significance details (p-value) but keeps the substantive ensemble performance claim the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ImageNet pre-trained models trained on medical images converge sooner and generalize better than randomly initialized models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models initialized with ImageNet-pretrained weights demonstrate superior generalizability over randomly initialized counterparts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms superior generalizability but does not report the 'converge sooner' aspect, so the convergence component is not supported and the claim is narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study underscores the promising potential of employing ImageNet-pretrained models for medical images and merging them into ensembles. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study accentuates the benefits of ImageNet-pretrained weight initialization, especially when used with weight-level ensembles, for creating robust and generalizable deep learning solutions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's concluding statement conveys the same overall recommendation and emphasis on pretrained weights and ensembles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable machine learning can be used to monitor data drift in a predictive model deployed within a hospital emergency department.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable machine learning can be used to monitor data drift in clinical settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same idea but in broader terms (clinical settings vs emergency department), so the claim is clarified and its scope broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning classifier trained using pre-COVID-19 data identified patients at high risk of admission to hospital during emergency department attendance with an AUROC of 0.856 before the pandemic and 0.826 during the pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of a machine learning classifier trained on pre-COVID-19 data showed an AUROC of 0.856, while its performance during the COVID-19 pandemic was an AUROC of 0.826.
alignments.rationale: Numbers and wording match directly between preprint and publication, so the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tracking the variation in a feature’s SHAP value relative to its global importance provides a complementary measure of data drift that highlights the need to retrain a predictive model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tracking the variation in a feature’s SHAP value relative to its global importance provides a measure of data drift that indicates the need to retrain a predictive model.
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different phrasing (drops 'complementary' and frames it as 'a measure'), and its certainty is lower, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Observing the relative changes in feature importance can help identify emergent health risks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Observing changes in feature importance can help identify emergent health risks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with near-identical wording and the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A random forest model using 555 discrete, automated measurements achieves an area under the receiver operator characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.92, similar to a deep learning waveform model with an AUC of 0.94.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A random forest model using 555 discrete, automated measurements achieved an area under the receiver operator characteristic curve (AUC) of 0.92, similar to a deep learning waveform model with an AUC of 0.94.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric result and comparison, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A linear model based on 5 measurements achieves high performance with an AUC of 0.86, which is close to a deep learning model and better than NT-proBNP.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A logistic regression model based on five measurements achieved high performance with an AUC of 0.86, close to a deep learning model and better than N-terminal prohormone brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP).
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same performance claim; only minor wording/model-name specifics change (linear→logistic), so content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simpler models generalize better to other sites, as demonstrated by experiments at two independent, external sites.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simpler models were found to be more portable across sites, with experiments at two independent, external sites.
alignments.rationale: The publication communicates the same finding (portable/generalizable across two external sites) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the value of simple electrocardiographic models which perform nearly as well as deep learning models while being much easier to implement and interpret.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the value of simple electrocardiographic models that perform nearly as well as deep learning models, while being much easier to implement and interpret.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multimodal LLM tended to make more errors in extracting and verbalizing image features than in describing preconditions and making logical inferences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multimodal LLM tended to make more errors in extracting and verbalizing image features than in describing preconditions and making logical inferences.
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical in wording and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Erroneous descriptions of image features were common even when the answers were correct.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Erroneous descriptions of image features were common even when the answers were correct.
alignments.rationale: The content is unchanged but the publication presents the claim with higher stated certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need for improved control over image hallucination in zero-shot VQA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for improved control over image hallucination in zero-shot VQA.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance evaluation using the percentage of correct answers to multiple-choice questions may not be sufficient for performance assessment in VQA tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance evaluation using the percentage of correct answers to multiple-choice questions may not be sufficient for performance assessment in VQA tasks.
alignments.rationale: The claim and its expressed certainty are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed automated detection system achieved 97% accuracy, 91% specificity, and 100% sensitivity in diagnosing COVID-19 from X-ray images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning technique achieved an accuracy of 99.4% in diagnosing COVID-19 from X-ray images.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports markedly different (improved) performance numbers for the same overall performance claim, so the result is revised rather than unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of convolutional neural network (CNN) and long short-term memory (LSTM) is effective for automatic COVID-19 diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that the CNN+LSTM combination is effective or mention that architecture, so this architecture-specific claim appears omitted/removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated detection framework can assist doctors in diagnosing COVID-19, providing fast and consistent results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated detection system can assist doctors in diagnosing and treating COVID-19 patients more easily.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the assistance claim to include treatment and frames it as making care easier, so the contribution is clarified and its scope broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed system can be further improved with a larger dataset of COVID-19 images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed system can be further improved with the availability of more COVID-19 images.
alignments.rationale: The limitation/qualification is restated with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed NER implementation achieves an accuracy (F1-score) of 92.98% on the test set and generalizes well on benchmark data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed NER implementation achieves an accuracy (F1-score) of 92.98% on the test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric result (F1 92.98%) verbatim, so this part of the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed NER implementation achieves an accuracy (F1-score) of 92.98% on the test set and generalizes well on benchmark data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NLP framework generalizes well on benchmark data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint included the generalization statement as part of P0; the publication separates it into its own claim but the content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The careful analysis of case examples demonstrates the superiority of the proposed approach in correctly classifying the named entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates superiority in correctly classifying named entities.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the superiority claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NLP can be used to extract key information, such as SDOH factors from free texts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP can be used to extract key information, such as SDOH factors from free texts.
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A more accurate understanding of SDOH is needed to further improve healthcare outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 3D printed cochleae replicate clinical scenarios of electric field imaging profiles at off-stimuli positions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3D printing-neural network co-modelling can interpret electric field imaging profiles of cochlear implant patients.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The co-modelling framework demonstrated autonomous and robust predictions of patient profiles or cochlear geometry.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication (tense shift only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework inferred patients’ in vivo cochlear tissue resistivity, with an estimated mean of 6.6 kΩcm, by cochlear implant telemetry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated mean in vivo cochlear tissue resistivity is 6.6 kΩcm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework is anticipated to facilitate physical modelling and digital twin innovations for electrical prostheses in healthcare.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework is anticipated to facilitate physical modelling and digital twin innovations for neuromodulation implants.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework unfolded the electro-anatomical factors causing current spread in cochlear implants.
alignments.rationale: This explicit claim about electro-anatomical factors does not appear in the preprint and is newly stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study achieved a patient testing accuracy of 75% in distinguishing between COVID-19 and non-COVID-19 thrombosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study achieved a patient testing accuracy of 75% in distinguishing between COVID-19 and non-COVID-19 thrombosis.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Platelet formation differs between COVID-19 and non-COVID-19 thrombosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Platelet formation differs between COVID-19 and non-COVID-19 thrombosis.
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis steps were optimized for efficiency and implemented in a plugin for the image viewer napari.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis steps were optimized for efficiency and implemented in a plugin for the image viewer napari.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DARE, a model based on the transformer encoder, is designed for analyzing longitudinal heterogeneous diabetes data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DARE, a model based on the transformer encoder, is designed for analyzing longitudinal heterogeneous diabetes data.
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DARE outperformed baseline models in predicting comorbidities, treatment, and glycaemic control with AUC scores of 0.88, 0.91, and 0.82 respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DARE outperformed baseline models in predicting comorbidities, treatment, and glycemic control outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention-based encoders improve results compared to different deep learning and classical baseline models for predicting clinical outcomes from T2DM data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention-based encoders improve results compared to different deep learning and classical baseline models for predicting clinical outcomes from T2DM data.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about attention-based encoders is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new deep learning model was proposed to identify unnecessary hemoglobin tests for hospitalized patients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study introduces a novel deep learning model with the potential to significantly reduce healthcare costs and improve patient outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the proposed model in broader, more impact-oriented terms (costs and outcomes) that extend beyond the preprint's descriptive contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves hemoglobin prediction performance with a normality AUC of 95.89% and a stability AUC of 95.94%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrated remarkable Hgb prediction performance, achieving a normality AUC of 95.89% and a Hgb stability AUC of 95.94%.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model recommends a reduction of 9.91% of hemoglobin tests that were deemed unnecessary.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model recommended a reduction of 9.91% of Hgb tests that were deemed unnecessary.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model could generalize well to external patients admitted to another hospital.
alignments.rationale: The generalizability claim to external hospital patients is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed AI system for COVID-19 diagnosis achieves an accuracy of 94.98% on an independent external verification dataset.
alignments.rationale: The specific 94.98% accuracy on an independent external verification dataset is not present in the publication, which reports different metrics/datasets; thus the exact preprint claim was removed or replaced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system demonstrates an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 97.91%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed AI system achieves an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) of 97.81% for multi-way classification on a test cohort of 3,199 scans.
alignments.rationale: The AUC reported in the publication is essentially the same as the preprint's 97.91% (minor numeric rounding difference) and refers to the same performance metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In a reader study, only one radiologist was slightly more accurate than the AI system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI system outperforms five radiologists in more challenging tasks at a speed of two orders of magnitude above them.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim that the AI outperforms five radiologists and emphasizes speed contrasts with the preprint's narrower reader-study statement; this represents a substantive revision and amplification of claimed performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI system is two orders of magnitude faster than radiologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI system outperforms five radiologists in more challenging tasks at a speed of two orders of magnitude above them.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's speed claim (two orders of magnitude faster) within a broader statement about comparative performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI system demonstrates effective multi-class diagnosis capabilities using a large dataset of over 10 thousand CT volumes.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new claim emphasizing multi-class capability and a much larger dataset size that does not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) presents a novel architectural paradigm that redefines the structural foundations of AI models based on Multilayer Perceptron (MLP).
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study advances our understanding of innovative neural network architectures.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts a novel, foundational paradigm while the publication tones this down to advancing understanding of architectures — a weaker, narrower claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KAN-EEG model is effective for efficient seizure detection and has been successfully generalized on three different datasets from diverse geographical regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KAN model exhibits high-level out-of-sample generalization across datasets from diverse geographical regions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims both seizure-detection effectiveness and generalization; the publication retains the generalization claim but omits the specific EEG/seizure-detection emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KAN model exhibits high-level out-of-sample generalization across datasets, demonstrating its inherent adaptability and efficacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KAN model exhibits high-level out-of-sample generalization across datasets from diverse geographical regions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same out-of-sample generalization claim using nearly identical language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The KAN architecture is resilient to model size reduction and shallow network configurations, preventing over-fitting in sample datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The KAN architecture demonstrates resilience to model size reduction and shallow network configurations.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the resilience to smaller/shallow architectures; the over-fitting prevention part is split into a separate publication claim, so this preprint claim is clarified into focused pieces.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study advances the understanding of innovative neural network architectures and highlights the potential of KANs in medical diagnostics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study advances our understanding of innovative neural network architectures.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the general advancement claim but drops the specific statement about potential in medical diagnostics, making the published claim narrower and weaker.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine-learning model developed in this study can forecast whether an individual will develop chronic kidney disease (CKD) within the next 6 or 12 months.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models can facilitate close monitoring of at-risk individuals and enable early detection of CKD.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts direct forecasting ability for individual 6/12-month outcomes; the publication frames the models as tools to facilitate monitoring/early detection, a slightly narrower/less direct formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Convoluted Neural Networks (CNN) approach achieved the best performance metrics with an AUROC of 0.957 for the 6-month model and 0.954 for the 12-month model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) model achieved a test set AUROC of 0.957 for 6-month predictions and 0.954 for 12-month predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUROC values and model performance as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key features impacting the progression of CKD identified in the study include diabetes mellitus, age, gout, and certain medications such as sulfonamides and angiotensins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified diabetes mellitus, age, gout, and medications such as sulfonamides and angiotensins as the most prominent predictors of CKD.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model can assist policy-makers in predicting trends of CKD in the population, enabling early detection and better resource allocation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning model developed in this study could serve as a useful tool for policymakers in predicting CKD trends in the population.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the policy-relevance claim with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After balancing for covariates, both sexes similarly benefit from cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT).
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include any statement about sex-based equivalence of CRT benefit; this claim does not appear to be carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning (ML) predicts short-term CRT response and may help with CRT procedure planning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning predicts short-term CRT response and may assist in CRT procedure and early post-CRT care planning.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the ML prediction and procedural-planning utility but extends it to early post-CRT care; the certainty in the publication is slightly lower, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive lasso model was more accurate than class I ACC/AHA guidelines criteria for predicting CRT response.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptive lasso model was the most accurate with an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.759.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports adaptive lasso accuracy but omits the explicit comparison to ACC/AHA guidelines present in the preprint, so the claim is narrowed and softened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The primary endpoint was met by 337 patients (45.5%) in the study.
alignments.rationale: None of the publication claims reproduce this specific primary-endpoint count or percentage; the numerical outcome is not present in the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shout is a free mental health text messaging service that provides support for individuals in the UK experiencing mental or emotional distress.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shout is a free mental health text messaging service that provides support for individuals in the UK experiencing mental or emotional distress.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of natural language processing (NLP) to Shout's data set allows for accurate classification of conversation stages, behaviours, and texter demographics.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same claim about NLP enabling accurate classification of multiple label types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides insights into the mental health needs of individuals not using traditional mental health services.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides insights into the mental health needs of individuals not using traditional mental health services.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed method achieved an F-score of 85.0% for identifying the medication mentions in the test set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed method achieved an F-score of 85.0% for identifying the medication mentions in the test set.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the identical reported F-score result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method significantly outperformed existing medication information extraction systems for medical records, which had F-scores ranging from 42.9% to 68.9%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method significantly outperformed existing medication information extraction systems for medical records, which had F-scores ranging from 42.9% to 68.9%.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the comparative performance claim with the same baseline ranges.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our medication information extraction approach extracted about 27% more medication mentions from our evaluation set while eliminating many false positives compared to existing baseline systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our medication information extraction approach extracted about 27% more medication mentions from our evaluation set while eliminating many false positives compared to existing baseline systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same quantitative extraction and false-positive reduction claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integration of our annotation system with clinical recording applications has the potential to improve patients’ understanding and recall of key information from their clinic visits.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integration of our annotation system with clinical recording applications has the potential to improve patients' understanding and recall of key information from their clinic visits.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same stated potential application and expected benefit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion by sex in health research is inconsistent and often unreported, with men being disproportionately less included than women. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of sex in health research is inconsistent and often unreported, with men being disproportionately less included than women. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Few studies utilize artificial intelligence and natural language processing techniques to investigate sex-related differences or disparities in health research. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Researchers are increasingly aware of sex-based data bias, but the process toward correction is slow. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q2 matches P2 verbatim in content and emphasis, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Best practices for using big data analytics to address sex-specific biases in understanding diseases need to be established. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a claim about establishing best practices for big data analytics to address sex-specific biases; instead it introduces a different/consequential claim (Q3), so P3 is not represented in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning system achieved good performance in identifying COVID-19 from other pneumonia with AUC values of 0.87 and 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning system achieved good performance in identifying COVID-19 from other pneumonia with AUC values of 0.87 and 0.88. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning system succeeded in stratifying patients into high-risk and low-risk groups with significant differences in hospital-stay time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning system succeeded in stratifying patients into high- and low-risk groups with significant differences in hospital-stay time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning system automatically focused on abnormal areas that showed consistent characteristics with reported radiological findings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning system automatically focused on abnormal areas that showed consistent characteristics with reported radiological findings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 mirrors P2 in content and implication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fully automatic deep learning system provides a convenient method for COVID-19 diagnostic and prognostic analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed pipeline utilizes reservoir computing to define effective connectivity of the brain in stroke patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reservoir computing-based pipeline effectively defines brain connectivity representations in stroke data derived from magnetic resonance imaging. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P0 with slightly more specific wording (mentions MRI-derived stroke data and 'connectivity representations'), clarifying but not changing the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification module of the pipeline achieved an area under the curve of 0.69 for categorizing effective connectivity in stroke patients versus matched healthy controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification module within the pipeline achieved an area under the curve of 0.69 using graph convolutional networks, 0.72 using local topological profiling random forest, and 0.71 using support vector machine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 expands the original single AUC result by reporting AUCs for multiple classifiers, revising the result presentation while preserving the original 0.69 value for one method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of explainable AI tools allowed for the interpretation of disrupted networks across brain networks in stroke patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of explainable AI tools allows for the interpretation of disrupted networks in brain connectivity, providing insights into stroke classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q2 retains the core claim about explainable AI enabling interpretation of disrupted networks but clarifies the application by linking it explicitly to insights for stroke classification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analytical framework enhances clinical interpretability and instills confidence in decision-making processes for translating research findings into clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed machine learning pipeline enhances clinical interpretability and supports decision-making processes in translating research findings into clinical practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q3 expresses the same conclusion as P3 with nearly identical meaning and emphasis on clinical interpretability and decision support.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning pipeline has the potential to improve stroke patient stratification and may be applicable to other brain diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the broader claim about applicability to other brain diseases or explicitly claim potential to improve stratification beyond what is covered by the matched claims, so P4 is not represented.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT has potential applications in healthcare education, research, and practice, with benefits cited in 85.0% of the records reviewed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT demonstrates benefits in health care education, research, and practice, including improved scientific writing and enhanced research equity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q0 conveys the same overall claim about benefits in the same domains but omits the specific 85% statistic and provides examples of benefits, thereby clarifying the nature of the benefits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Concerns regarding the use of ChatGPT were expressed in 96.7% of the records, highlighting ethical issues such as bias, plagiarism, and inaccurate citations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Concerns regarding ChatGPT use include ethical, copyright, transparency, and legal issues, as well as risks of bias and inaccurate content. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 reflects the same concern theme but expands/clarifies the list of specific issues (adding copyright, transparency, legal) while dropping the numeric prevalence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT can enhance healthcare practice by streamlining workflow and refining personalized medicine. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The promising applications of ChatGPT can induce paradigm shifts in health care education, research, and practice. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the potential impact more strongly ("induce paradigm shifts") and more broadly across domains than the preprint's more modest statement about workflow and personalized medicine, indicating a substantive strengthening and broadening of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT does not currently qualify to be listed as an author in scientific articles unless guidelines are revised. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT does not qualify to be listed as an author in scientific articles unless the ICMJE/COPE guidelines are revised or amended. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Q3 reiterates P3's limitation claim with specific reference to authorship guidelines, preserving the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An initiative involving all stakeholders is urgently needed to establish a code of ethics for the responsible use of ChatGPT in healthcare. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An initiative involving all stakeholders in health care education, research, and practice is urgently needed to set a code of ethics for the responsible use of ChatGPT. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Q4 restates P4 with equivalent content and urgency, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUROC and AUPRC for predicting new onset AF within 1 year of an ECG were 0.83 and 0.21, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific baseline AUROC/AUPRC pair (0.83/0.21); instead it reports an AUROC of 0.85 (likely for a model variant), so the exact preprint numeric claim is not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding age and sex improved the AUROC to 0.85 and the AUPRC to 0.23. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved an area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.85 for predicting new-onset AF within 1 year of an ECG. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q1 reports the AUROC value (0.85) but omits the explicit statement that this was achieved by adding age and sex and does not report the AUPRC; thus the numeric result is present but the attribution/detail is altered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hazard ratio for the predicted high- vs. low-risk groups over a 30-year span was 7.2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hazard ratio for the predicted high-risk versus low-risk groups over a 30-year span was 7.2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Q2 matches P2 exactly in value and interpretation, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicted new onset AF at 1 year with a sensitivity of 69%, specificity of 81%, and positive predictive value of 12%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the same sensitivity/specificity/PPV triplet; instead it introduces a different performance-related claim (Q3) about detecting high-risk patients linked to subsequent stroke, so P3's specific metrics are not present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can predict new onset AF from the 12-lead ECG in patients with no prior history of AF. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep neural network can predict new-onset atrial fibrillation (AF) from the resting 12-lead ECG in patients without a history of AF. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Q0 restates P4's core conclusion using synonymous wording (deep neural network vs deep learning) and the same population, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of an AI-powered chatbot in enhancing surgical education through oral board simulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the potential of an AI-powered chatbot in enhancing surgical education through oral board simulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in wording and certainty to the preprint claim, so meaning, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation revealed that 11 to 25% of chatbot simulations had no errors and an additional 11% to 35% contained errors of minimal clinical significance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation revealed that 11–25% of chatbot simulations had no errors and an additional 11%–35% contained errors of minimal clinical significance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric results and interpretation are the same in the publication as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chatbot limitations included incorrect management advice and critical omissions of information. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chatbot limitations included incorrect management advice and critical omissions of information. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation text is unchanged but the publication expresses higher certainty, so the semantic content is the same while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating domain-specific knowledge and expert insights is crucial for improving the efficacy of AI tools in medical education. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating domain-specific knowledge and expert insights is crucial for improving the efficacy of AI tools in medical education. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation appears verbatim with identical framing and certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ensemble of classifiers improved performance in detecting Goals Of Care discussions in EHR notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using active learning, we reduced the annotation cost to fine-tune our ensemble by 70% while improving its performance in our test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication links the ensemble performance improvement to the active learning approach (adds context about how improvement was achieved), so the claim is clarified rather than changed in substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using active learning reduced the annotation cost to fine-tune the classifier by 70%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using active learning, we reduced the annotation cost to fine-tune our ensemble by 70% while improving its performance in our test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same cost-reduction result but additionally ties the active learning to performance improvement in the test set, clarifying the overall effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier achieved a true positive rate of 72% and a false positive rate of 8% when classifying notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our classifier achieved a true positive rate of 72% and a false positive rate of 8% when classifying notes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance metrics are reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Improvements are needed before the classifier can be used to enrich trial populations or as an outcome measure. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Improvements are needed before using the classifier to enrich trial populations or as an outcome measure. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation and its scope remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm achieved an AUC of 0.810 when predicting type 2 diabetes using non-invasive traditional risk factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports an AUC of 0.810 for a model using only non-invasive traditional risk factors; this preprint-specific result does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUC for the deep learning model using only fundus images was 0.731. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model for prediction of type 2 diabetes achieved an AUC of 0.731 when using only fundus images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The fundus-image-only AUC is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The addition of traditional risk factors to the deep learning algorithm improved its discriminative performance to an AUC of 0.844. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of traditional risk factors (TRFs) to the deep learning algorithm improved the discriminative performance to an AUC of 0.844. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The combined-model AUC and interpretation are the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall net reclassification improvement (NRI) from adding the deep learning algorithm to the traditional risk factors model was 50.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall net reclassification improvement (NRI) from adding the deep learning algorithm to the TRF model was 50.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The NRI value and its framing are unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate that the deep learning algorithm can be a useful tool for stratifying individuals at high risk of type 2 diabetes in the general population. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning algorithm can be a useful tool for stratifying individuals at high risk of type 2 diabetes in the general population. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the model's potential use for risk stratification is maintained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Missed death cases are 3.14 times higher than missed survival cases for mortality prediction in a typical machine learning setting without bias correction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Missed death cases are 3.14 times higher than missed survival cases for mortality prediction in a typical machine learning setting without bias correction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric ratio and context are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The double prioritized (DP) bias correction technique consistently boosts the minority class recall for underrepresented groups by up to 38.0%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The double prioritized (DP) bias correction technique consistently boosts the minority class recall for underrepresented groups by up to 38.0%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance improvement magnitude and claim are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DP reduces relative disparities across race and age groups, achieving up to 88.0% better performance than existing sampling solutions in terms of minority class recall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DP reduces relative disparities across race and age groups, achieving up to 88.0% better performance than existing sampling solutions in terms of minority class recall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative improvement and scope are presented the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biases exist in the widely accepted one-machine-learning-model-fits-all-population approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biases exist in the widely accepted one-machine-learning-model-fits-all-population approach. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about bias in the standard approach is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The invention of a bias correction method that produces specialized machine learning prognostication models for underrepresented racial and age groups may reduce life-threatening prediction mistakes for minority populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bias correction method may reduce potentially life-threatening prediction mistakes for minority populations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly rephrases the potential impact (adds 'potentially') but preserves the same cautioned conclusion and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models without genetic liabilities achieved AUCs of 0.70 and 0.72 using RF and NN methods, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models without genetic liabilities achieved AUCs of 0.70 and 0.72 using the random forest and the neural network methods, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported AUCs and methods are the same between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding genetic liabilities resulted in a modest improvement in the AUC for RF but not for NN. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adding genetic liabilities improved the AUC for the random forest but not for the neural network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication expresses stronger certainty about the RF improvement, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best prediction model was achieved using RF with an AUC of 0.71. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best classification model was achieved when feature selection and classification were performed using random forest with an AUC of 0.71. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The identification of the best model and its AUC are consistent; the publication adds procedural detail but does not change the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating genetic factors in the model may provide a modest incremental value for hypertension prediction beyond baseline characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating genetic liabilities for lipids in a machine learning model may provide incremental value for hypertension classification beyond baseline characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about incremental value of genetic information is preserved; the publication specifies 'genetic liabilities for lipids' which aligns with the preprint's genetic focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlighted the importance of genetic liabilities for both total cholesterol and LDL within the same prediction model adds value to the classification of hypertension. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating genetic liabilities for lipids in a machine learning model may provide incremental value for hypertension classification beyond baseline characteristics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's specific cholesterol/LDL focus to 'lipids' more broadly, which broadens the scope while preserving the core conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The consultation process produced nine ranked priorities for the use of AI in primary care in Ontario, Canada.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The consultation process produced nine ranked priorities for AI in primary care in Ontario, Canada.
alignments.rationale: The two statements are effectively identical in scope and wording, conveying the same result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are immediate opportunities to use existing resources to develop and test AI for priority areas at the patient, provider, and system level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are immediate opportunities to use existing resources to develop and test AI for priority areas at the patient, provider, and system level.
alignments.rationale: Wording is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high), so the claim is unchanged semantically but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Study findings can be used to inform future research and development of AI for primary care, and to guide resource planning and allocation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Study findings can inform future research and development of AI for primary care and guide resource planning and allocation.
alignments.rationale: Text and implication are the same; publication restates the same conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An environmental scan identified 110 artificial intelligence-driven tools with potential relevance to primary care.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this specific environmental-scan result; therefore the claim appears removed from the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Larger scale, sustainable innovations in AI for primary care require longer-term projects that focus on data and interdisciplinary work.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about longer-term, data- and interdisciplinary-focused projects does not appear in the preprint and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mean subchondral bone length (SBL) values for knees with joint space narrowing (JSN) were consistently different from knees without JSN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean subchondral bone length (SBL) values for knees with joint space narrowing (JSN) were consistently different from knees without JSN.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Greater changes of SBL from baseline were associated with greater pain and disability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Greater changes of SBL from baseline were associated with greater pain and disability in knee osteoarthritis patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the patient population but preserves the original association and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For knees with medial or lateral JSN, the odds ratios between lowest and highest quartiles corresponding to SBL changes for future knee replacement (KR) were 5.68 and 7.19, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For knees with medial or lateral JSN, the odds ratios for future knee replacement between the lowest and highest quartiles corresponding to SBL changes were 5.68 and 7.19, respectively.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SBL quantified osteoarthritis (OA) status based on JSN severity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SBL quantified osteoarthritis status based on JSN severity and shows promise as an imaging marker in predicting clinical and structural OA outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the quantification claim with an added statement of promise as a marker; the core claim is retained but presented together with the related conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SBL has promise as an imaging marker in predicting clinical and structural OA outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SBL quantified osteoarthritis status based on JSN severity and shows promise as an imaging marker in predicting clinical and structural OA outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges this conclusion with the quantification result; content is consistent though consolidated in a single concluding sentence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bladder4Net, a deep learning pipeline, can classify whole-slide histopathology images of bladder cancer into low-risk and high-risk categories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bladder4Net deep learning pipeline achieved a weighted average F1-score of 0.91 on the NHBCS dataset and 0.99 on the TCGA dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports performance metrics rather than the high-level capability statement; the metrics support the classification claim, so the preprint claim is clarified by quantitative results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The weighted average F1-score of the Bladder4Net approach was 0.91 on the NHBCS dataset and 0.99 on the TCGA dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bladder4Net deep learning pipeline achieved a weighted average F1-score of 0.91 on the NHBCS dataset and 0.99 on the TCGA dataset.
alignments.rationale: Performance numbers are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients predicted as high-risk had worse overall survival than those predicted as low-risk, with a Log-rank P-value of 0.004.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients predicted as high risk had worse overall survival than those predicted as low risk in the NHBCS test set, with a log-rank p-value of 0.004.
alignments.rationale: Survival comparison and p-value are reported identically; publication clarifies the dataset (NHBCS) but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If validated through prospective trials, the Bladder4Net model could be used in clinical settings to improve patient care.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model, if validated through prospective trials, could be used in clinical settings to improve patient care.
alignments.rationale: The conditional clinical-implementation statement is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimized deep neural network (DNN) achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.90 in distinguishing Bronchiolitis Obliterans Syndrome (BOS) from non-BOS CT scans.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance correlates with BOS progression, with ROC-AUC values of 0.88 for stage I, 0.91 for stage II, and 0.94 for stage III BOS.
alignments.rationale: Stage-wise performance numbers and the relationship to progression are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DNN demonstrated a robust ROC-AUC of 0.87 in predicting BOS in at-risk patients with FEV1 between 80% and 90% of best FEV1.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DNN demonstrates the ability to predict BOS in at-risk patients with a ROC-AUC of 0.87.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach shows potential for improving BOS diagnosis by enabling early detection and management.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Techniques to limit overfitting are crucial for unlocking the potential of DNNs in low-data settings.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning model trained on body silhouettes enables accurate estimation of VAT, ASAT, and GFAT volumes.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Body silhouette images can estimate important measures of fat distribution, laying the scientific foundation for scalable population-based assessment.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning-based network can distinguish between eyes with prior acute optic neuritis and healthy control eyes using peripapillary ring scans. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The final network achieved an accuracy of 0.85 and an AUC of 0.86 in recognizing eyes with prior optic neuritis. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning network outperformed the pRNFL measurement, which had an AUC of 0.77 in recognizing optic neuritis eyes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using data from a second center, the deep learning network achieved an accuracy of 0.77 and an AUC of 0.90 compared to pRNFL's AUC of 0.84. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DL-based disease classification of prior optic neuritis has the potential to outperform thickness-based classification. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks (NN) allow for an accurate estimation of patient dose based on dose-area product (DAP) in dental cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks (NN) allow for an accurate estimation of patient dose based on dose-area product (DAP) in dental cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is carried forward verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean absolute error (MAE) for organ dose/DAP in 'Coordinate' mode ranged from 0.18% to 2.90%, while in 'AP/JAW' mode it ranged from 2.74% to 14.13%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean absolute error (MAE) for organ dose/DAP in 'Coordinate' mode ranged from 0.18% to 2.90%, while in 'AP/JAW' mode it ranged from 2.74% to 14.13%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MAE for effective dose (ED) was 0.23% in 'Coordinate' mode and 4.30% in 'AP/JAW' mode, compared to 5.70% for the multiple linear regression (MLR) model and 20.19%-32.67% for direct conversion coefficients (CCs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MAE for effective dose (ED) was 0.23% for 'Coordinate' mode and 4.30% for 'AP/JAW' mode, compared to 5.70% for the multiple linear regression (MLR) model and 20.19%-32.67% for direct conversion coefficients (CCs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance comparisons and numeric values are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning model predicts the onset of Atrial Fibrillation (AF) on average 30.8 minutes before it appears. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep-learning model can predict the transition from sinus rhythm to atrial fibrillation on average 30.8 minutes before the onset appears. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same average prediction lead time and target transition with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves an accuracy of 83% and an F1-score of 85% on the test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves an accuracy of 83% and an F1 score of 85% on the test data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics are identical between preprint and published versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions can be made using R-to-R interval signals obtained from common wearable devices such as smartwatches and smart bands. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The WARN model allows for continuous heart monitoring and early detection of atrial fibrillation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has low computational time and could be embedded in smartphones for continuous heart monitoring. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The low computational cost of the WARN model makes it suitable for integration into wearable technology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes embedding-in-smartphones as suitability for wearables; same practical implication and similar certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Early detection of atrial fibrillation using the WARN model can potentially reduce emergency interventions and improve patient outcomes. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This outcome/impact claim about reducing emergency interventions is new in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current research on mitigating bias issues in real-world data (RWD) is limited in terms of disease variety and health care applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current research on mitigating bias issues in real-world data (RWD) is limited in terms of disease variety and health care applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The observation about limited scope of current research is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing studies often indicate positive outcomes when using pre-processing techniques to address algorithmic bias. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing studies often indicate positive outcomes when using pre-processing techniques to address algorithmic bias. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report similar findings regarding pre-processing techniques and positive outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There remain unresolved questions within the field that require further research, including pinpointing the root causes of bias in machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There remain unresolved questions within the field that require further research, including pinpointing the root causes of bias in machine learning models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same identified knowledge gaps and call for further research.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper provides useful reference material and insights to researchers regarding AI/ML fairness in real-world health care data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fair AI/ML in health care is a burgeoning field that requires a heightened research focus to cover diverse applications and different types of RWD. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fair AI/ML in health care is a burgeoning field that requires a heightened research focus to cover diverse applications and different types of real-world data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The call for increased research focus on fair AI/ML in healthcare is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vaccine uptake can be modeled as a reinforcement learning dynamic between vaccine adopters and the vaccine hesitant. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vaccine uptake can be modeled as a reinforcement learning dynamic between vaccine adopters and the vaccine hesitant. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Modeling approach claim is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Well designed and timed incentive programs can improve vaccine uptake by shifting the Nash equilibrium upward in large populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Well designed and timed incentive programs can improve vaccine uptake by shifting the Nash equilibrium upward in large populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The incentive-programs result is reported unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incentive sizes above a certain threshold show diminishing returns in improving vaccine uptake. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incentive sizes above a certain threshold show diminishing returns in improving vaccine uptake. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The interaction between vaccine adopters and the vaccine hesitant can be described as a Hawk-Dove evolutionary game with an internal evolutionarily stable Nash equilibrium. (certainty=high, type=result)
