alignments.preprint_claim_text: COMPOSER achieved a consistently high area under the curve (AUC) in predicting sepsis across different patient cohorts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COMPOSER achieved a consistently high area under the curve (AUC) in a sequential prediction setting, with values ranging from 0.925 to 0.953 in ICU and 0.938 to 0.945 in ED.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim but adds the sequential setting and exact AUC ranges, which clarifies the original claim without changing its meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COMPOSER flags unfamiliar cases as ‘indeterminate’ rather than making spurious predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COMPOSER is specifically designed to reduce false alarms by flagging unfamiliar cases as indeterminate rather than making spurious predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same functional claim (flagging unfamiliar cases as indeterminate) while framing it as a design goal to reduce false alarms, so the core claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 20% of non-septic and 8% of septic patients were identified as ‘indeterminate’ by COMPOSER.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximately 20% of non-septic patients and 8% of septic patients were identified as indeterminate by COMPOSER.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numeric indeterminate rates exactly but reports them with higher certainty; the factual content is unchanged while the stated confidence is stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COMPOSER provided early warning within a clinically actionable timeframe prior to the first antibiotics order.
alignments.pub_claim_text: COMPOSER provided early warning within a clinically actionable timeframe, identifying patients at high risk for sepsis up to 22.8 hours prior to the first antibiotics order in ICU and 4.5 hours in ED.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete lead-time estimates (ICU and ED) while preserving the original claim that warnings occur within clinically actionable timeframes, thus clarifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models have the potential to assist in the diagnosis of infectious keratitis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent studies have demonstrated the potential of using DL in assisting the diagnosis of infectious keratitis (IK).
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same cautious conclusion about DL's potential for IK diagnosis, aligning closely with the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This systematic review and meta-analysis aims to compare the performance of various deep learning models with clinical experts and microbiological results in diagnosing infectious keratitis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This systematic review and meta-analysis aims to compare the performance of various DL models with clinical experts and microbiological results in diagnosing IK.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the stated aim of the study verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of the systematic review will depend on the quality of the available published literature related to deep learning in infectious keratitis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of DL models in diagnosing IK remains to be elucidated.
alignments.rationale: The preprint notes a methodological limitation (dependence on literature quality) while the publication emphasizes an evidence gap (uncertain accuracy); these are related but the focus shifts, so the claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study will help identify gaps in the current clinical evidence regarding the use of deep learning for diagnosing infectious keratitis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of DL models in diagnosing IK remains to be elucidated.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly highlights an evidence gap (uncertain accuracy), which clarifies and operationalizes the preprint's more general claim about identifying gaps in the literature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AssistDent ® Artificial Intelligence software significantly improves dentists’ ability to detect enamel-only proximal caries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AssistDent AI software significantly improves dentists' ability to detect enamel-only proximal caries.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about AssistDent's impact on detection, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The control group found 44.3% of the caries, whereas the experimental group found 75.8%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The control group found 44.3% of the caries, whereas the experimental group found 75.8%.
alignments.rationale: The numerical comparison between control and experimental groups is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increase in sensitivity of 71% and decrease in specificity of 11% are statistically significant (p<0.01).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The increase in sensitivity of 71% and decrease in specificity of 11% are statistically significant (p <0.01).
alignments.rationale: The statistical significance and effect sizes are reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial Intelligence software could be considered as a tool to support preventive dentistry in general practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the broader interpretive/concluding claim about adopting AI for preventive dentistry; it instead reports experimental results without this general practice recommendation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modeling methods such as mathematical and statistical models, AI, and data science have addressed the challenges posed by the COVID-19 pandemic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reviewed modeling methods traverse all relevant disciplines, including statistical modeling, AI, and simulation.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts that methods have addressed pandemic challenges, whereas the publication neutrally surveys methods and highlights gaps; this tempers the original claim to a descriptive statement about coverage rather than achievement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review identifies gaps in the current modeling of COVID-19 and suggests potential research directions for future studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Critical analyses identify significant issues and gaps in COVID-19 modeling, such as the overwhelming focus on simple techniques while overlooking intrinsic and foundational issues.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the nature of gaps and issues identified, providing more detailed critical analyses that clarify the review's recommendations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A comprehensive review of COVID-19 modeling processes, data, and impacts has been conducted, summarizing the progress made in various methodologies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This review generates a systematic, structured, and contrastive landscape and taxonomy of global COVID-19 modeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution more specifically as a systematic, structured taxonomy and landscape, which clarifies the preprint's statement about a comprehensive review.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OtoXNet with multiple composite images achieved 84.8% class-weighted accuracy in eardrum classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: OtoXNet achieved 84.8% class-weighted accuracy with 3.8% standard deviation in classifying eardrum diseases using multiple composite images.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same accuracy result with an added standard deviation, preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a statistically significant difference in performance between OtoXNet and human-selected keyframes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a statistically significant difference in performance between OtoXNet with multiple composite images and the human-selected keyframes.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the statistically significant performance difference between OtoXNet and human keyframes, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The difference in means of keyframe and single composites was not significant.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report this specific non-significance claim about keyframe versus single composites, so the claim is not present in the published text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of multiple composite images in analyzing eardrum abnormalities is advantageous compared to using single composite images or manual keyframe selection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of multiple composite images is advantageous compared to using single composite images or manual keyframe selection for analyzing eardrum abnormalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication endorses the advantage but with a slightly lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so the substantive claim is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seventy-six studies were included in this review, highlighting the current use of ML-based methods in epigenome-wide studies for identifying DNA methylation signatures associated with cancer prognosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eighty-three studies were included in this review, revealing a great heterogeneity in ML-based methodological strategies used by epigenome-wide studies to identify DNA methylation markers associated with cancer prognosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same result but with a different study count (83 vs 76) and slightly rephrases the finding, so the claim content was revised (numeric change) while scope and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The systematic review revealed that the performance of ML-based approaches has not been adequately evaluated yet. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The systematic review revealed that the performance of ML-based approaches has not been adequately evaluated yet and that methodological and reporting flaws were common in the identified studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the original evaluation gap claim but explicitly couples it with methodological/reporting flaws, broadening and clarifying the original point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is great heterogeneity in ML-based methodological strategies used by epigenome-wide studies to identify DNA methylation markers associated with cancer prognosis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eighty-three studies were included in this review, revealing a great heterogeneity in ML-based methodological strategies used by epigenome-wide studies to identify DNA methylation markers associated with cancer prognosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The heterogeneity claim is present in the publication with identical phrasing but embedded in a result sentence that also updates the study count, so it is clarified (minor contextual change) without scope shift.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methodological and reporting flaws were common in the identified studies using ML techniques. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The systematic review revealed that the performance of ML-based approaches has not been adequately evaluated yet and that methodological and reporting flaws were common in the identified studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about methodological and reporting flaws but reports it with higher certainty and bundles it with the evaluation gap, so the claim is essentially unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmarking studies are needed to compare the relative performance of various approaches for specific cancer types. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmarking studies are needed to compare the relative performance of various ML approaches for specific cancer types. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same recommendation for benchmarking studies with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thirty-nine differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified between controls and AMI individuals from the training sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Thirty-nine differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified between controls and AMI individuals from the training sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim, so there is no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two genes (AQP9 and SOCS3) show their diagnosis value with the area under the curve (AUC) > 0.9 in both the training and testing sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two genes, AQP9 and SOCS3, show diagnostic value with the area under the curve (AUC) > 0.9 in both the training and testing sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical diagnostic performance claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AQP9 and SOCS3 in leukocytes may be conducive to identifying AMI patients with SCAD patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AQP9 and SOCS3 in leukocytes may be conducive to identifying AMI patients with SCAD patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about leukocyte markers is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AQP9 and SOCS3 are closely associated with monocytes and neutrophils, which might contribute to advancing AMI diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AQP9 and SOCS3 are closely associated with monocytes and neutrophils, which might contribute to advancing AMI diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The association with specific immune cells and its potential diagnostic relevance is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple clinical characteristics, multicenter, and large-sample relevant trials are still needed to confirm its clinical value. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple clinical characteristics, multicenter, and large-sample relevant trials are still needed to confirm the clinical value of AQP9 and SOCS3. (certainty=low, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the needed trials are specifically to confirm the clinical value of the two genes, but the underlying limitation and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QTNet achieves mean absolute errors of 12.63ms for internal-test and 12.30ms for external-validation in estimating absolute QT intervals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QTNet achieves mean absolute errors of 12.63ms for internal-test and 12.30ms for external-validation in estimating absolute QT intervals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact same performance numbers are reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QTNet detects Dofetilide-induced QTc prolongation with 87% sensitivity and 77% specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QTNet detects Dofetilide-induced QTc prolongation with 87% sensitivity and 77% specificity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The detection performance metrics are reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The negative predictive value of QTNet is greater than 95% when the pre-test probability of drug-induced QTc prolongation is below 25%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The negative predictive value of QTNet is greater than 95% when the pre-test probability of drug-induced QTc prolongation is below 25%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The NPV claim with the same pre-test probability condition is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Drug-induced QT prolongation risk can be tracked from ECG lead-I using deep learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: QTNet, a deep learning model, infers QT intervals from Lead-I ECG and detects clinically meaningful QT-prolongation episodes during Dofetilide drug loading. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint made a broad claim about tracking drug-induced QT risk generally from Lead-I; the publication frames the finding more specifically around inferring QT and detecting episodes during Dofetilide loading, narrowing the claim's generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QTNet is a novel regression model that can be used on Lead-I ECG streams for tracking drug-induced QT prolongation in out-of-hospital settings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the out-of-hospital application claim present in the preprint, so this usage/contribution statement was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-classification model achieved greater than 85% training and testing accuracy to distinguish clinical malaria from non-malarial infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-classification model achieved greater than 85% training and testing accuracy in distinguishing clinical malaria from non-malarial infections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The key accuracy result is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: To distinguish uncomplicated malaria from non-malarial infections, the approach identified platelet counts, red blood cell counts, lymphocyte counts, and percentages as the top classifiers with a test accuracy of 0.801. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Platelet counts, red blood cell counts, lymphocyte counts, and percentages were identified as the top classifiers for uncomplicated malaria with a test accuracy of 0.801. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The same top features and accuracy are reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classifier for severe malaria achieved a test accuracy of 0.960, with mean platelet volume and mean cell volume being the unique classifiers of severe malaria. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier for severe malaria achieved a test accuracy of 0.96, with mean platelet volume and mean cell volume being the unique classifiers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance and feature claims for severe malaria are present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides proof of concept methods that classify uncomplicated malaria and severe malaria from non-malarial infections, demonstrating that machine learning approaches are feasible tools for clinical decision support. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that machine learning approaches can be a feasible tool for clinical decision support in distinguishing malaria from non-malarial infections. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the proof-of-concept conclusion that ML is a feasible clinical decision support tool for distinguishing malaria from other infections.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning approaches could be incorporated into clinical decision-support algorithms for the diagnosis of acute febrile illness in the future. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning approaches could potentially be incorporated into clinical decision-support algorithms for diagnosing acute febrile illness in endemic settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the prospective incorporation claim but clarifies/narrows the intended deployment to endemic settings, without changing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed deep learning-based histological grade model provides similar prognostic performance as the conventional Nottingham Histological Grade (NHG) method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning model (predGrade) provides a similar prognostic performance as clinical Nottingham Histological Grade (NHG). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same overall conclusion about comparable prognostic performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model demonstrated a Hazard Ratio (HR) of 2.27 for grade 3 vs 1 in the internal test set, indicating its prognostic capability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or repeat the specific internal-test HR value (2.27) in the provided claims, so this numeric internal-test claim appears omitted in the published summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a high degree of inter-assessor variability in routine histopathology assessment of NHG, which motivates the need for model-based decision support. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Routine histopathology assessment of NHG has a high degree of inter-assessor variability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same variability concern but states it with higher certainty, amplifying the confidence in this conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No significant difference was found between the HR estimates of NHG and the deep learning model in the independent test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There was no significant difference in hazard ratio estimates between clinical NHG and predGrade in the independent test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The independent-test result about no significant difference in HR estimates is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An artificial intelligence model was developed to predict which hospitalized patients are at increased risk of mortality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LightGBM model achieved a Receiver Operating Characteristics Curve-Area Under the Curve (ROC-AUC) of 0.89 and an accuracy of 0.72 when trained with 22 variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same underlying model but with specific performance metrics and variable count, which clarifies the original general development claim rather than contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LightGBM model achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.89 when including Length of Stay and Days to Intensive Care Unit Transfer variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LightGBM model achieved a Receiver Operating Characteristics Curve-Area Under the Curve (ROC-AUC) of 0.89 and an accuracy of 0.72 when trained with 22 variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same ROC-AUC and adds accuracy and the explicit number of features (22), clarifying the model specification that the preprint reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive mortality model may help providers optimize resource utilization when managing large caseloads during shift changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive mortality model can assist providers in optimizing resource utilization when managing large caseloads.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same intended clinical utility with essentially the same scope and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrated a sensitivity of 0.97 and specificity of 0.68 with the dataset that included all variables.
alignments.rationale: No sensitivity/specificity values appear in the publication claims; the specific performance metrics reported in the preprint are not present in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model trained with 20 variables achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.86 and an accuracy of 0.71.
alignments.rationale: This additional model variant (20 variables) and its metrics are present in the publication but were not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The top features for the model trained with 22 variables included 'Total length of Stay,' 'Admit to ICU Transfer Days,' and 'Lymphocyte Next Lab Value.'
alignments.rationale: Feature importance details appear in the publication but were not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The top features for the model trained with 20 variables included 'Lymphocyte First Lab Value,' 'Hemoglobin First Lab Value,' and 'Hemoglobin Next Lab Value.'
alignments.rationale: The publication includes additional top-feature findings for the 20-variable model that were not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogiqSuite is certified and compliant with international medical data and privacy legislations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogiqSuite is certified and compliant with international medical data and privacy legislations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same certification and compliance claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MedDMS is used as an electronic case report form (eCRF) that allows real-time data management and analytics in long covid and pulmonary diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MedDMS is evaluated in seven precision medicine data science projects across various medical fields.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the specific eCRF/long-COVID usage to evaluation across multiple projects and fields, broadening the scope and modifying the original specific application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data from different sources were integrated in an acute myeloid leukaemia study to facilitate easy descriptive analytics for various research questions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MedDMS is evaluated in seven precision medicine data science projects across various medical fields.
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes broader evaluation across projects which likely includes the described AML study, providing a more generalized statement rather than the specific integration example.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LogiqCare processes patient data while LogiqScience is used for pseudonymous CAR-T cell production for cancer treatment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LogiqCare and LogiqScience are used for processing patient data and pseudonymous CAR-T cell production for cancer treatment.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same roles for LogiqCare and LogiqScience with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integrated MedDMS application supports data management for care and research in precision medicine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integrated MedDMS application supports data management for care and research in precision medicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about MedDMS supporting care and research in precision medicine.

alignments.pub_claim_text: LogiqAnalytics facilitates machine learning to develop new prediction models for clinical-decision support.
alignments.rationale: This capability of LogiqAnalytics appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Marginal bone loss (MBL) is one of the leading causes of dental implant failure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Marginal bone loss (MBL) is one of the leading causes of dental implant failure.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Severe MBL cases showed a significant increase of structure model index and trabecular pattern factor in peri-implant alveolar bone at the early stage of functional loading.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Severe MBL cases showed a significant increase of structure model index and trabecular pattern factor in peri-implant alveolar bone at the early stage of functional loading.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVM model exhibited the best outcome in predicting MBL with an AUC of 0.967, sensitivity of 91.67%, and specificity of 100.00%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVM model exhibited the best outcome in predicting MBL with an AUC of 0.967, sensitivity of 91.67%, and specificity of 100.00%.
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics and the conclusion about the SVM model are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms based on the morphological variation of trabecular bone can be used to predict severe MBL.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning algorithms based on the morphological variation of trabecular bone can be used to predict severe MBL.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same concluding claim about the utility of ML on trabecular morphology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning aids in diagnosing and classifying spondyloarthropathies (SpA), differentiating arthritis forms, and estimating disease progression and structural changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models, particularly advanced CNNs and U-Nets, demonstrated high accuracy in diagnosing spondyloarthropathies (SpA) across MRI, CT, and X-ray modalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes diagnostic accuracy across imaging modalities and specific architectures, which clarifies and focuses the broader preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models, particularly those using advanced U-Net architectures, consistently outperformed traditional diagnostic methods, showing a notable increase in diagnostic accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance metrics of deep learning models frequently surpassed traditional methods, with some models achieving AUCs up to 0.98.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete performance examples and AUC figures, specifying the degree to which deep learning outperformed traditional methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models are excellent for augmenting the accuracy of SpA imaging diagnostics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The studies reviewed demonstrated high diagnostic accuracy for deep learning in SpA imaging diagnostics.
alignments.rationale: The publication reaches the same overall conclusion about high diagnostic accuracy and the potential to augment diagnostics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Challenges in overcoming retrospective study biases and integrating these models into clinical practice remain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of small sample sizes in some studies highlights the need for more extensive datasets and further prospective and external validation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation by specifying small sample sizes and the need for further validation, which clarifies the preprint's broader concern about retrospective biases and implementation challenges.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future directions should aim to validate these models in real-world clinical settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of small sample sizes in some studies highlights the need for more extensive datasets and further prospective and external validation.
alignments.rationale: Both statements call for prospective/external validation in real-world settings; the publication provides more detail on the need and its rationale.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models can enhance diagnostic precision in SpA imaging, potentially reducing diagnostic delays and improving treatment decisions.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific downstream impact (reduced delays and improved treatment decisions) that was not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-task pipeline for COVID-19 detection achieves an accuracy of 99.4%, outperforming other deep models in recent literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-task pipeline for COVID-19 detection achieves an accuracy of 99.4%.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 99.4% accuracy but omits the explicit claim of outperforming other models, tempering the comparative assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-modal learning approach using both X-ray and CT scans provides a significant advantage over learning from X-ray scans alone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-modal learning approach outperforms learning from X-ray scans alone.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative advantage of the multi-modal approach without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed pipeline results in an increase of approximately 4% for the dice coefficient and 7% for mean intersection-over-union (mIoU), while also reducing computational time by 60%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models demonstrate an increase of approximately 2.5% for dice coefficient and 4.5% for mean intersection-over-union (mIoU).
alignments.rationale: The publication reports smaller improvements for dice and mIoU than the preprint, indicating a revision (reduced effect sizes) and thus a narrowing/tempering of the performance claims; note that computational time reduction is reported separately in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework's specialized streams for training multiple deep models separately significantly impact various performance metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed pipeline achieves a 60% reduction in computational time compared to recent literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the 60% computational time reduction; this aligns with the preprint's claim about the framework's impactful performance improvements, specifically the computational time metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall AUC for glaucoma screening increased from 0.82 to 0.85 when using RNFLT maps with the FIN module for racial groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fair identify normalization (FIN) module improved model performance equity, increasing the overall AUC from 0.82 to 0.85 and the ES-AUC from 0.77 to 0.81 for racial groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same overall AUC change and context (FIN, racial groups) with matching numbers, indicating the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The equity-scaled AUC (ES-AUC) for racial groups improved from 0.76 to 0.81 with the implementation of the FIN module. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fair identify normalization (FIN) module improved model performance equity, increasing the overall AUC from 0.82 to 0.85 and the ES-AUC from 0.77 to 0.81 for racial groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report ES-AUC increasing to 0.81, but the publication lists the baseline as 0.77 instead of 0.76, a small numeric revision to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AUC for Blacks increased from 0.77 to 0.81 when applying the FIN module for racial groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AUC for Blacks increased from 0.77 to 0.82 and for Hispanics from 0.75 to 0.79 with the implementation of FIN. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same direction and population (Blacks) but updates the post-FIN value to 0.82 (vs 0.81 in preprint) and additionally reports Hispanics, so the original claim is numerically revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our deep learning model enhances screening accuracy for underrepresented groups and promotes identity equity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fair identify normalization (FIN) module improved model performance equity, increasing the overall AUC from 0.82 to 0.85 and the ES-AUC from 0.77 to 0.81 for racial groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supports the preprint's conclusion with quantified performance and equity metrics (FIN results), thereby clarifying and substantiating the original concluding claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NLP has the potential to improve cancer care via several mechanisms, including information extraction and classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP has the potential to improve cancer care via several mechanisms, including information extraction and classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level claim verbatim, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NLP tools such as chatbots show promise in improving patient communication and support. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP tools such as chatbots show promise in improving patient communication and support. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim in the publication and retains the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Few technologies had been evaluated within clinical settings, none were evaluated against clinical outcomes, and none had been implemented into clinical care. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Few technologies have been evaluated within clinical settings, and none have been evaluated against clinical outcomes. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the statement about implementation into clinical care, narrowing the original limitation claim while retaining the evaluations/outcomes points.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interdisciplinary collaboration between computer scientists and clinicians will be essential if NLP technologies are to fulfil their potential to improve patient experience and outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interdisciplinary collaboration between computer scientists and clinicians will be essential for NLP technologies to improve patient experience and outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this recommendation with equivalent wording and emphasis, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning can effectively predict participants’ age from brain MRI data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning can be used effectively to predict participants' age from brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert the same core result that deep learning can predict age from MRI with high confidence; wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a systematic increase in brain-predicted age difference (brain-PAD) with worsening image quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a systematic increase in brain-predicted age difference (brain-PAD) with worsening image quality for both models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same systematic association (explicitly noting both models), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain-PAD is larger for lower-quality images, indicating a spurious effect of advanced brain aging due to head motion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are significant associations between brain-PAD and standard image quality metrics, indicating larger brain-PAD for lower-quality images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both report larger brain-PAD with lower-quality images, but the publication frames this as associations with image-quality metrics rather than explicitly attributing the effect to head motion, weakening the causal wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Controlling for image quality is important when using brain-predicted age as a proxy measure for brain health. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underline the importance of controlling for image quality when using brain-predicted age as a proxy measure for brain health. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly draws the same conclusion about the importance of controlling for image quality, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR outperforms baseline methods on both synthetic and real-world benchmark datasets with known ground-truth cluster labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR outperforms baseline methods on both synthetic and real-world benchmark datasets with known ground-truth cluster labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR successfully identifies four distinct subgroups with divergent diagnosis trajectories and risk profiles in Crohn’s disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR successfully identifies four distinct subgroups with divergent diagnosis trajectories and risk profiles in Crohn’s disease. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same specific subgroup-identification result, unchanged from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR reveals clinically and genetically relevant factors in Crohn’s disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR reveals clinically and genetically relevant factors in Crohn’s disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about revealing clinically/genetically relevant factors appears unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR can be a powerful tool for discovering novel patient subgroups in the development of precision medicine approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: VaDeSC-EHR can be a powerful tool for discovering novel patient subgroups in the development of precision medicine approaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication echoes the same concluding claim about VaDeSC-EHR's utility for precision medicine, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Women have a smaller spatial QRS-T angle, SAI QRST, and SVG elevation than men, but larger SVG azimuth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these sex-differentiated GEH measurements; the specific preprint result is omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAI QRST is larger in women with CVD compared to CVD-free women or men. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding result about SAI QRST differences by sex and CVD status appears in the publication, so the claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Men with CVD have a smaller T area than CVD-free men, but there are no differences when comparing women with CVD to CVD-free women. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's reported results do not include this specific T-area-by-sex/CVD comparison, indicating removal of the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GEH varies by sex and sex modifies the association of GEH with CVD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not state that sex modifies GEH-CVD associations; that broader conclusion from the preprint is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated CVD detection is feasible with machine-learning models achieving ROC AUC of 0.69-0.74. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models detected CVD with an area under the receiver operator curve (ROC AUC) ranging from 0.690 to 0.740. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUC range for ML detection of prevalent CVD, matching the preprint's feasibility claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can utilize heart rate as a biomarker to forecast high and low risk seizure periods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models can utilize heart rate as a biomarker to forecast high and low risk seizure periods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are textually identical and convey the same conclusion and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seizures were predicted above chance in 100% of participants using an hourly forecast and in 91% using a daily forecast. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seizures were predicted above chance in 100% of participants using an hourly forecast and in 91% using a daily forecast. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical results and wording are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average time spent in high risk before a seizure occurred was 37 minutes for the hourly forecast and 3 days for the daily forecast. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average time spent in high risk before a seizure occurred was 37 minutes for the hourly forecast and 3 days for the daily forecast. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported timings and certainty are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cyclic features, particularly circadian and multiday heart rate cycles, added the most predictive value to the forecasts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cyclic features, particularly circadian and multiday heart rate cycles, added the most predictive value to the forecasts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same conclusion about the importance of circadian and multiday cycles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Wearable devices can produce patient-specific seizure forecasts when utilizing biomarkers of seizure and epileptic activity cycles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Wearable devices can produce patient-specific seizure forecasts when biomarkers of seizure and epileptic activity cycles are utilized. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent meaning and certainty, differing only in minor wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 52% of the patients in the study had narrative text containing relevant mentions of physical pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 52% of the patients had narrative text containing relevant mentions of physical pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statistic and assertion are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Older patients, females, and those of Asian or Black ethnicities, as well as those living in deprived neighborhoods, showed higher odds of recorded pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Older patients, females, Asians, and black ethnicities showed higher odds of recorded pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the mention of people living in deprived neighborhoods, narrowing the described risk groups relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with a serious mental illness diagnosis were found to be less likely to report pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with severe mental illnesses were found to be less likely to report pain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly ('serious' vs 'severe') but the meaning and result are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 17% of the CRIS cohort also had records within LDN, and 31% of these had recorded pain in both records. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific cohort linkage and dual-record prevalence statement does not appear in the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings have significant implications for the assessment and management of physical pain in patients with mental health disorders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings indicate the need for better screening protocols and training on recognizing varied pain presentations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the general implication more specific by recommending screening protocols and training, clarifying the preprint's broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OpenMAP-T1 significantly reduced the processing time per image from several hours to less than 90 seconds without compromising accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenMAP-T1 significantly reduced the processing time per image from several hours to less than 90 seconds without compromising accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about processing time and accuracy in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OpenMAP-T1 demonstrates robustness across different datasets with variations in scanner types, magnetic field strengths, and image processing techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenMAP-T1 demonstrates robustness across different datasets with variations in scanner types, magnetic field strengths, and image processing techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim of robustness across datasets is stated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OpenMAP-T1 is particularly effective in handling images with intensity inhomogeneity and varying head positions, conditions commonly seen in clinical settings. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OpenMAP-T1 effectively handles images with intensity inhomogeneity and varying head positions, conditions commonly seen in clinical settings. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance claim with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptability of OpenMAP-T1 to a wide range of MRI datasets highlights its potential as a versatile tool in neuroimaging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication instead adds a technical description of model integration; the broader adaptability-as-potential claim from the preprint is not listed among publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 8.3% of patients with HF and LVEF ≥ 50% had a clinician-assigned diagnosis of HFpEF. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 8.3% of patients with HF and LVEF ≥50% had a clinician-assigned diagnosis of HFpEF. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric result and wording are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 75.4% of patients with HF and LVEF ≥ 50% met the ESC criteria for HFpEF but did not have a formal diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 75.4% of patients with LVEF ≥50% met the ESC criteria for HFpEF but did not have a formal diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Substantively the same claim about the proportion meeting ESC criteria but lacking diagnosis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with confirmed HFpEF were hospitalized more frequently than those who met ESC criteria but were undiagnosed. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patients with confirmed HFpEF were hospitalized more frequently than those without a diagnosis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the comparator from specifically 'those who met ESC criteria but were undiagnosed' to a broader 'those without a diagnosis,' widening the comparison group.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ESC criteria group had a higher 5-year mortality despite being less co-morbid and experiencing fewer acute cardiovascular events. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ESC criteria group had a higher 5-year mortality despite being less comorbid and experiencing fewer acute cardiovascular events. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The finding is restated with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates that NLP methods can identify likely HFpEF patients from EHR data who would benefit from expert clinical review. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NLP methods can be used to identify likely HFpEF patients from EHR data for expert clinical review. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions express the same conclusion about using NLP to flag patients for expert review.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CROSS-TRACKS cohort comprises population-based data from primary care, secondary care, and national registries. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not restate this specific description of data sources for the cohort.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 27% of individuals aged ≥65 years had at least one potentially preventable hospital admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 23% of individuals aged ≥65 years had at least one potentially preventable hospital admission. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in nature but the reported proportion changed from 27% to 23%, representing a revision of the numeric result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cohort will be expanded to include additional catchment areas in the Central Denmark Region, comprising a total population of 1.3 million individuals. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention the planned expansion or the 1.3M population figure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cohort provides new knowledge on how to best organise interventions across healthcare sectors and prevent potentially preventable hospital admissions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cohort can provide new knowledge on how to best organise interventions across healthcare sectors and prevent potentially preventable hospital admissions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about the cohort's potential to inform organization of interventions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cohort is currently limited to the catchment area of Horsens Regional Hospital. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is not present among the publication claims provided.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The commonly used ROC curve is not sufficiently informative when evaluating the performance of deep learning models in the imbalanced data in OA studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The commonly used ROC curve is not sufficiently informative, especially in the case of imbalanced data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same point about ROC curves being uninformative for imbalanced data but removes the OA-specific framing, thus broadening the scope while keeping the core message the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The class ratios coupled with results of ROC, PR, and Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) should be reported in OA studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ROC-AUC is recommended for balanced data.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from a general recommendation to report multiple metrics and class ratios toward specific metric recommendations by imbalance level (e.g., ROC-AUC for balanced data), narrowing and altering the original recommendation and presenting it with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep-learning model was trained to predict the presence of bone marrow lesions (BMLs) from knee MRI images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In a subregion with an extremely high imbalance ratio, the model achieved a ROC-AUC of 0.84, a PR-AUC of 0.10, a sensitivity of 0, and a specificity of 1.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete performance results for the model and reveals severe limitations (zero sensitivity) in an extremely imbalanced subregion, revising the simple statement that a model was trained into a more qualified result highlighting poor performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can be used to investigate whether patients would have been treated differently had they attended a different hospital.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can be used to investigate whether patients would have been treated differently had they attended a different hospital.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can group hospitals according to clinical decision making practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can group hospitals according to clinical decision making practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can identify where there is variation in decision making between hospitals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can identify where there is variation in decision making between hospitals.
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can characterise patients that hospitals find it hard to agree on how to treat.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning can characterise patients that hospitals find it hard to agree on how to treat.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods presented should be applicable to any clinical registry and any machine learning algorithm.
alignments.rationale: This broader methodological generalization appears in the preprint but is not present in the publication, so it was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed electronic health record pipeline effectively combines structured data with free text to simulate recruitment into the LeoPARDS trial.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of EHR data using NLP could effectively replicate recruitment in a critical care trial.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's specific pipeline into a broader statement about EHR+NLP analysis replicating recruitment, and presents it with somewhat lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm identified 308 patients, of whom 208 were screened in the actual trial.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method identified 376 patients, including 34 patients with EHR data available who were actually recruited to LeoPARDS in our centre.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different (higher) total identification numbers and different counts of recruited/screened patients, revising the quantitative result from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm identified all 40 patients with CCHIC data available who were actually recruited to LeoPARDS in our centre.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method identified 376 patients, including 34 patients with EHR data available who were actually recruited to LeoPARDS in our centre.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different count (34 vs 40) of actually recruited patients present in EHR data, indicating a revision to the preprint's specific numerical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis of electronic health records incorporating natural language processing tools could effectively replicate recruitment in a critical care trial.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of EHR data using NLP could effectively replicate recruitment in a critical care trial.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion about EHR+NLP replicating recruitment with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If implemented in real-time, this approach could improve the efficiency of clinical trial recruitment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CogStack identified some eligible patients at an earlier stage, potentially improving trial recruitment if implemented in real time.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the same potential real-time recruitment benefit, adding the mechanism (earlier identification) but keeping the original conditional claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NLP demonstrated robust performance across the 10 LVH subtypes with overall sensitivity and specificity exceeding 96%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NLP module demonstrated robust performance across the 10 LVH subtypes, with overall sensitivity and specificity exceeding 96%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-performance result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NLP module demonstrated excellent performance detecting HCM diagnoses, with sensitivity and specificity exceeding 93%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NLP module demonstrated excellent performance in detecting HCM diagnoses, with sensitivity and specificity exceeding 93%.
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We designed a highly accurate NLP module to determine the presence of LVH and HCM on echocardiogram reports.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We designed a highly accurate NLP module to determine the presence of LVH and HCM on echocardiogram reports.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this contribution claim with the same wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work demonstrates the feasibility of NLP to detect diagnoses on imaging reports, even when described in free-text.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work demonstrates the feasibility and accuracy of NLP to detect diagnoses on imaging reports, even when described in free text.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit emphasis on accuracy in addition to feasibility, slightly strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhanced External Counterpulsation (EECP) significantly promotes blood flow perfusion to vital organs such as the heart, brain, and kidneys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EECP has been widely applied in the cardiovascular field and confirmed to promote blood flow perfusion to vital organs.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result that EECP promotes perfusion to vital organs, adding context about widespread application but preserving the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models like ChatGPT and Ernie Bot could serve as powerful tools for identifying and prioritizing research priorities in the EECP domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models like ChatGPT and Ernie Bot can identify and prioritize research priorities in the EECP domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the capability more strongly (can vs could) and at higher certainty, amplifying confidence while keeping the claim content the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average and median scores for evaluation parameters of research priorities generated by language models were high, indicating strong endorsement from experts in the EECP field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A panel of nine experienced EECP experts provided high average and median scores for the generated research priorities based on relevance, originality, clarity, and specificity.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives additional detail about the expert panel (nine experienced experts and scoring dimensions), clarifying the preprint's reported high scores.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many potential mechanisms of EECP remain insufficiently validated, necessitating further in-depth investigations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many potential mechanisms of EECP remain insufficiently validated, requiring further investigation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the limitation and presents it with higher certainty, amplifying the confidence in the need for further investigation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models with hematology indices alone showed Harrell’s concordance index ranging from 0.60–0.80 on an external validation set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models using only hematology indices had a concordance index ranging from 0.60 to 0.80 on an external validation set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (same metric and range) about models using only hematology indices with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hematology indices added significantly in terms of discrimination and calibration performance compared to models using only demographic data and diagnostic codes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models that included hematology indices showed better discrimination and calibration compared to models trained only on demographic data and diagnostic codes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative improvement in discrimination and calibration when hematology indices are included, with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predictive features varied by outcome and included red blood cell, leukocyte, and platelet indices. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the statement about which specific hematologic features were predictive; that result appears omitted from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low-cost ubiquitous inputs, if biologically informative, can provide population-level readouts of risk. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low-cost, ubiquitous inputs, if biologically informative, can provide population-level readouts of risk. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about low-cost ubiquitous inputs providing population-level risk readouts verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diverse deep learning applications, including image quality enhancement, bowel segmentation, and motility measurement are useful and promising for Crohn's disease assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diverse deep learning applications, including image quality enhancement, bowel segmentation for disease burden quantification, and 3D reconstruction for surgical planning, are useful and promising for Crohn's disease assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the overall claim but changes the specific listed applications (replacing motility measurement with 3D reconstruction and adding a purpose for segmentation), so the content is revised and slightly broadened while overall certainty is similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most of the studies on deep learning applications for Crohn's disease assessment are preliminary, retrospective studies with a high risk of bias in at least one category. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most of the studies on deep learning applications for Crohn's disease assessment are preliminary, retrospective studies, and have a high risk of bias in at least one category. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation nearly verbatim, so semantic content, scope, and expressed confidence remain unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research is needed to assess how automated deep learning can impact patient care in the context of Crohn's disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future research is needed to assess how deep learning can impact Crohn's disease patient diagnostics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the future-research recommendation from general patient care to the narrower domain of diagnostics, so the claim is revised with a narrowed scope while similar in expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-biopsy can distinguish aggressive prostate cancer from non-aggressive disease with an AUC of 0.855 and a 79.02% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI-biopsy model achieved an AUC of 0.78 for distinguishing high-risk tumors from low-risk tumors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same classification task but with a lower AUC than the preprint, so the result is revised and the performance claim is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-biopsy provides a data-driven and reproducible way to assess cancer aggressiveness from MRI images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-biopsy provides a data-driven and reproducible method to assess cancer risk from MR images, potentially reducing unnecessary biopsies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim but frames it slightly more cautiously and adds an explicit link to potentially reducing biopsies; overall tone of certainty is reduced, so it is clarified and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI-biopsy offers a personalized strategy to reduce the number of unnecessary biopsies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI-biopsy provides a data-driven and reproducible method to assess cancer risk from MR images, potentially reducing unnecessary biopsies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the biopsy-reduction claim more cautiously as a potential outcome; the substance is the same but the wording is softened, so it is clarified with similar expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PI-RADS score is subjective and its assessment varies between physicians. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reiterate or discuss PI-RADS subjectivity; that specific limitation is absent from the published claims, so it appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 demonstrated significantly higher performance than GPT-3.5 and Bard in answering nephrology questions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 significantly outperformed GPT-3.5 and Bard in nephrology applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same comparative performance conclusion without substantial change in scope or certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 met the passing criteria in three years of the nephrology board renewal questions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4 passed the Nephrology Board renewal standards in three years, barely meeting the minimum threshold in two. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds nuance (barely meeting thresholds in two years) and lowers expressed certainty, so the original claim is clarified and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correct answer rate for GPT-4 was intermediate between the rates for third- and fourth-year nephrology residents. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report a direct comparison to resident year-level rates; instead it provides model-specific rates and task breakdowns, so the resident-comparison statement is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings underline the potential applications of large language models in nephrology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results highlight LLMs’ potential and limitations in nephrology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the general conclusion about potential but also emphasizes limitations; this clarifies the original claim by adding nuance about limits while retaining similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High correlations were observed between some of the socio-economic and epidemiologic parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: High correlations were observed between some socio-economic and epidemiological parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same observation but reports it with reduced expressed certainty, so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We established a mathematical and statistical relationship between Theil and Gini index. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mathematical relationship was established between Theil and Gini indices. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same methodological contribution with equivalent scope and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results show a sharp difference between the first and second wave of the pandemic. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a clear difference between the first and second waves of the pandemic in terms of socio-economic impact. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the difference pertains to socio-economic impact (narrowing the scope of the original more general statement) and frames it as a conclusion, so the claim is clarified and narrowed in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study confirms the real dynamics of the spread of the outbreak in several countries and ways by which it was mitigated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published claims focus on socio-economic impacts and index relationships and do not reiterate a general confirmation of outbreak spread dynamics and mitigation strategies, so this broader claim is absent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the zero-shot condition, GPT-4’s average balanced accuracy was 57%, which is lower than the average balanced accuracy of experienced neurologists at 71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the zero-shot condition, GPT-4's average balanced accuracy was 57%, which is lower than the mean balanced accuracy of experienced neurologists at 71%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this numeric comparison identically, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Balanced accuracy improved in the one-shot condition to 64%, but did not improve further in the two-shot or three-shot conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Balanced accuracy improved in the one-shot condition to 64%, but did not improve further in the two-shot and three-shot conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental finding with the same metrics and conditions, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT-4 showed noticeable improvement in diagnostic performance when given just one example of a patient describing epilepsy and FDS. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The finding that GPT-4 performed better in cases correctly diagnosed by neurologists suggests that more extensive clinical data could enhance the diagnostic potential of LLMs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication interprets improved performance in a different subset of cases as evidence that more clinical data could help, shifting the emphasis from a one-shot example improvement to a broader recommendation about data richness; thus the claim is revised and broadened in focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More elaborate approaches, such as refined prompt engineering or fine-tuning, could unlock the full diagnostic potential of LLMs. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication instead emphasizes clinical-data richness rather than advocating for prompt engineering or fine-tuning, so the specific suggestion about prompt engineering/fine-tuning is not present in the published claims and appears removed.
